translations.source: Kichocho (Bilharzia) Muhtasari: Kichocho ni ugonjwa wa umaskini unaosababisha magonjwa sugu. Maambukizi hupatikana wakati watu wanagusana na maji safi yaliyoathiriwa na aina za mabuu (cercariae) ya mafua ya vimelea ya damu, inayojulikana kama schistosomes. Minyoo wadogo wadogo wazee huishi katika mishipa inayounda njia ya mkojo na utumbo. Mayai mengi wanayotaga yananaswa kwenye tishu na majibu ya mwili kwao yanaweza kusababisha uharibifu mkubwa.
translations.reference: Schistosomiasis (Bilharzia) Overview: Schistosomiasis is a disease of poverty that leads to chronic ill-health. Infection is acquired when people come into contact with fresh water infested with the larval forms (cercariae) of parasitic blood flukes, known as schistosomes. The microscopic adult worms live in the veins draining the urinary tract and intestines. Most of the eggs they lay are trapped in the tissues and the body’s reaction to them can cause massive damage.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Schistosomiasis (Bilharzia) Summary:** Schistosomiasis is a disease of poverty that causes chronic illness. Infection occurs when people come into contact with contaminated freshwater containing parasitic larvae (cercariae) of blood flukes, known as schistosomes. The small, immature worms live in the blood vessels forming the urinary and intestinal tracts. The large number of eggs they lay become trapped in tissues, and the body's reaction to them can cause significant damage.

translations.source: Kichocho huathiri karibu watu milioni 240 duniani kote, na zaidi ya watu milioni 700 wanaishi katika maeneo ya ugonjwa huo. Maambukizi yameenea katika maeneo ya tropiki na chini ya tropiki, katika jamii maskini bila maji ya kunywa na usafi wa mazingira wa kutosha. Kichocho kwenye urogenital husababishwa na Schistosoma haematobium na schistosomiasis ya matumbo na kiumbe chochote S. guineensis, S. intercalatum, S. mansoni, S. japonicum, na S. mekongi. Watu milioni kadhaa ulimwenguni kote wanaugua ugonjwa mbaya kama matokeo ya kichocho. Mkakati wa WHO juu ya matumizi ya dawa za anthelminthic sasa unawezesha kudhibiti kichocho katika jamii maskini na zilizotengwa, kwa kushirikiana na hatua dhidi ya filariasis ya limfu, onchocerciasis na helminthiasis inayopitishwa kwenye udongo.
translations.reference: Schistosomiasis affects almost 240 million people worldwide, and more than 700 million people live in endemic areas. The infection is prevalent in tropical and sub-tropical areas, in poor communities without potable water and adequate sanitation. Urogenital schistosomiasis is caused by Schistosoma haematobium and intestinal schistosomiasis by any of the organisms S. guineensis, S. intercalatum, S. mansoni, S. japonicum, and S. mekongi. Several million people all over the world suffer from severe morbidity as a consequence of schistosomiasis. The WHO strategy on use of anthelminthic drugs now makes it possible to control schistosomiasis in poor and marginalized communities, in conjunction with interventions against lymphatic filariasis, onchocerciasis and soil transmitted helminthiasis.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Schistosomiasis affects nearly 240 million people worldwide, and over 700 million live in endemic areas. Infections are widespread in tropical and subtropical regions, in poor communities lacking sufficient safe water and environmental sanitation. Genital schistosomiasis is caused by *Schistosoma haematobium*, and intestinal schistosomiasis by any of the parasites *S. guineensis, S. intercalatum, S. mansoni, S. japonicum,* and *S. mekongi*. Millions of people worldwide suffer from severe illness as a result of schistosomiasis. The WHO strategy on the use of anthelmintic drugs now enables control of schistosomiasis in poor and marginalized communities, in conjunction with measures against lymphatic filariasis, onchocerciasis, and soil-transmitted helminthiasis.

translations.source: Katika maeneo ambayo yameenea sana, ugonjwa mbaya wa kichocho unaweza kuzuiwa kwa matibabu ya mara kwa mara ya vikundi vilivyo hatarini vinavyolengwa kulingana na utambuzi wa jamii kulingana na vikundi vya walinzi. Praziquantel imetumiwa kwa usalama pamoja na albendazole na ivermectin, katika maeneo ambayo dawa hizi zimetumika kando kwa ajili ya kuzuia chemotherapy. Dalili: Dalili za kichocho husababishwa na mmenyuko wa mwili kwa mayai ya minyoo. Kichocho cha matumbo kinaweza kusababisha maumivu ya tumbo, kuhara, na damu kwenye kinyesi. Kuongezeka kwa ini ni kawaida katika hali ya juu, na mara nyingi huhusishwa na mkusanyiko wa maji katika cavity ya peritoneal na shinikizo la damu ya mishipa ya damu ya tumbo.
translations.reference: In highly endemic areas, severe morbidity due to schistosomiasis can be prevented by regular treatment of at risk groups targeted based on community diagnosis based on sentinel groups. Praziquantel has been safely co-administered with albendazole and ivermectin, in areas where these drugs have been used separately for preventive chemotherapy. Symptoms: Symptoms of schistosomiasis are caused by the body’s reaction to the worms' eggs. Intestinal schistosomiasis can result in abdominal pain, diarrhoea, and blood in the stool. Liver enlargement is common in advanced cases, and is frequently associated with an accumulation of fluid in the peritoneal cavity and hypertension of the abdominal blood vessels.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"In areas where schistosomiasis is widespread, the disease can be prevented through regular treatment of at-risk groups targeted according to community diagnosis based on risk groups. Praziquantel has been used safely alongside albendazole and ivermectin, in areas where these drugs have been used separately for chemoprophylaxis. Symptoms: Symptoms of schistosomiasis are caused by the body's reaction to worm eggs. Intestinal schistosomiasis can cause abdominal pain, diarrhea, and blood in the stool. Liver enlargement is common in severe cases, and is often associated with fluid accumulation in the peritoneal cavity and increased pressure in the abdominal blood vessels."

translations.source: Katika kesi kama hizo kunaweza pia kuwa na upanuzi wa wengu. Ishara kuu ya kichocho ya urogenital ni hematuria (damu katika mkojo). Fibrosis ya kibofu na ureta, na uharibifu wa figo wakati mwingine hugunduliwa katika hali ya juu. Saratani ya kibofu ni shida nyingine inayowezekana katika hatua za baadaye. Kwa wanawake, kichocho kwenye urogenital kinaweza kujitokeza na vidonda vya sehemu za siri, kutokwa na damu ukeni, maumivu wakati wa kujamiiana, na vinundu kwenye uke.
translations.reference: In such cases there may also be enlargement of the spleen. The classic sign of urogenital schistosomiasis is haematuria (blood in urine). Fibrosis of the bladder and ureter, and kidney damage are sometimes diagnosed in advanced cases. Bladder cancer is another possible complication in the later stages. In women, urogenital schistosomiasis may present with genital lesions, vaginal bleeding, pain during sexual intercourse, and nodules in the vulva.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"In such cases, there may also be an extension of the condition. A key sign of urogenital fistula is hematuria (blood in the urine). Fibrosis of the bladder and ureter, and kidney damage are sometimes found in advanced stages. Bladder cancer is another potential problem in later stages. For women, a urogenital fistula can manifest with sores in the genital area, vaginal bleeding, pain during intercourse, and ulcers in the vagina."

translations.source: Kwa wanaume, kichocho ya urogenital inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa vesicles ya seminal, prostate, na viungo vingine. Ugonjwa huu unaweza pia kuwa na matokeo mengine ya muda mrefu yasiyoweza kurekebishwa, ikiwa ni pamoja na utasa. Madhara ya kiuchumi na kiafya ya kichocho ni makubwa na ugonjwa huo huzima zaidi kuliko kuua. Kwa watoto, kichocho kinaweza kusababisha upungufu wa damu, kudumaa na uwezo mdogo wa kujifunza, ingawa madhara kwa kawaida huweza kubadilishwa kwa matibabu. Matibabu: Praziquantel ndiyo tiba inayopendekezwa dhidi ya aina zote za kichocho.
translations.reference: In men, urogenital schistosomiasis can induce pathology of the seminal vesicles, prostate, and other organs. This disease may also have other long-term irreversible consequences, including infertility. The economic and health effects of schistosomiasis are considerable and the disease disables more than it kills. In children, schistosomiasis can cause anaemia, stunting and a reduced ability to learn, although the effects are usually reversible with treatment. Treatment: Praziquantel is the recommended treatment against all forms of schistosomiasis.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"For men, infection of the genitals can cause seminal vesicle disease, prostate, and other organs. This disease can also have other long-term, irreversible consequences, including infertility. The economic and health impacts of schistosomiasis are significant, and the disease is more debilitating than deadly. For children, schistosomiasis can cause anemia, stunting, and impaired learning ability, although the effects are usually reversible with treatment. Treatment: Praziquantel is the recommended treatment for all types of schistosomiasis."

**Note:** "Kichocho" refers to schistosomiasis, a parasitic disease. I've used that term in the translation for accuracy.

translations.source: Ni ya ufanisi, salama, na ya gharama nafuu. Ingawa kuambukizwa tena kunaweza kutokea baada ya matibabu, hatari ya kupata ugonjwa mbaya hupunguzwa na hata kubadilishwa wakati matibabu yanapoanzishwa na kurudiwa katika utoto. Udhibiti wa kichocho umetekelezwa kwa mafanikio katika kipindi cha miaka 40 katika nchi kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na Brazil, Kambodia, China, Misri, Mauritius, Jamhuri ya Kiislamu ya Iran, Oman, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Morocco, Tunisia, nk. Nchini Burundi, Burkina Faso, Ghana, Niger, Rwanda, Sierra Leone, Jamhuri ya Muungano wa Tanzania, na Yemen, imewezekana kuongeza matibabu ya kichocho hadi ngazi ya kitaifa na kuwa na athari kwa ugonjwa huo katika miaka michache. Tathmini ya hali ya maambukizi inahitajika katika nchi kadhaa.
translations.reference: It is effective, safe, and low-cost. Even though re-infection may occur after treatment, the risk of developing severe disease is diminished and even reversed when treatment is initiated and repeated in childhood. Schistosomiasis control has been successfully implemented over the past 40 years in several countries, including Brazil, Cambodia, China, Egypt, Mauritius, Islamic Republic of Iran, Oman, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Morocco, Tunisia, etc. In Burundi, Burkina Faso, Ghana, Niger, Rwanda, Sierra Leone, the United Republic of Tanzania, and Yemen, it has been possible to scale-up schistosomiasis treatment to the national level and have an impact on the disease in a few years. An assessment of the status of transmission is required in several countries.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"It is effective, safe, and cost-effective. Although reinfection can occur after treatment, the risk of developing severe disease is reduced and even eliminated when treatment is initiated and repeated in childhood. Control of schistosomiasis has been successfully implemented over a period of 40 years in several countries, including Brazil, Cambodia, China, Egypt, Mauritius, Islamic Republic of Iran, Oman, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Morocco, Tunisia, etc. In Burundi, Burkina Faso, Ghana, Niger, Rwanda, Sierra Leone, United Republic of Tanzania, and Yemen, it has been possible to scale up schistosomiasis treatment to the national level and have an impact on the disease in a few years. Assessment of the infection situation is needed in several countries."

translations.source: Katika kipindi cha miaka 10 iliyopita, kumekuwa na ongezeko la kampeni za matibabu katika nchi kadhaa zilizo kusini mwa jangwa la Sahara, ambapo wengi wa wale walio katika hatari wanaishi.
translations.reference: Over the past 10 years, there has been scale-up of treatment campaigns in a number of sub-Saharan countries, where most of those at risk live.
translations.hypothesis: Over the past 10 years, there has been an increase in medical campaigns in several countries south of the Sahara Desert, where many of those at risk live.

translations.source: Hatua za kujitunza kwa afya Muhtasari: Kujitunza ni uwezo wa watu binafsi, familia na jamii kukuza afya, kuzuia magonjwa, kudumisha afya, na kukabiliana na ugonjwa na ulemavu kwa msaada au bila msaada wa mfanyakazi wa afya. Hivi sasa watu bilioni 3.6 - nusu ya dunia - kukosa huduma muhimu za afya. WHO inapendekeza uingiliaji wa huduma za kibinafsi kwa kila nchi na mazingira ya kiuchumi, kama njia muhimu ya kufikia chanjo ya afya kwa wote, kukuza afya, kuweka ulimwengu salama, na kuwahudumia walio hatarini. Kujitunza hutambua watu binafsi kama mawakala hai katika kusimamia huduma zao za afya, katika maeneo ikiwa ni pamoja na kukuza afya; kinga na udhibiti wa magonjwa; kujitibu; kutoa huduma kwa watu tegemezi, na urekebishaji, ikiwa ni pamoja na huduma shufaa. Kuingilia uangalizi binafsi ni msingi wa ushahidi, zana za ubora zinazosaidia kujitunza.
translations.reference: Self-care interventions for health Overview: Self-care is the ability of individuals, families and communities to promote health, prevent disease, maintain health, and cope with illness and disability with or without the support of a health worker.Currently 3.6 billion people – half of the world – lack access to essential health services. WHO recommends self-care interventions for every country and economic setting, as a critical path to reach universal health coverage, promote health, keep the world safe, and serve the vulnerable. Self-care recognises individuals as active agents in managing their own health care, in areas including health promotion; disease prevention and control; self-medication; providing care to dependent persons, and rehabilitation, including palliative care. Self-care interventions are evidence-based, quality tools that support self-care.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Self-Care Steps for Health**

**Summary:** Self-care is the ability of individuals, families, and communities to promote health, prevent disease, maintain health, and cope with illness and disability with or without the assistance of a healthcare worker. Currently, 3.6 billion people – half the world – lack essential health services. WHO recommends personalized care interventions for every country and economic environment, as a crucial way to achieve health coverage for all, promote health, keep the world safe, and serve the vulnerable. Self-care recognizes individuals as active agents in managing their health services, in areas including promoting health; disease prevention and control; self-treatment; providing care for dependents, and rehabilitation, including palliative care. Personalized care interventions are evidence-based, quality tools that support self-care.

translations.source: Zinajumuisha dawa, ushauri nasaha, uchunguzi na/au teknolojia za kidijitali ambazo zinaweza kufikiwa kikamilifu au kwa kiasi nje ya huduma rasmi za afya. Kulingana na uingiliaji kati, zinaweza kutumika na au bila usimamizi wa moja kwa moja wa wafanyakazi wa afya. Kuingilia utunzaji binafsi kunaweza: kuwawezesha watu binafsi na jamii kusimamia afya na ustawi wao kuimarisha taasisi za kitaifa kwa matumizi bora ya rasilimali za ndani kwa afya
translations.reference: They include medicines, counselling, diagnostics and/or digital technologies which can be accessed fully or partially outside of formal health services. Depending on the intervention, they can be used with or without the direct supervision of health workers. Self-care interventions can: empower individuals and communities to manage their health and well-being strengthen national institutions with efficient use of domestic resources for health
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"They include medicines, advice and counseling, examinations and/or digital technologies that can be accessed fully or partially outside of formal health services. Depending on the intervention, they can be used with or without the direct supervision of healthcare workers. Self-care interventions can: empower individuals and communities to manage their health and well-being, and strengthen national institutions through better use of local health resources."

translations.source: kuboresha huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC) na kuchangia katika kufikia UHC Mbinu: Mtazamo unaozingatia watu Uingiliaji kati ya kujitunza unasaidia mahitaji na haki za watu binafsi kupitia mkabala unaozingatia watu ambao umejikita katika haki za binadamu na usawa wa kijinsia. Huu ni mtazamo wa jumla wa afya na ustawi wa kila mtu, kwa kuzingatia hali zao, mahitaji na tamaa katika maisha yote, pamoja na mazingira anamoishi. Kujitunza ni uwezo wa watu binafsi wa kuzuia na kutibu magonjwa wenyewe, katika muktadha wa mazingira wezeshi salama na yanayosaidia. Haichukui nafasi ya mfumo wa utunzaji wa afya, lakini badala yake hutoa chaguzi za ziada na chaguzi za utunzaji wa afya.
translations.reference: improve primary healthcare (PHC) and contribute to achieving UHC Approach: A people-centred approach Self-care interventions support the needs and rights of individuals through a people-centred approach that is grounded in human rights and gender equality. This is a holistic approach to the health and well-being of each person, taking account of their circumstances, needs and desires across a lifetime, as well as the environment in which they live. Self-care is the power of individuals to prevent and treat diseases themselves, in the context of a safe and supportive enabling environment. It does not replace the health care system, but instead provides additional choices and options for health care.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Swahili document into English:

**Improving Primary Health Care (PHC) and Contributing to Achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC)**

**Approach: A People-Centered Perspective**

Self-care interventions support the needs and rights of individuals through a people-centered approach that focuses on human rights and gender equality. This is a holistic view of health and well-being for each person, considering their circumstances, needs, and aspirations throughout life, including the environment in which they live. Self-care is the ability of individuals to prevent and treat illnesses themselves, within the context of enabling and supportive environments. It does not replace the health care system, but rather provides additional options and choices for health care.

translations.source: Afua zinazopatikana, zinazoweza kufikiwa, nafuu, zinazokubalika na bora ni sehemu kuu za kukuza na kulinda haki ya afya ya watu. Hatua za kujihudumia zinaweza kuboresha ufanisi wa utoaji wa huduma za afya, upatikanaji na chanjo. Hili linahitaji sheria na sera zinazounga mkono, ufadhili wa afya, bidhaa na uingiliaji bora uliodhibitiwa, na elimu ambayo inakuza ushiriki wa watu binafsi, pamoja na faragha na usiri. Mazingatio ya haki za binadamu husaidia kupunguza unyanyasaji, shuruti, unyanyapaa na ubaguzi kwa kuboresha utumiaji wa kila mtu haki yake ya afya, habari, na kufanya maamuzi huru. Muktadha wa dharura
translations.reference: Available, accessible, affordable, acceptable and good quality self-care interventions are core components of promoting and protecting people’s right to health. Self-care interventions can improve the efficiency of health service delivery, access and coverage. This requires supportive laws and policies, health financing, regulated quality products and interventions, and education which promotes the participation of individuals, as well as privacy and confidentiality. Human rights considerations help to reduce violence, coercion, stigma and discrimination by improving each person’s exercise of their right to health, information, and independent decision-making. Emergency context
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Accessible, affordable, acceptable, and quality health inputs are key components of promoting and protecting the right to people's health. Self-care measures can improve the efficiency of health service delivery, accessibility, and immunization. This requires supportive laws and policies, health funding, regulated and effective products and interventions, and education that promotes individual engagement, along with privacy and confidentiality. Human rights considerations help reduce abuse, coercion, exploitation, and discrimination by improving everyone's ability to exercise their right to health, information, and make free decisions. Emergency context."

translations.source: Magonjwa na migogoro ya kibinadamuKatika hali ambapo mifumo iliyopo ya afya imeenea, mara nyingi kuna mahitaji ambayo hayajawahi kushuhudiwa kwa watu binafsi na jamii kusimamia afya zao. Mikakati bunifu inayoenda zaidi ya mwitikio wa kawaida wa sekta ya afya inahitajika haraka kutokana na kwamba: rekodi ya watu milioni 274 kwa sasa wanahitaji msaada wa kibinadamu na ulinzi na milipuko ya magonjwa ni tishio la kimataifa. angalau watu milioni 400 duniani kote wanakosa huduma muhimu zaidi za afya. 1 kati ya 5 ya idadi ya watu duniani sasa wanaishi katika majanga ya kibinadamu, ambapo mifumo ya afya ina changamoto kutoa huduma muhimu.
translations.reference: Pandemics and humanitarian crisesIn situations where existing health systems are stretched, there is often an unprecedented demand on individuals and communities to manage their health. Innovative strategies that go beyond the conventional health sector response is urgently needed given that: a record 274 million people are currently in need of humanitarian assistance and protection and disease outbreaks are a constant global threat at least 400 million people worldwide lack access to the most essential health services. 1 in 5 of the world’s population are now living in humanitarian crises, in which health systems are challenged to deliver essential services.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Diseases and Humanitarian Crises**

In situations where existing health systems are stretched, there are often unprecedented needs for individuals and communities to manage their health. Innovative strategies that go beyond the usual response of the health sector are urgently needed because: a record 274 million people currently need humanitarian assistance and protection, and disease outbreaks are a global threat. At least 400 million people worldwide lack essential health services. 1 in 5 of the world's population now lives in humanitarian disasters, where health systems struggle to provide essential services.

translations.source: Uingiliaji kati wa kujitunza unaweza kutoa chaguzi muhimu, za ziada kwa kituo cha afya cha kawaida- au huduma za wafanyikazi wa afya, kama sehemu ya mfumo wa afya unaounga mkono. Hili linafaa hasa wakati wa janga la UVIKO-19, wakati kujitenga na hatua za umbali wa kimwili hufanya iwe vigumu zaidi kwa watu kupata huduma zao za kawaida za afya na dawa na mifumo ya afya ya kitaifa inaweza kutatizwa. WHO inajumuisha hatua za kujitunza kama hatua za kipaumbele katika mwongozo wa uendeshaji wa kudumisha huduma muhimu za afya kwa muktadha wa UVIKO-19.
translations.reference: Self-care interventions can provide valuable, additional options to the usual health facility- or health worker-based services, as part of a supportive health system. This is particularly relevant during a pandemic like COVID-19, when self-isolation and physical distancing measures make it more difficult for people to access their normal health care services and medications and national health systems can be disrupted. WHO includes self-care interventions as priority actions in operational guidance on maintaining essential health services for the COVID-19 context.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Self-care interventions can provide valuable, supplementary options for a typical health facility or healthcare workforce, as part of a supportive health system. This is particularly relevant during the COVID-19 pandemic, when isolation and physical distancing measures make it more difficult for people to access their regular health services and medications, and national health systems can be strained. WHO includes self-care actions as priority measures in its operational guidance to maintain essential health services in the context of COVID-19."

translations.source: Sepsis Muhtasari: Sepsis ni hali ya kutishia maisha ambayo hutokea wakati majibu ya mwili kwa maambukizi husababisha kuumia kwa tishu na viungo vyake. Mara nyingi ni njia ya mwisho ya kifo kwa magonjwa mengi ya kuambukiza ulimwenguni. Inahusisha ulemavu wa viungo unaosababishwa na mwitikio usio na udhibiti wa mwenyeji kwa maambukizi na ikiwa haitatambuliwa mapema na kudhibitiwa mara moja, inaweza kusababisha mshtuko wa septic, kushindwa kwa viungo vingi na kifo. Ingawa makadirio sahihi ya mzigo wa kimataifa wa ugonjwa wa sepsis ni vigumu kuthibitishwa, uchapishaji wa hivi karibuni wa kisayansi uliripoti kwamba sepsis huathiri takriban watu milioni 49 na husababisha vifo milioni 11 duniani kote kila mwaka.
translations.reference: Sepsis Overview: Sepsis is a life-threatening condition that arises when the body’s response to infection causes injury to its own tissues and organs. It is frequently a final common pathway to death for many infectious diseases worldwide. It involves organ dysfunction caused by a dysregulated host response to infection and if not recognized early and managed promptly, it can lead to septic shock, multiple organ failure and death. Although a precise estimate of the global epidemiological burden of sepsis is difficult to ascertain, a recent scientific publication reported that sepsis affects an estimated 49 million people and causes 11 million deaths globally every year.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

**Sepsis Summary:** Sepsis is a life-threatening condition that occurs when the body's response to an infection causes damage to its tissues and organs. It is often the final pathway of death in many infectious diseases worldwide. It involves organ dysfunction caused by an uncontrolled host response to infection and, if not recognized early and treated immediately, it can lead to septic shock, multiple organ failure, and death. While accurate estimates of the global burden of sepsis are difficult to confirm, recent scientific publications have reported that sepsis affects approximately 49 million people and causes 11 million deaths worldwide each year.

translations.source: Katika mazingira ya jamii, sepsis mara nyingi hujidhihirisha kama kuzorota kwa magonjwa ya kawaida na yanayoweza kuzuilika. Sepsis pia mara nyingi hutokana na maambukizo yanayopatikana katika mazingira ya huduma za afya, ambayo ni mojawapo ya matukio mabaya ya mara kwa mara wakati wa utoaji wa huduma na huathiri mamia ya mamilioni ya wagonjwa duniani kote kila mwaka. Ikiwa sepsis inakua wakati wa ujauzito, wakati au baada ya kuzaa, au baada ya kutoa mimba, inaitwa sepsis ya uzazi. Sepsis katika watoto wachanga huitwa sepsis ya watoto wachanga. Licha ya kuzuilika kwa kiwango cha juu, ugonjwa wa sepsis wa mama na wachanga unaendelea kuwa sababu kuu ya vifo kwa wajawazito na watoto wachanga.
translations.reference: In the community setting, sepsis often presents as the clinical deterioration of common and preventable infections. Sepsis also frequently results from infections acquired in health care settings, which are one of the most frequent adverse events during care delivery and affect hundreds of millions of patients worldwide every year. If sepsis develops during pregnancy, while or after giving birth, or after an abortion, it is called maternal sepsis. Sepsis in newborn babies is called neonatal sepsis. Despite being highly preventable, maternal and neonatal sepsis continues to be a major cause of death for pregnant women and newborn babies.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"In community settings, sepsis often manifests as a deterioration of common and preventable illnesses. Sepsis also frequently arises from infections acquired in healthcare settings, which is one of the most common adverse events during service delivery and affects hundreds of millions of patients worldwide each year. If sepsis develops during pregnancy, labor, or after childbirth, or after induced abortion, it is called obstetric sepsis. Sepsis in newborns is called neonatal sepsis. Despite being largely preventable, maternal and neonatal sepsis continues to be a major cause of death for pregnant women and newborns."

translations.source: Dalili: Sepsis ni dharura ya kimatibabu na inahitaji uangalizi wa haraka ili kuzuia matatizo zaidi au kifo. Inaweza kuwasilisha ishara na dalili tofauti tofauti wakati mwingine, ambazo zinaweza kusababisha kutotambuliwa na hali zingine. Maambukizi ya septic yanaweza kusababisha homa, lakini pia inaweza kusababisha joto la chini la mwili, ncha za baridi na kutetemeka. Kunaweza kuwa na ugumu wa kupumua au kupumua kwa haraka, pamoja na kuongezeka kwa kiwango cha moyo, au kinyume chake mapigo dhaifu na shinikizo la chini la damu. Inaweza kusababisha kutokwa kwa mkojo kidogo, pamoja na ngozi yenye rangi ya buluu, madoadoa au iliyopauka isivyo kawaida, na kubadilika kwa hali ya akili.
translations.reference: Symptoms: Sepsis is a medical emergency and requires immediate attention to prevent further complications or death. It may present different warning signs and symptoms at times, some of which may cause misidentification with other conditions. A septic infection may cause a fever, but may also cause low body temperature, cold extremities and shivering. There may be difficulty breathing or rapid breathing, as well as an increased heart rate, or conversely a weak pulse and low blood pressure. It may cause low urine output, as well as blue-tinted, mottled or abnormally pale skin, and an altered mental state.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

**Symptoms: Sepsis is a medical emergency and requires prompt monitoring to prevent further complications or death. It can present different and varied signs and symptoms at times, which can lead to misdiagnosis with other conditions. Septic infections can cause fever, but can also cause a low body temperature, cold extremities, and chills. There may be difficulty breathing or rapid breathing, along with an increased heart rate, or conversely weak pulses and low blood pressure. It can cause reduced urine output, along with bluish skin, unusual rashes or peeling, and altered mental status.**

translations.source: Hatimaye, inaweza kusababisha maumivu makali ya mwili au usumbufu, ambayo inafanya kuwa rahisi kuchanganya kwa mafua au sumu ya chakula. Kwa watoto, sepsis inaweza kutokea kwa kupumua kwa haraka sana, degedege, ngozi iliyopauka, uchovu au ugumu wa kuamka au kuhisi baridi isivyo kawaida kwa kuguswa. Kwa watoto chini ya miaka 5, inaweza kusababisha ugumu wa kulisha, kutapika mara kwa mara au ukosefu wa mkojo. Kushuku sepsis na kuchukua hatua haraka ni muhimu kwa utambuzi wa mapema na utambuzi. Matibabu: Utambulisho wa mapema wa dalili na uanzishwaji wa wakati unaofaa wa usimamizi wa kliniki unaofaa ni vipengele muhimu vya kupunguza hatari na athari za sepsis.
translations.reference: Finally, it may cause extreme body pain or discomfort, which makes it easy to confuse for influenza or food poisoning. In children, sepsis may present through very fast breathing, convulsions, pale skin, lethargy or difficulty waking up or feeling abnormally cold to the touch. For children under 5 years, it may cause difficulty feeding, repeated vomiting or a lack of urination. Suspecting sepsis and acting quickly is crucial for early recognition and diagnosis. Treatment: Early identification of symptoms and timely establishment of appropriate clinical management are critical elements to reducing the risk and impact of sepsis.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Finally, it can lead to severe physical pain or discomfort, which makes it easy to confuse it with the flu or food poisoning. In children, sepsis can occur with rapid breathing, lethargy, peeling skin, fatigue, or difficulty waking up or feeling unusually cold to the touch. In children under 5, it can cause difficulty feeding, frequent vomiting, or lack of urination. Suspecting sepsis and taking action quickly is crucial for early diagnosis and treatment. Treatment: Early identification of symptoms and timely initiation of appropriate clinical management are key components of reducing the risk and effects of sepsis.

translations.source: Ugunduzi wa baadhi ya vialama, kama vile protini tendaji ya C na procalcitonin, unaweza kuwa na ufanisi katika kutambua hali hiyo. Baada ya utambuzi wa mapema, uchunguzi wa kusaidia kutambua kisababishi magonjwa ya kuambukiza inayoongoza kwa sepsis ni muhimu ili kuongoza matibabu yaliyolengwa ya antimicrobial. Tiba inayofaa ya antimicrobial (kwa mfano, antibiotiki na antifungal) inapaswa kutolewa ndani ya saa moja baada ya kutambuliwa kwa sepsis. Ukinzani wa viua viini (AMR) unaweza kuhatarisha usimamizi wa kimatibabu wa sepsis kwa sababu matibabu ya majaribio ya viuavijasumu mara nyingi yanahitajika. Ufufuaji wa maji ya mapema ni muhimu katika awamu ya awali na vasopressors zinaweza kuhitajika ili kuboresha na kudumisha upenyezaji wa tishu.
translations.reference: The detection of some biomarkers, such as C reactive protein and procalcitonin, can be effective in diagnosing the condition. After early recognition, diagnostics to help identify a causal pathogen of infection leading to sepsis are important to guide targeted antimicrobial treatment. The appropriate antimicrobial therapy (for example, antibiotics and antifungals) should be given within one hour of sepsis identification. Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) can jeopardize clinical management of sepsis because empirical antibiotic treatment is often required. Early fluid resuscitation is important in the initial phase and vasopressors may be required to improve and maintain tissue perfusion.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"The discovery of certain biomarkers, such as C-reactive protein and procalcitonin, can be effective in identifying the condition. Following early diagnosis, investigations to identify the causative agent of the infectious disease leading to sepsis are crucial to guide targeted antimicrobial therapy. Appropriate antimicrobial treatment (e.g., antibiotics and antifungals) should be administered within one hour of sepsis recognition. Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) can jeopardize the clinical management of sepsis because empirical treatment with antimicrobials is often required. Early fluid resuscitation is essential in the initial phase and vasopressors may be needed to improve and maintain tissue perfusion."

translations.source: Kuna hatua mbili kuu za kuzuia sepsis: Kuzuia maambukizi ya microbial na maambukizi Kuzuia maambukizi yanayobadilika kuwa sepsis Ndani ya jamii, kuzuia sepsis husaidiwa kwa kutumia njia bora za usafi, kuboresha usafi wa mazingira na ubora wa maji na upatikanaji, kutoa upatikanaji wa chanjo, hasa kwa wale walio katika hatari kubwa, pamoja na lishe sahihi, ikiwa ni pamoja na kunyonyesha kwa watoto wachanga. Kinga katika vituo vya kutolea huduma za afya hutegemea programu za kuzuia na kudhibiti maambukizi (IPC), kanuni bora za usafi, na vifaa safi vinavyofanya kazi vizuri.
translations.reference: There are two main steps to preventing sepsis: Prevention of microbial transmission and infection Prevention of an infection evolving into sepsis Within the community, sepsis prevention is assisted through the use of effective hygiene practices, improving sanitation and water quality and availability, providing access to vaccines, particularly for those at high risk, as well as appropriate nutrition, including breastfeeding for newborns. Prevention in health care facilities relies on infection prevention and control (IPC) programmes, effective hygiene practices, and clean, well-functioning equipment.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"There are two main steps to prevent sepsis: Preventing microbial infections and infections Preventing infections from turning into sepsis. Within the community, sepsis prevention is supported by using good hygiene practices, improving environmental sanitation and water quality and accessibility, providing access to vaccinations, especially for those at high risk, as well as proper nutrition, including breastfeeding for infants. Immunity in healthcare delivery centers relies on programs to prevent and control infections (IPC), best hygiene practices, and functional clean equipment."

translations.source: Afya ya ngono Muhtasari: Afya ya ngono ni msingi kwa afya na ustawi wa jumla wa watu binafsi, wanandoa na familia, na kwa maendeleo ya kijamii na kiuchumi ya jumuiya na nchi. Afya ya ngono, inapotazamwa kwa uthibitisho, inahitaji mtazamo chanya na wa heshima kwa ujinsia na mahusiano ya ngono, pamoja na uwezekano wa kuwa na uzoefu wa kijinsia wa kufurahisha na salama, bila kulazimishwa, ubaguzi na unyanyasaji. Uwezo wa wanaume na wanawake kufikia afya ya ngono na ustawi hutegemea: upatikanaji wa taarifa za kina, za ubora mzuri kuhusu ngono na ujinsia;
translations.reference: Sexual health Overview: Sexual health is fundamental to the overall health and well-being of individuals, couples and families, and to the social and economic development of communities and countries. Sexual health, when viewed affirmatively, requires a positive and respectful approach to sexuality and sexual relationships, as well as the possibility of having pleasurable and safe sexual experiences, free of coercion, discrimination and violence. The ability of men and women to achieve sexual health and well-being depends on their: access to comprehensive, good-quality information about sex and sexuality;
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

**Sexual Health Summary:** Sexual health is fundamental to the overall health and well-being of individuals, couples, and families, and to the social and economic development of communities and countries. Sexual health, when addressed with evidence, requires a positive and respectful approach to gender and sexual relationships, as well as the possibility of experiencing pleasurable and safe sexual experiences, without coercion, discrimination, and abuse. The ability of men and women to achieve sexual health and well-being depends on: access to comprehensive, high-quality information about sex and gender;

translations.source: maarifa juu ya hatari wanazoweza kukabiliana nazo na kuathirika kwao kwa matokeo mabaya ya shughuli za ngono zisizo salama; uwezo wa kupata huduma ya afya ya ngono; kuishi katika mazingira ambayo yanathibitisha na kukuza afya ya ngono. Masuala yanayohusiana na afya ya ngono ni mapana, na yanajumuisha mwelekeo wa kijinsia na utambulisho wa kijinsia, kujieleza kingono, mahusiano na furaha. Pia ni pamoja na matokeo mabaya au masharti kama vile:
translations.reference: knowledge about the risks they may face and their vulnerability to adverse consequences of unprotected sexual activity; ability to access sexual health care; living in an environment that affirms and promotes sexual health. Sexual health-related issues are wide-ranging, and encompass sexual orientation and gender identity, sexual expression, relationships, and pleasure. They also include negative consequences or conditions such as:

Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

Issues related to sexual health are broad, and include gender orientation and gender identity, sexual expression, relationships, and well-being. It also includes negative outcomes or conditions such as:"

translations.source: maambukizo ya virusi vya ukimwi (VVU), magonjwa ya zinaa (STIs) na magonjwa ya njia ya uzazi (RTIs) na matokeo yake mabaya (kama vile saratani na utasa); mimba zisizotarajiwa na utoaji mimba; dysfunction ya ngono; ukatili wa kijinsia; na vitendo vyenye madhara (kama vile ukeketaji, FGM).
translations.reference: infections with human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), sexually transmitted infections (STIs) and reproductive tract infections (RTIs) and their adverse outcomes (such as cancer and infertility); unintended pregnancy and abortion; sexual dysfunction; sexual violence; and harmful practices (such as female genital mutilation, FGM).
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"HIV infection (AIDS), sexually transmitted infections (STIs), and reproductive tract infections (RTIs) and their adverse consequences (such as cancer and infertility); unintended pregnancies and abortions; sexual dysfunction; gender-based violence; and harmful practices (such as female genital mutilation, FGM)."

translations.source: Ufafanuzi: WHO imekuwa ikifanya kazi katika eneo la afya ya ngono tangu angalau 1974, wakati mijadala ya kamati ya wataalamu ilisababisha kuchapishwa kwa ripoti ya kiufundi yenye kichwa "Elimu na matibabu katika ujinsia wa binadamu" (WHO, 1975). Kuanzia mwaka wa 2000, Shirika la Afya la Pan American (PAHO) na WHO waliitisha idadi ya mashauriano ya wataalam ili kukagua istilahi na kutambua chaguzi za programu. Kilele cha mikutano hii kilikuwa Mashauriano ya Kiufundi mwaka 2002, ambapo ufafanuzi wa kazi wa masharti muhimu yanayohusiana na afya ya ngono hapa chini yalitayarishwa. Jinsia: jinsia inarejelea sifa za kibayolojia ambazo hufafanua binadamu kama mwanamke au mwanamume. Ingawa seti hizi za sifa za kibayolojia hazitengani, kwani kuna watu binafsi ambao wana zote mbili, huwa na tabia ya kuwatofautisha wanadamu kama wanaume na wanawake.
translations.reference: Definitions: WHO has been working in the area of sexual health since at least 1974, when the deliberations of an expert committee resulted in the publication of a technical report entitled “Education and treatment in human sexuality” (WHO, 1975). Starting in 2000, the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) and WHO convened a number of expert consultations to review terminology and identify programme options. The culmination of these meetings was a Technical Consultation in 2002, where the working definitions of the key sexual health-related terms below were developed. Sex: Sex refers to the biological characteristics that define humans as female or male. While these sets of biological characteristics are not mutually exclusive, as there are individuals who possess both, they tend to differentiate humans as males and females.
translations.hypothesis: Explanation: WHO has been working in the field of sexual health since at least 1974, when committee discussions led to the publication of a technical report entitled "Education and treatment in human sexuality" (WHO, 1975). Starting in 2000, the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) and WHO convened a number of expert consultations to review terminology and identify program options. The culmination of these meetings was the Technical Consultation in 2002, where the working definitions of key terms related to sexual health listed below were prepared. Sex: sex refers to the biological characteristics that define humans as female or male. Although these sets of biological characteristics are not distinct, as there are individuals who have both, they tend to distinguish humans as male and female.

translations.source: Katika matumizi ya jumla katika lugha nyingi, neno ngono au jinsia mara nyingi hutumika kumaanisha "shughuli za ngono", lakini kwa madhumuni ya kiufundi katika muktadha wa mijadala ya ujinsia na afya ya ngono, ufafanuzi ulio hapo juu unapendekezwa. Afya ya ngono Kulingana na ufafanuzi wa sasa wa kufanya kazi, afya ya ngono ni: “…hali ya ustawi wa kimwili, kihisia, kiakili na kijamii kuhusiana na kujamiiana; sio tu kukosekana kwa ugonjwa, kutofanya kazi au udhaifu. Afya ya ngono inahitaji mtazamo chanya na wa heshima kwa ujinsia na uhusiano wa kimapenzi, pamoja na uwezekano wa kuwa na uzoefu wa kijinsia wa kufurahisha na salama, usio na shuruti, ubaguzi na unyanyasaji. Ili afya ya ngono ipatikane na kudumishwa, haki za ngono za watu wote lazima ziheshimiwe, zilindwe na kutimizwa.” (WHO, 2006a)
translations.reference: In general use in many languages, the term sex is often used to mean “sexual activity”, but for technical purposes in the context of sexuality and sexual health discussions, the above definition is preferred. Sexual health According to the current working definition, sexual health is:“…a state of physical, emotional, mental and social well-being in relation to sexuality; it is not merely the absence of disease, dysfunction or infirmity. Sexual health requires a positive and respectful approach to sexuality and sexual relationships, as well as the possibility of having pleasurable and safe sexual experiences, free of coercion, discrimination and violence. For sexual health to be attained and maintained, the sexual rights of all persons must be respected, protected and fulfilled.” (WHO, 2006a)
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"In general usage across many languages, the word sex or gender is often used to mean "sexual activity," but for technical purposes within the context of discussions on gender and sexual health, the definition above is recommended. Sexual Health According to the current working definition, sexual health is: “…a state of physical, emotional, mental and social well-being related to sexuality; not merely the absence of disease, dysfunction, or infirmity. Sexual health requires a positive and respectful approach to sexuality and intimate relationships, as well as the ability to have pleasurable and safe sexual experiences, free of coercion, discrimination and violence. For sexual health to be achieved and maintained, the sexual rights of all people must be respected, protected, and fulfilled.” (WHO, 2006a)"

translations.source: Ujinsia: Afya ya ngono haiwezi kufafanuliwa, kueleweka au kufanywa kufanya kazi bila kuzingatia kwa mapana ujinsia, ambayo ni msingi wa tabia na matokeo muhimu yanayohusiana na afya ya ngono. Ufafanuzi wa kazi wa ujinsia ni: “… kipengele kikuu cha kuwa binadamu katika maisha yote kinajumuisha ngono, utambulisho wa kijinsia na majukumu, mwelekeo wa kijinsia, hisia za kimapenzi, furaha, ukaribu na uzazi. Ujinsia ni uzoefu na kuonyeshwa katika mawazo, fantasia, tamaa, imani, mitazamo, maadili, tabia, mazoea, majukumu na mahusiano. Ingawa kujamiiana kunaweza kujumuisha vipimo hivi vyote, sio vyote vina uzoefu au kuonyeshwa kila wakati. Ujinsia huathiriwa na mwingiliano wa mambo ya kibayolojia, kisaikolojia, kijamii, kiuchumi, kisiasa, kitamaduni, kisheria, kihistoria, kidini na kiroho. (WHO, 2006a)
translations.reference: Sexuality: Sexual health cannot be defined, understood or made operational without a broad consideration of sexuality, which underlies important behaviours and outcomes related to sexual health. The working definition of sexuality is:“…a central aspect of being human throughout life encompasses sex, gender identities and roles, sexual orientation, eroticism, pleasure, intimacy and reproduction. Sexuality is experienced and expressed in thoughts, fantasies, desires, beliefs, attitudes, values, behaviours, practices, roles and relationships. While sexuality can include all of these dimensions, not all of them are always experienced or expressed. Sexuality is influenced by the interaction of biological, psychological, social, economic, political, cultural, legal, historical, religious and spiritual factors.” (WHO, 2006a)
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Swahili document into English:

**Gender: Sexual health cannot be defined, understood, or effectively addressed without considering the broad spectrum of gender, which is fundamental to key behaviors and outcomes related to sexual health. A functional definition of gender is: “…a central aspect of being human throughout life encompasses sex, gender identity and roles, sexual orientation, romantic feelings, pleasure, intimacy, and reproduction. Gender is experienced and expressed in thoughts, fantasies, desires, beliefs, attitudes, behaviors, practices, roles, and relationships. While sexual interaction may encompass all of these dimensions, not all are experienced or expressed at all times. Gender is influenced by the interaction of biological, psychological, social, economic, political, cultural, legal, historical, religious, and spiritual factors. (WHO, 2006a)**

translations.source: Haki za kujamiiana: Kuna maafikiano yanayokua kwamba afya ya ngono haiwezi kupatikana na kudumishwa bila kuheshimu, na kulindwa, baadhi ya haki za binadamu. Ufafanuzi wa kufanya kazi wa haki za ngono uliotolewa hapa chini ni mchango katika mazungumzo yanayoendelea kuhusu haki za binadamu kuhusiana na afya ya ngono. Utumiaji wa haki za binadamu zilizopo kwa ujinsia na afya ya ngono hujumuisha haki za ngono. Haki za kujamiiana zinalinda haki za watu wote kutimiza na kueleza ujinsia wao na kufurahia afya ya ngono, kwa kuzingatia haki za wengine na ndani ya mfumo wa ulinzi dhidi ya ubaguzi." WHO, 2006a, updated 2010)“Utimilifu wa afya ya ngono unafungamana na kiwango ambacho haki za binadamu zinaheshimiwa, kulindwa na kutimizwa.
translations.reference: Sexual rights: There is a growing consensus that sexual health cannot be achieved and maintained without respect for, and protection of, certain human rights. The working definition of sexual rights given below is a contribution to the continuing dialogue on human rights related to sexual health. The application of existing human rights to sexuality and sexual health constitute sexual rights. Sexual rights protect all people's rights to fulfil and express their sexuality and enjoy sexual health, with due regard for the rights of others and within a framework of protection against discrimination." (WHO, 2006a, updated 2010)“The fulfilment of sexual health is tied to the extent to which human rights are respected, protected and fulfilled.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Swahili document into English:

"Sexual Rights: There is growing consensus that sexual health cannot be achieved and maintained without respecting and protecting certain human rights. The working definition of sexual rights provided below is a contribution to the ongoing dialogue about human rights in relation to sexual health. The application of existing human rights to sexuality and sexual health includes sexual rights. Sexual rights protect the rights of all people to express their sexuality and to enjoy sexual health, respecting the rights of others and within a system of protection against discrimination." (WHO, 2006a, updated 2010) "The fulfillment of sexual health is linked to the extent to which human rights are respected, protected, and realized."

translations.source: Haki za ngono zinajumuisha haki fulani za binadamu ambazo tayari zinatambuliwa katika hati za kimataifa na za kikanda za haki za binadamu na hati nyingine za makubaliano na katika sheria za kitaifa. Haki muhimu kwa utambuzi wa afya ya ngono ni pamoja na: Haki muhimu kwa utambuzi wa afya ya ngono ni pamoja na: Haki muhimu kwa utambuzi wa afya ya ngono ni pamoja na: haki za usawa na kutobaguliwa
translations.reference: Sexual rights embrace certain human rights that are already recognized in international and regional human rights documents and other consensus documents and in national laws. Rights critical to the realization of sexual health include: Rights critical to the realization of sexual health include: Rights critical to the realization of sexual health include: the rights to equality and non-discrimination

Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Sexual rights include certain human rights that are already recognized in international and regional human rights instruments, other treaties, and national laws. Key rights for the recognition of sexual health include: Key rights for the recognition of sexual health include: Key rights for the recognition of sexual health include: rights to equality and non-discrimination.

translations.source: haki ya kuwa huru kutokana na mateso au kutendewa kikatili, kinyama au kudhalilisha au kuadhibiwa haki ya faragha haki za kufikia kiwango cha juu zaidi cha afya (ikiwa ni pamoja na afya ya ngono) na usalama wa kijamii haki ya kuoa na kuanzisha familia na kuingia kwenye ndoa kwa ridhaa ya bure na kamili ya wanaokusudia kuoana, na usawa ndani na katika kuvunjika kwa ndoa. haki ya kuamua idadi na nafasi ya watoto
translations.reference: the right to be free from torture or to cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment or punishment the right to privacy the rights to the highest attainable standard of health (including sexual health) and social security the right to marry and to found a family and enter into marriage with the free and full consent of the intending spouses, and to equality in and at the dissolution of marriage the right to decide the number and spacing of one's children

Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"The right to be free from torture or to be treated cruelly, inhumanly or with degradation or punishment, the right to privacy, the rights to achieve the highest possible standard of health (including sexual health) and social security, the right to marry and found a family and to enter into marriage freely and fully with the consent of the intending spouses, and equality within and upon dissolution of marriage. The right to decide on the number and spacing of children."

translations.source: haki ya kupata taarifa, pamoja na elimu haki ya uhuru wa maoni na kujieleza, na haki ya kupata suluhisho la ufanisi kwa ukiukwaji wa haki za kimsingi. Utekelezaji wa uwajibikaji wa haki za binadamu unahitaji kwamba watu wote waheshimu haki za wengine. Masuala: "Haki za binadamu hutoa mfumo wa kisheria wa kimataifa ambamo mahitaji na matarajio ya afya ya ngono na uzazi ya watu wote yanaweza kuzingatiwa." (GRUSKIN, 2005)
translations.reference: the rights to information, as well as education the rights to freedom of opinion and expression, and the right to an effective remedy for violations of fundamental rights. The responsible exercise of human rights requires that all persons respect the rights of others. Issues: “Human rights provide an international legal framework within which the sexual and reproductive health needs and aspirations of all people can be considered.” (GRUSKIN, 2005)
