Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"The right to information, including education, the right to freedom of opinion and expression, and the right to effective remedies for violations of fundamental rights. The implementation of human rights accountability requires that all people respect the rights of others. Issues: "Human rights provide a legal framework within which the needs and expectations of the sexual and reproductive health of all people can be considered." (GRUSKIN, 2005)"

translations.source: Katika miaka ya hivi majuzi, sheria za kimataifa za haki za binadamu zimekuwa zikitumika zaidi kuunga mkono na kuendeleza madai ya kisheria ya watu binafsi na jumuiya nzima ili serikali za kitaifa zihakikishe heshima, ulinzi na utimilifu wa haki zao za afya ya ngono na uzazi (Cook et al., 2003). Ufafanuzi wa kazi na mfumo wa utayarishaji uliowasilishwa hapa umejikita katika haki za binadamu zinazotambulika kimataifa na hutoa mkabala unaozingatia haki za upangaji programu katika afya ya ngono. Vipengele muhimu vya dhana ya afya ya ngono vimeorodheshwa hapa chini. Vipengele muhimu vya dhana ya afya ya ngono Ikitazamwa kwa ujumla na chanya:
translations.reference: In recent years, international human rights instruments have been increasingly used to support and advance legal claims by individuals and whole communities so that national governments will guarantee the respect, protection and fulfilment of their sexual and reproductive health rights (Cook et al., 2003). The working definitions and framework for programming presented here are grounded in internationally recognized human rights and offer a rights-based approach to programming in sexual health. The key conceptual elements of sexual health are listed below. Key conceptual elements of sexual health When viewed holistically and positively:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"In recent years, international human rights laws have been increasingly used to support and advance legal claims of individuals and entire communities to ensure that national governments respect, protect, and fulfill their rights to sexual and reproductive health (Cook et al., 2003). The definition of work and the preparation system presented here are focused on internationally recognized human rights and provide a framework that considers the right to program planning in sexual health. Key elements of the concept of sexual health are listed below. Key elements of the concept of sexual health Viewed holistically and positively:"

translations.source: Afya ya ngono inahusu ustawi, sio tu kutokuwepo kwa ugonjwa. Afya ya ngono inahusisha heshima, usalama na uhuru dhidi ya ubaguzi na unyanyasaji. Afya ya ngono inategemea utimilifu wa haki fulani za binadamu. Afya ya ngono ni muhimu katika maisha yote ya mtu binafsi, sio tu kwa wale walio katika miaka ya uzazi, lakini pia kwa vijana na wazee. Afya ya ngono inaonyeshwa kupitia jinsia tofauti na aina za kujieleza kwa ngono.
translations.reference: Sexual health is about well-being, not merely the absence of disease. Sexual health involves respect, safety and freedom from discrimination and violence. Sexual health depends on the fulfilment of certain human rights. Sexual health is relevant throughout the individual’s lifespan, not only to those in the reproductive years, but also to both the young and the elderly. Sexual health is expressed through diverse sexualities and forms of sexual expression.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Sexual health refers to well-being, not just the absence of disease. Sexual health involves dignity, safety, and freedom from discrimination and abuse. Sexual health depends on the fulfillment of certain human rights. Sexual health is important throughout a person's life, not just for those of reproductive age, but also for young people and the elderly. Sexual health is expressed through different genders and types of sexual expression.

translations.source: Afya ya ngono inathiriwa sana na kanuni za kijinsia, majukumu, matarajio na mienendo ya nguvu. Afya ya ngono inahitaji kueleweka ndani ya miktadha mahususi ya kijamii, kiuchumi na kisiasa.
translations.reference: Sexual health is critically influenced by gender norms, roles, expectations and power dynamics. Sexual health needs to be understood within specific social, economic and political contexts.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Sexual health is greatly influenced by gender norms, roles, expectations, and power dynamics. Sexual health needs to be understood within specific social, economic, and political contexts.

translations.source: Maambukizi ya zinaa (STIs) Muhtasari: Maambukizi ya zinaa (STIs) huenezwa zaidi na kujamiiana bila kinga. Baadhi ya magonjwa ya zinaa yanaweza pia kuambukizwa wakati wa ujauzito, kujifungua na kunyonyesha na kupitia damu iliyoambukizwa au bidhaa za damu. Magonjwa ya zinaa yana athari kubwa kwa afya. Iwapo haitatibiwa, inaweza kusababisha madhara makubwa ikiwa ni pamoja na ugonjwa wa mishipa ya fahamu na mishipa ya damu, ugumba, mimba nje ya kizazi, kujifungua mtoto mfu, na hatari ya kuongezeka ya Virusi vya Ukimwi (VVU).
translations.reference: Sexually transmitted infections (STIs) Overview: Sexually transmitted infections (STIs) are spread predominantly by unprotected sexual contact. Some STIs can also be transmitted during pregnancy, childbirth and breastfeeding and through infected blood or blood products. STIs have a profound impact on health. If untreated, they can lead to serious consequences including neurological and cardiovascular disease, infertility, ectopic pregnancy, stillbirths, and increased risk of Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV).
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

**Sexually Transmitted Infections (STIs) Summary:** Sexually transmitted infections (STIs) are most commonly spread through unprotected sexual intercourse. Some STIs can also be transmitted during pregnancy, childbirth, and breastfeeding, and through infected blood or blood products. STIs have a significant impact on health. If left untreated, they can cause serious consequences including neurological and cardiovascular disease, infertility, ectopic pregnancy, stillbirth, and an increased risk of HIV.

translations.source: Pia zinahusishwa na unyanyapaa, unyanyasaji wa nyumbani, na huathiri ubora wa maisha. Magonjwa mengi ya zinaa hayana dalili. Zinapokuwapo dalili za kawaida za magonjwa ya zinaa ni kutokwa na uchafu ukeni au urethra, kidonda sehemu ya siri na maumivu chini ya tumbo. Magonjwa ya zinaa ya kawaida na yanayotibika ni trichomonas, chlamydia, kisonono na kaswende. Kuongezeka kwa kasi kwa upinzani wa antimicrobial ni tishio linalokua kwa kisonono isiyoweza kutibika.
translations.reference: They are also associated with stigma, domestic violence, and affects quality of life. The majority of STIs have no symptoms. When they are present common symptoms of STIs are vaginal or urethral discharge, genital ulcer and lower abdominal pain. The most common and curable STIs are trichomonas, chlamydia, gonorrhoea and syphilis. Rapidly increasing antimicrobial resistance is a growing threat for untreatable gonorrhoea.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"They are also associated with abuse, domestic violence, and affect the quality of life. Many sexually transmitted infections (STIs) have no symptoms. When common STI symptoms are present, they include discharge from the vagina or urethra, sores in the genital area, and abdominal pain. Common and treatable STIs include trichomoniasis, chlamydia, gonorrhea, and syphilis. The rapid increase in antimicrobial resistance is a growing threat to untreatable gonorrhea."

translations.source: Magonjwa ya zinaa yatokanayo na VVU, virusi vya herpes simplex ya sehemu za siri (HSV), virusi vya hepatitis B, human papillomavirus (HPV) na virusi vya human T-lymphotropic virus aina 1 (HTLV-1) hayana au yana chaguo chache za matibabu. Chanjo zinapatikana kwa hepatitis B ili kuzuia maambukizi ambayo yanaweza kusababisha saratani ya ini na kwa HPV kuzuia saratani ya shingo ya kizazi. VVU, HSV na HTLV-1 ni maambukizi ya maisha yote: kwa VVU na HSV kuna matibabu ambayo yanaweza kukandamiza virusi, lakini kwa sasa hakuna tiba kwa yoyote ya magonjwa haya ya zinaa ya virusi. Kondomu zinazotumiwa kwa usahihi na mara kwa mara ni njia bora za kujikinga na magonjwa ya zinaa na VVU. Uchunguzi na utambuzi wa mapema wa watu wenye magonjwa ya zinaa na wapenzi wao wa ngono hutoa fursa bora ya matibabu ya ufanisi na kuzuia matatizo na maambukizi zaidi.
translations.reference: Viral STIs including HIV, genital herpes simplex virus (HSV), viral hepatitis B, human papillomavirus (HPV) and human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) lack or have limited treatment options. Vaccines are available for hepatitis B to prevent infection that can lead to liver cancer and for HPV to prevent cervical cancer. HIV, HSV and HTLV-1 are lifelong infections: for HIV and HSV there are treatments that can suppress the virus, but currently there are no cures for any of these viral STIs. Condoms used correctly and consistently are effective methods to protect against STIs and HIV. Screening with early diagnosis of people with STIs and their sexual partners offers the best opportunity for effective treatment and for preventing complications and further transmission.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Sexually transmitted infections (STIs) caused by HIV, herpes simplex virus (HSV), hepatitis B virus, human papillomavirus (HPV), and human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) have no or few treatment options. Vaccines are available for hepatitis B to prevent infection that can lead to liver cancer and for HPV to prevent cervical cancer. HIV, HSV, and HTLV-1 are lifelong infections: for HIV and HSV there are treatments that can suppress the virus, but currently there is no cure for any of these viral STIs. Condoms used correctly and consistently are the best ways to protect against STIs and HIV. Screening and early diagnosis of people with STIs and their sexual partners provide the best opportunity for effective treatment and to prevent complications and further infections.

translations.source: Dalili: Mtu anaweza kuwa na magonjwa ya zinaa bila kuwa na dalili za wazi za ugonjwa huo. Dalili za kawaida za magonjwa ya zinaa zinapoonekana ni pamoja na: kutokwa na uchafu usio wa kawaida, kutokwa na urethra, vidonda vya sehemu za siri na uvimbe, na maumivu chini ya tumbo. Dalili za magonjwa ya zinaa maalum ni: Kisonono na maambukizi ya chlamydial Magonjwa haya ya zinaa husababisha cervicitis kwa wanawake, urethritis kwa wanaume na maambukizi ya nje ya uzazi, ikiwa ni pamoja na maonyesho ya rectal na oropharyngeal.
translations.reference: Symptoms: A person can have an STI without having obvious symptoms of disease. When present, common symptoms of STIs include: abnormal vaginal discharge, urethral discharge, genital ulcers and lumps, and lower abdominal pain. Symptoms of specific STIs are: Gonorrhoea and chlamydial infection These STIs cause cervicitis in women, urethritis in men and extra-genital infections, including rectal and oropharyngeal manifestations.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

**Symptoms:** A person can have sexually transmitted infections (STIs) without having obvious symptoms of the infection. Common symptoms of STIs, when they appear, include: unusual discharge, urethral discharge, sores on the genitals and swelling, and abdominal pain. Specific STI symptoms are: Gonorrhea and Chlamydial infections. These STIs cause cervicitis in women, urethritis in men, and extra-genital infections, including rectal and oropharyngeal manifestations.

translations.source: Dalili za kawaida ni pamoja na kutokwa na uchafu ukeni au uume na kuungua kwa kukojoa. Watoto wachanga wa akina mama walioambukizwa wanaweza kuambukizwa kiwambo cha sikio cha mtoto mchanga (macho mekundu) kutokana na kuathiriwa na magonjwa ya zinaa wakati wa kujifungua ukeni. Maambukizi ya rectal na pharyngeal yanaweza kuwa bila dalili. Kaswende: Kaswende mara nyingi haina dalili, dalili zinapotokea, kaswende ya msingi hujidhihirisha kama kidonda cha pekee kisicho na uchungu. Kaswende ya pili inaweza kujidhihirisha kama vidonda vya jumla vinavyoathiri ngozi, utando wa mucous na nodi ya limfu ikijumuisha upele wa kawaida kwenye viganja vya mikono na nyayo za miguu.
translations.reference: Common symptoms include vaginal or penile discharge and burning with urination. Infants of infected mothers can contract neonatal conjunctivitis (red eyes) due to exposure to the STIs during vaginal delivery. Rectal and pharyngeal infections can be asymptomatic. Syphilis: Syphilis is often asymptomatic, when symptoms occur, primary syphilis presents as a solitary, painless ulcer. Secondary syphilis may manifest as generalized lesions affecting skin, mucous membranes and lymph-node including a classic rash on the palms of the hands and soles of the feet.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Common symptoms include discharge from the vagina or penis and a burning sensation during urination. Newborns of infected mothers can contract childhood ear infections (red eye) due to exposure to sexually transmitted infections during childbirth in the vagina. Rectal and pharyngeal infections may be asymptomatic. Syphilis: Syphilis often has no symptoms; when symptoms do occur, primary syphilis manifests as a single, painless sore. Secondary syphilis can manifest as widespread sores affecting the skin, mucous membranes, and lymph nodes, including a common rash on the palms of the hands and soles of the feet."

translations.source: Kaswende iliyojificha haina dalili na ina tabia ya serolojia chanya ya kaswende. Trichomoniasis: Dalili kuu ni pamoja na kutokwa na uchafu usio wa kawaida wa uke na uwekundu wa uke, kuwasha na kujamiiana kwa maumivu. Virusi vya herpes simplex ya sehemu ya siri (HSV) HSV mara nyingi hujidhihirisha kama vidonda vyenye uchungu, vesicles au vidonda kwenye sehemu ya siri ya nje na mdomo. Dalili ya HSV ya sehemu za siri ni hali ya maisha yote ambayo inaweza kuonyeshwa na kujirudia kwa dalili mara kwa mara.
translations.reference: Latent syphilis is asymptomatic and characterized by positive syphilis serology. Trichomoniasis: The predominant symptoms include abnormal vaginal discharge with redness of the vulva, itching and painful intercourse. Genital herpes simplex virus (HSV) HSV most commonly presents as painful sores, vesicles or ulcerations on the external genitalia and mouth. Symptomatic genital HSV is a lifelong condition that can be characterized by frequent symptomatic recurrences.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Hidden syphilis has no symptoms and has a positive serological behavior for syphilis. Trichomoniasis: The main symptoms include abnormal vaginal discharge and redness, itching, and painful intercourse of the vagina. Herpes simplex virus of the genitals (HSV) HSV often manifests as painful sores, vesicles, or ulcers on the external genitals and mouth. Genital HSV is a lifelong condition that can be expressed with recurring symptoms."

translations.source: Virusi vya T-lymphotropic ya binadamu aina 1 (HTLV-1) Kwa ujumla bila dalili, aina sugu ya HTLV-1 inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa mbaya, ikiwa ni pamoja na T-cell leukemia/lymphoma (ATL) ya watu wazima na hali ya mfumo wa neva inayoendelea inayojulikana kama myelopathy inayohusishwa na HTLV-1 au paraparesis ya kitropiki ya spastic (HAM/TSP). Matibabu: Matibabu madhubuti kwa sasa yanapatikana kwa magonjwa kadhaa ya zinaa STIs. Magonjwa matatu ya zinaa ya kibakteria (klamidia, kisonono na kaswende) na moja ya vimelea vya magonjwa ya zinaa (trichomoniasis) kwa kawaida hutibika kwa kutumia dawa zilizopo, zenye ufanisi za dozi moja au za dozi nyingi regimens ya antibiotiki. Kwa magonjwa ya zinaa ya virusi (VVU, HSV na HTLV-1), dawa zinazofaa zaidi zinazopatikana ni dawa za kuzuia virusi au za saratani (kwa upande wa HTLV-1), ambazo zinaweza kurekebisha mwendo wa magonjwa, ingawa haziwezi kuponya haya matatu.
translations.reference: Human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) Generally asymptomatic, the chronic form of HTLV-1 can cause severe disease, including adult T-cell leukaemia/lymphoma (ATL) and a progressive nervous system condition known as HTLV-1-associated myelopathy or tropical spastic paraparesis (HAM/TSP). Treatment: Effective treatment is currently available for several STIs. Three bacterial STIs (chlamydia, gonorrhoea and syphilis) and one parasitic STI (trichomoniasis) are usually curable with existing, effective single-dose or multiple-dose regimens of antibiotics. For viral STIs (HIV, HSV and HTLV-1), the most effective medications available are antivirals or anti-cancer drugs (in the case of HTLV-1), that can modulate the course of the diseases, though they cannot cure these three diseases.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

Human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) Generally asymptomatic, chronic HTLV-1 infection can lead to serious disease, including adult T-cell leukemia/lymphoma (ATL) and a progressive neurological condition known as HTLV-1-associated myelopathy or tropical spastic paraparesis (HAM/TSP). Treatment: Effective treatments are currently available for several sexually transmitted infections (STIs). Three bacterial STIs (chlamydia, gonorrhea, and syphilis) and one parasitic STI (trichomoniasis) are typically curable using existing, effective single-dose or multiple-dose regimens of antibiotics. For viral STIs (HIV, HSV, and HTLV-1), the most effective drugs available are antiviral or anticancer drugs (in the case of HTLV-1), which can modify the course of the disease, although they cannot cure these three.

translations.source: Ukinzani wa viua viini (AMR) kwa viua vijasumu vinavyotumika kutibu magonjwa ya zinaa STIs, hasa kisonono, umeongezeka kwa kasi katika miaka ya hivi karibuni na umepunguza matokeo ya matibabu yaliyofaulu. Matokeo kutoka kwa Mpango wa sasa wa Ufuatiliaji wa Gonococcal AMR (GASP) yanaonyesha mwelekeo wa viwango vya juu vya ukinzani wa kwinoloni, kuongezeka kwa upinzani wa azithromycin na kuibuka kwa upinzani wa cephalosporins za wigo uliopanuliwa. Kujitokeza kwa kupungua kwa unyeti wa kisonono kwa cephalosporins ya wigo mpana, pamoja na viwango vya juu vya upinzani dhidi ya penicillins, sulfonamides, tetracyclines, quinolones na macrolides hufanya kisonono kuwa kiumbe sugu kwa dawa nyingi. AMR kwa magonjwa mengine ya zinaa STIs, ingawa si ya kawaida, pia ipo, na hivyo kufanya kuzuia na matibabu ya haraka kuwa muhimu. Ili kutibu ipasavyo magonjwa ya zinaa ni muhimu kuchukua dawa zinazofaa za kuua viini, pamoja na kipimo sahihi na muda wa magonjwa ya zinaa ili kuhakikisha matibabu au tiba ya kutosha, na kuzuia ukuzaji wa ukinzani wa viini.
translations.reference: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) to antibiotics used to treat STIs, in particular gonorrhoea, has increased rapidly in recent years and has reduced successful treatment outcomes. Results from the current Gonococcal AMR Surveillance Programme (GASP) show trends of high rates of quinolone resistance, increasing azithromycin resistance and emerging resistance of extended-spectrum cephalosporins. The emergence of decreased susceptibility of gonorrhoea to extended-spectrum cephalosporins, together with established high-levels of resistance to penicillins, sulphonamides, tetracyclines, quinolones and macrolides make gonorrhoea a multidrug-resistant organism. AMR for other STIs, though less common, also exists, making prevention and prompt treatment critical. To adequately treat STIs it is important to take the appropriate antimicrobials, with the correct dose and duration for the specific STI to ensure adequate treatment or cure, and to prevent the development of antimicrobial resistance.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) in Neisseria gonorrhoeae (gonorrhea), the causative agent of sexually transmitted infections (STIs), has increased rapidly in recent years and has reduced successful treatment outcomes. Results from the current Gonococcal Antimicrobial Resistance Surveillance Program (GASP) show trends of high levels of quinolone resistance, increasing resistance to azithromycin, and the emergence of resistance to extended-spectrum cephalosporins. The emergence of decreased susceptibility of gonorrhea to broad-spectrum cephalosporins, along with high rates of resistance to penicillins, sulfonamides, tetracyclines, quinolones, and macrolides, makes gonorrhea a multi-drug resistant organism. AMR in other STIs, although less common, also exists, making prompt prevention and treatment crucial. To appropriately treat STIs, it is essential to use effective antimicrobials, along with correct dosage and duration of treatment for STIs to ensure adequate cure and prevent the development of antimicrobial resistance."

translations.source: Ndui Muhtasari: Ndui ni ugonjwa wa kuambukiza kwa papo hapo unaosababishwa na virusi vya variola, mwanachama wa familia ya orthopoxvirus. Ilikuwa ni moja ya magonjwa mabaya zaidi yanayojulikana kwa wanadamu na kusababisha mamilioni ya vifo kabla ya kutokomezwa. Inaaminika kuwa imekuwepo kwa angalau miaka 3000. Chanjo ya ndui, iliyoundwa na Edward Jenner mnamo 1796, ilikuwa chanjo ya kwanza iliyofanikiwa kutengenezwa.
translations.reference: Smallpox Overview: Smallpox is an acute contagious disease caused by the variola virus, a member of the orthopoxvirus family. It was one of the most devastating diseases known to humanity and caused millions of deaths before it was eradicated. It is believed to have existed for at least 3000 years. The smallpox vaccine, created by Edward Jenner in 1796, was the first successful vaccine to be developed.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Summary: Smallpox is a highly contagious infectious disease caused by the variola virus, a member of the orthopoxvirus family. It was one of the most devastating diseases known to humans and caused millions of deaths before it was eradicated. It is believed to have existed for at least 3000 years. Smallpox vaccination, developed by Edward Jenner in 1796, was the first successful vaccine ever created.**

translations.source: Aliona kuwa wahudumu wa maziwa ambao hapo awali walishika ndui hawakupata ugonjwa wa ndui na alionyesha kuwa chanjo kama hiyo inaweza kutumika kuzuia ugonjwa wa ndui kwa watu wengine. Shirika la Afya Ulimwenguni lilizindua mpango ulioimarishwa wa kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui mnamo 1967. Chanjo iliyoenea na ufuatiliaji ulifanyika duniani kote kwa miaka kadhaa. Kisa cha mwisho kilichojulikana kilikuwa Somalia mnamo 1977. Mnamo 1980 WHO ilitangaza kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui - ugonjwa pekee wa kuambukiza kufikia tofauti hii.
translations.reference: He observed that milkmaids who previously had caught cowpox did not catch smallpox and showed that a similar inoculation could be used to prevent smallpox in other people. The World Health Organization launched an intensified plan to eradicate smallpox in 1967. Widespread immunization and surveillance were conducted around the world for several years. The last known natural case was in Somalia in 1977. In 1980 WHO declared smallpox eradicated – the only infectious disease to achieve this distinction.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

He observed that milkmaids who previously handled smallpox pustules did not contract smallpox and suggested that a similar vaccination could be used to prevent smallpox in other people. The World Health Organization launched an intensified smallpox eradication program in 1967. Widespread vaccination and surveillance were carried out worldwide for several years. The last known case was in Somalia in 1977. In 1980 WHO declared smallpox eradicated – the only infectious disease to have achieved this distinction.

translations.source: Hii inasalia kuwa miongoni mwa mafanikio mashuhuri na makubwa ya afya ya umma katika historia. Dalili: Dalili za awali za ndui ni pamoja na homa kali, uchovu na maumivu makali ya mgongo, na mara chache zaidi, maumivu ya tumbo na kutapika. Siku mbili hadi 3 baadaye virusi hutoa upele wenye tabia ya matuta yaliyojaa kioevu wazi, ambayo baadaye hujaa na usaha na hatimaye kuendeleza ukoko ambao hukauka na kumomonyoka. Upele huanza kwenye uso na mikono, kisha huenea kwa mwili wote. Vidonda vinakua kwenye utando wa mucous wa pua na mdomo na vidonda mara baada ya kuundwa.
translations.reference: This remains among the most notable and profound public health successes in history. Symptoms: Early symptoms of smallpox include high fever, fatigue and severe back pain, and less often, abdominal pain and vomiting. Two to 3 days later the virus produces a characteristic rash with bumps full of a clear liquid, which later fill with pus and finally develop a crust that dries and falls off. The rash begins on the face and hands, then spreads to the rest of the body. Lesions develop in the mucous membranes of the nose and mouth and ulcerate soon after formation.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"This remains among the most celebrated and significant public health achievements in history. Symptoms: Early symptoms of monkeypox include high fever, fatigue, and severe back pain, and sometimes more, abdominal pain and vomiting. Two to 3 days later, the virus produces a characteristic rash of pimple-like lesions filled with clear fluid, which later fill with pus and eventually develop a crust that dries and falls off. The rash begins on the face and hands, then spreads throughout the body. Lesions grow on the mucous membranes of the nose and mouth and sores form shortly after the lesions are created."

translations.source: Ndui huambukizwa kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kupitia matone ya kuambukiza wakati wa kuwasiliana kwa karibu na watu walioambukizwa ambao wana dalili za ugonjwa huo, au katika visa vingine kupitia nguo zilizochafuliwa na matandiko. Ina kipindi cha incubation cha siku 7-17 baada ya kuambukizwa na huambukiza mara tu homa inapoanza. Watu hubakia kuambukizwa hadi vipele vya mwisho vitoke. Ndui iliua hadi asilimia 30 ya visa.Kadi ya utambuzi wa ndui ya WHO Jibu la WHO: Kipindi tangu kutokomezwa kimefafanuliwa na mjadala mrefu na tata unaolenga uharibifu wa hifadhi ya mwisho iliyobaki ya virusi vya variola hai.
translations.reference: Smallpox is transmitted from person to person via infective droplets during close contact with infected people who have symptoms of the disease, or in some cases through contaminated clothing and bedding. It has an incubation period of 7–17 days after exposure and only becomes infectious once a fever develops. People remain infectious until the last scabs fall off. Smallpox was fatal in up to 30% of cases.WHO smallpox recognition card WHO response: The period since eradication has been defined by a lengthy and complex debate focussed on the destruction of the last remaining stocks of live variola virus.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

Smallpox is transmitted from person to person through infectious droplets during close contact with infected people who have symptoms of the disease, or in other cases through clothing contaminated with bedding. It has an incubation period of 7-17 days after infection and becomes infectious as soon as fever begins. People remain infectious until the last rash appears. Smallpox killed up to 30 percent of cases. WHO Smallpox Diagnostic Card WHO Response: The period since eradication has been defined by a long and complex discussion focusing on the destruction of the last remaining stock of live variola virus.

translations.source: Mnamo 1996, katika Mkutano wa 49 wa Afya Ulimwenguni, Nchi Wanachama ziliamua kuharibu hisa mnamo Juni 1999, na kuruhusu tu sampuli ndogo kuhifadhiwa kwa madhumuni ya utafiti katika maabara mbili zilizowekwa salama - moja nchini Merika na moja katika Urusi. Uhifadhi wa muda wa hifadhi zilizopo za virusi vya variola hutumiwa kwa utafiti zaidi muhimu, ambao unasimamiwa na Kamati ya Ushauri ya Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola. Kikundi hiki hukutana kila mwaka. Sekretarieti ya Ugonjwa wa Ndui ya WHO iliyoko Makao Makuu ya WHO inaratibu shughuli zote zinazohusiana na kutokomeza ndui na baada ya kutokomeza. Hizi ni pamoja na kusimamia utafiti unaoidhinishwa na virusi vya variola hai kwa ajili ya kutengeneza hatua za kupinga kama vile chanjo na dawa za kuzuia virusi.
translations.reference: In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States decided to have this stock destroyed in June 1999, and only to allow small samples to be kept for research purposes at two designated secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in the Russian Federation. Temporary retention of the existing stocks of variola virus are used for further essential research, which is overseen by the Advisory Committee for Variola Virus Research. This group meets on an annual basis. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat based in WHO Headquarters coordinates all smallpox eradication-related and post-eradication activities. These include overseeing permitted research with live variola virus for the development of countermeasures such as vaccine and antivirals.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States decided to destroy stocks by June 1999, allowing only small samples to be retained for research purposes in two secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in Russia. The temporary preservation of existing variola virus stocks is used for further essential research, which is overseen by the Variola Virus Research Advisory Committee. This committee meets annually. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat, located at WHO headquarters, coordinates all activities related to smallpox eradication and post-eradication. These include managing approved research using live variola virus to develop countermeasures such as vaccines and antiviral drugs.

translations.source: Tangu enzi ya kutokomeza, chanjo salama na matibabu mahususi yametengenezwa kwa ugonjwa wa ndui na magonjwa yanayohusiana kama vile ndui ya nyani. Sekretarieti pia inawajibika kwa maandalizi ya dharura na inasimamia hifadhi ya dharura ya chanjo ya ndui katika tukio lisilowezekana kwamba ugonjwa huo utaibuka tena. Sekretarieti inasimamia ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na usalama wa viumbe hai wa hifadhi zilizoidhinishwa za virusi vya variola kama ilivyoagizwa na Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni, na kuhakikisha ripoti ya kila mwaka kwa Mashirika Yanayoongoza ya WHO. Sekretarieti ya Ndui inaunga mkono Hifadhi za WHO katika kuhifadhi historia ya kimataifa ya ndui na kuweka kumbukumbu za mafunzo tuliyopata kutokana na kutokomeza kabisa ugonjwa wa ndui. Majukumu ya sasa ya Sekretarieti na wajibu wa Ndui kwa WHO ni pamoja na:
translations.reference: Since the eradication era, safer vaccines and specific treatments have been developed for smallpox and related diseases such as monkeypox. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a smallpox vaccine emergency stockpile in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat manages the biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the authorized variola virus repositories as mandated by the World Health Assembly, and ensures annual reporting to WHO Governing Bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from successful eradication of smallpox. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat's current roles and responsibilities include:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Since the era of eradication, safe vaccination and specific treatment have been developed for smallpox and related diseases such as monkeypox. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a strategic reserve of smallpox vaccine in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat oversees the safety oversight of agents and the safety of living organisms of approved variola virus repositories, as mandated by the World Health Organization, and ensures annual reporting to the WHO's governing bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO depots in preserving the international history of smallpox and maintaining records of lessons learned from the complete eradication of smallpox. The current responsibilities of the Secretariat and the role of Smallpox within WHO include:"

translations.source: Utafiti wa virusi vya Variola na maendeleo ya hatua za kupinga kuandaa mikutano ya kila mwaka ya Kamati ya Ushauri ya WHO kuhusu Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola (ACVVR) kuratibu mapitio ya mapendekezo ya utafiti wa kufanya kazi na virusi vya variola kupitia ACVVR kusimamia mahusiano hazina na virusi viwili vya variola (ambazo pia ni Vituo vya Kushirikiana vya WHO) kurekodi, kuchakata na kufuatilia maombi kutoka kwa watafiti wa DNA ya virusi vya variola kutoka kwa hazina 2 chini ya sheria maalum.
translations.reference: Variola virus research and development of countermeasures organizing annual meetings of the WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) coordinating review of research proposals for work with variola virus through the ACVVR managing relationships with the two variola virus repositories (which are also WHO Collaborating Centres) recording, processing and monitoring requests from researchers for variola virus DNA from the 2 repositories under specific rules

translations.source: kufanya ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na ulinzi wa viumbe hifadhi 2 za kimataifa katika hazina za virusi vya variola kuendeleza mwongozo wa kawaida kuhusu utafiti juu ya DNA ya virusi vya variola na masuala yanayohusiana Maandalizi ya dharura na majibu kuratibu na kuandaa mwongozo wa kawaida juu ya ukuzaji na utumiaji wa hatua za matibabu kwa virusi vya orthopox. kuandaa mikakati ya kujiandaa na kukabiliana, na zana za kutekeleza hatua za kujitayarisha
translations.reference: conducting biennial biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the 2 global variola virus repositories developing normative guidance regarding research on variola virus DNA and related matters Emergency preparedness and response coordinating and preparing normative guidance on development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses developing preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"To conduct biosafety and biosecurity inspections of 2 international facilities holding variola virus stocks, to develop standard guidelines on research into variola virus DNA and related issues. Emergency preparedness and response to coordinate and develop standard guidelines on the development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopox viruses. To prepare preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures."

translations.source: kudumisha na kusimamia hifadhi ya kimataifa ya chanjo ya ndui kuratibu maendeleo ya mtandao wa maabara kwa utambuzi na uchunguzi wa virusi vya orthopox kuunda mwongozo wa kawaida na mikakati ya kukabiliana na ndui ya nyani kwa binadamu na virusi vingine vya (ndui) orthopox kusaidia kujenga uwezo na kuendeleza nyenzo za mafunzo kuchangia mipango ya usalama wa afya duniani
translations.reference: maintaining and managing the smallpox global vaccine stockpile coordinating development of a laboratory network for orthopoxvirus detection and diagnostics developing normative guidance and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopoxviruses supporting capacity-building and developing training materials contributing to global health security initiatives
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"To maintain and manage the international bee vaccine reserve, coordinate the development of a network of laboratories for the diagnosis and investigation of orthopox viruses, create standard guidelines and strategies for responding to monkeypox and other (bee) orthopox viruses in humans, assist in building capacity and developing training materials, and contribute to global health security planning."

translations.source: Kuhifadhi historia ya ndui duniani kusimamia kumbukumbu za Mpango wa Kutokomeza Ugonjwa wa Ndui kwa ushirikiano na Shirika la Kumbukumbu la WHO na Maktaba ya WHO kuongoza mijadala ya sera na matukio ya ukumbusho kuhusu kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui kuripoti kila mwaka kwa Bodi ya Utendaji ya WHO na Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni Sekretarieti pia hukusanya na kusimamia ufadhili wa shughuli hizi na kwa matukio ya dharura kama inavyohitajika kama vile mashauriano ya kiufundi na mafunzo.
translations.reference: Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Preserving the history of polio globally involves managing the records of the Polio Eradication Initiative in collaboration with the WHO Archives and the WHO Library to lead policy discussions and commemorative events regarding polio eradication, reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly Secretariat. It also collects and manages funding for these activities and for emergency events as needed, such as technical consultations and training."

translations.source: Kung'atwa na nyoka Muhtasari: Kuumwa na nyoka ni ugonjwa unaoweza kuhatarisha maisha unaosababishwa na sumu wakati wa kuumwa na nyoka mwenye sumu. Uchafuzi unaweza pia kusababishwa na kunyunyiziwa sumu machoni na aina fulani za nyoka ambao wana uwezo wa kutema sumu kama njia ya kujikinga. Juhudi duni za hapo awali za kudhibiti kuumwa kwa nyoka zimetoa data iliyogawanyika, isiyo sahihi ya epidemiolojia. Waathiriwa wengi hawahudhurii vituo vya afya au hospitali na badala yake wanategemea matibabu ya kienyeji.
translations.reference: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Snakebite Summary:** Being bitten by a snake is a life-threatening illness caused by venom during a bite from a venomous snake. Contamination can also be caused by venom splashing into the eyes by certain snakes that have the ability to spray venom as a defense mechanism. Previous inadequate efforts to control snakebites have yielded divided, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centers or hospitals and instead rely on traditional medicine.

translations.source: Hata hivyo, takwimu zinazopatikana zinaonyesha watu milioni 4.5–5.4 huumwa na nyoka kila mwaka. Kati ya hizi, milioni 1.8-2.7 hupata ugonjwa wa kliniki na 81,000 hadi 138,000 hufa kutokana na matatizo. Vikundi vilivyo katika hatari kubwa ni pamoja na wafanyikazi wa kilimo vijijini, wafugaji, wavuvi, wawindaji, watoto wanaofanya kazi, watu wanaoishi katika nyumba ambazo hazijajengwa vizuri na wale ambao wana fursa ndogo ya kupata elimu na huduma za afya. Maradhi na vifo hutokea mara nyingi zaidi miongoni mwa vijana na watoto hukumbwa na vifo vingi zaidi. Zaidi ya hayo, wanawake hupata vikwazo vilivyoongezeka vya kupata huduma za matibabu katika baadhi ya tamaduni na wanawake wajawazito wako katika hatari kubwa.
translations.reference: However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"However, available statistics show that 4.5–5.4 million people are bitten by snakes each year. Of these, 1.8-2.7 million receive clinical care and 81,000 to 138,000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural farmers, herders, fishermen, hunters, child laborers, people living in poorly constructed homes, and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Illnesses and deaths occur more frequently among young people, and children suffer the most deaths. Furthermore, women face increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures, and pregnant women are at higher risk."

translations.source: Mgogoro unaoendelea unaozuia upatikanaji wa matibabu salama na madhubuti ya antivenom katika maeneo mengi, na hasa Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara, ni sababu moja inayochangia uwezekano wa kutafuta msaada kupitia dawa za jadi. Dalili: Kuumwa au kunyunyiziwa sumu kutoka kwa nyoka wenye sumu kunaweza kusababisha aina mbalimbali za dharura za kimatibabu. Ugonjwa wa sumu kutoka kwa aina tofauti za nyoka unaweza kusababisha dalili tofauti, zingine mbaya zaidi kuliko zingine. Hii inafanya utayarishaji wa antivenino sahihi kuwa tatizo linaloendelea. Evenoming inaweza kusababisha kupooza hatari ambayo inaweza kuzuia kupumua, na kufanya matibabu ya haraka muhimu.
translations.reference: An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

"The ongoing crisis hindering access to safe and effective antivenom treatment in many areas, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor contributing to the likelihood of seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Being bitten or sprayed by venom from poisonous snakes can cause a variety of medical emergencies. Poisoning from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more severe than others. This makes the preparation of appropriate antivenin a continuing challenge. Envenoming can cause dangerous paralysis that can obstruct breathing, making rapid treatment essential."

translations.source: Watu wanaweza pia kupata matatizo ya kutokwa na damu ambayo yanaweza kusababisha kuvuja damu mbaya au kushindwa kwa figo kusikoweza kurekebishwa. Uharibifu mkubwa wa tishu za ndani pia unaweza kutokea, ambayo inaweza kusababisha ulemavu wa kudumu na hata kukatwa kwa kiungo. Watoto wako katika hatari kubwa ya athari kali kutokana na uzito mdogo wa mwili kuliko watu wazima. Vifo vingi na madhara makubwa kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka vinaweza kuzuilika kabisa kwa kufanya antivenom salama na madhubuti zipatikane na kufikiwa zaidi, hasa katika maeneo yenye hatari kubwa. Dawa za kupambana na nyoka za ubora wa juu ndizo matibabu pekee madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za sumu za kuumwa na nyoka.
translations.reference: People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"People can also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to severe bleeding or irreversible kidney failure. Extensive internal tissue damage can also occur, which can result in permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at greater risk of severe effects due to their lower body weight compared to adults. Many deaths and serious consequences from snake bites can be completely prevented by making antivenom safe and effective more accessible, especially in high-risk areas. High-quality antivenom medications are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse many of the venomous effects of snake bites."

translations.source: Matibabu: Dawa za kuua nyoka ni matibabu madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za kuumwa na nyoka na zimejumuishwa katika orodha ya WHO ya dawa muhimu. Upatikanaji na ufikiwaji wa dawa hizi, pamoja na kuongeza uelewa juu ya mbinu za kimsingi za kuzuia miongoni mwa jamii na wahudumu wa afya, ni njia bora za kupunguza madhara makubwa na vifo kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka. Baada ya kuumwa na nyoka anayeshukiwa kuwa na sumu, fuata hatua hizi: Ondoka mara moja kutoka eneo ambalo kuumwa kulitokea. Ondoa kitu chochote kinachokaza karibu na sehemu ya mwili iliyoumwa ili kuepusha madhara ikiwa uvimbe utatokea.
translations.reference: Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Treatment:** Antivenom is an effective treatment for preventing or reversing many of the effects of snakebite and is included in the WHO's list of essential medicines. Availability and accessibility of these medicines, along with increasing understanding of basic prevention methods among communities and healthcare providers, are the best ways to reduce severe harm and deaths from snakebites. After a snakebite from a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything that is constricting around the affected part of the body to prevent harm if swelling occurs.

translations.source: Mhakikishie mhathiriwa, kwani kuumwa na nyoka wengi wenye sumu hakusababishi kifo cha papo hapo. Zuisha mtu huyo kabisa na umpeleke kwenye kituo cha afya haraka iwezekanavyo Kuweka shinikizo kwenye sehemu za kung'atwa na padi ya shinikizo inayoweza kufaa katika baadhi ya matukio. Epuka njia za jadi za misaada ya kwanza au dawa za mitishamba. Paracetamol inaweza kutolewa kwa maumivu ya ndani (ambayo inaweza kuwa kali).
translations.reference: Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe).
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Reassure the victim, as being bitten by many venomous snakes does not necessarily cause immediate death. Keep the person completely still and take them to a health center as quickly as possible. Apply pressure to the wound with a pressure bandage, which may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal remedies. Paracetamol can be given for internal pain (which can be severe)."

translations.source: Kutapika kunaweza kutokea, hivyo kumweka mtu upande wake wa kushoto katika nafasi ya kurejeshwa. Fuatilia kwa karibu njia ya hewa na kupumua na uwe tayari kufufua ikiwa ni lazima
translations.reference: Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili text:

"Vomiting may occur, so position the person on their left side. Closely monitor their breathing and airway, and be prepared to perform rescue breathing if necessary."

translations.source: helminthiases ya udongo Muhtasari: Maambukizi ya ugonjwa wa helminth (STH) ni miongoni mwa maambukizi ya kawaida duniani kote na huathiri jamii maskini zaidi na maskini zaidi. Husambazwa na mayai yaliyopo kwenye kinyesi cha binadamu ambayo nayo huchafua udongo katika maeneo ambayo usafi wa mazingira ni duni. Aina kuu zinazoambukiza watu ni minyoo (Ascaris lumbricoides), mjeledi (Trichuris trichiura) na ndoano (Necator americanus na Ancylostoma duodenale). Minyoo ya matumbo hutoa dalili nyingi ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara, maumivu ya tumbo), malaise ya jumla na udhaifu.
translations.reference: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

**Soil-Transmitted Helminthiases (STH) Summary:**

Infections with helminth (worm) diseases are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most marginalized communities. They are transmitted through eggs present in human feces, which contaminate the soil in areas with poor sanitation. The main species that infect people are roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), whipworm (Trichuris trichiura), and hookworm (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms cause many symptoms including gastrointestinal manifestations (diarrhea, abdominal pain), general malaise, and weakness.

translations.source: Hookworms husababisha kupoteza damu kwa muda mrefu kwa utumbo na kusababisha anemia. Helminths ya udongo hupitishwa kwa mayai ambayo hupitia kwenye kinyesi cha watu walioambukizwa. Minyoo ya watu wazima huishi kwenye utumbo ambapo hutoa maelfu ya mayai kila siku. Katika maeneo ambayo hayana usafi wa kutosha, mayai haya huchafua udongo.: mayai ambayo yameshikanishwa na mboga humezwa wakati mboga haijapikwa kwa uangalifu, kuosha au kuchambuliwa;
translations.reference: Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Hookworms cause prolonged blood loss in the intestines and lead to anemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted through eggs that pass through the feces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestines where they release thousands of eggs every day. In areas with inadequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil: eggs that adhere to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed, or inspected;"

translations.source: mayai humezwa kutoka kwa vyanzo vya maji vilivyochafuliwa; mayai humezwa na watoto wanaocheza kwenye udongo uliochafuliwa na kisha kuweka mikono midomoni mwao bila kunawa. Dalili: Ugonjwa unahusiana na idadi ya minyoo waliohifadhiwa. Watu walio na maambukizi ya nguvu ya mwanga (minyoo wachache) kwa kawaida hawateseki kutokana na maambukizi. Maambukizi mazito zaidi yanaweza kusababisha dalili mbalimbali ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara na maumivu ya tumbo), utapiamlo, malaise ya jumla na udhaifu, na kuharibika kwa ukuaji na ukuaji wa mwili.
translations.reference: eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Eggs are swallowed from contaminated water sources; eggs are swallowed by children playing in dirt and then putting their hands in their mouths without washing. Symptoms: The disease is related to the number of worms harbored. People with light infections (few worms) typically do not suffer from the infection. More severe infections can cause a variety of symptoms including gastrointestinal manifestations (diarrhea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and stunted growth and development."

translations.source: Maambukizi ya nguvu ya juu sana yanaweza kusababisha kizuizi cha matumbo ambacho kinapaswa kutibiwa kwa upasuaji. S. stercoralis inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa ngozi na utumbo na pia inajulikana kuhusishwa na utapiamlo sugu kwa watoto. Katika kesi ya kupungua kwa kinga ya mwenyeji, vimelea vinaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa hyperinfection/usambazaji ambao ni hatari kila wakati ikiwa haujaponywa mara moja na ipasavyo na mara nyingi husababisha kifo licha ya matibabu. Mnamo mwaka wa 2001, wajumbe katika Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni waliidhinisha kwa kauli moja azimio (WHA54.19) likizitaka nchi zilizo na ugonjwa huo kuanza kukabiliana na minyoo kwa umakini, haswa kichocho na helminths ya udongo. Mkakati wa udhibiti wa maambukizo ya helminth yanayopitishwa kwenye udongo ni kudhibiti maradhi kupitia matibabu ya mara kwa mara ya watu walio hatarini wanaoishi katika maeneo yenye ugonjwa.
translations.reference: Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Severe infections can cause intestinal obstruction which should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis can cause skin and intestinal disease and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In cases of host immune deficiency, parasites can cause a hyperinfection/dissemination disease which is always dangerous if not treated immediately and appropriately and often results in death despite treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously adopted a resolution (WHA54.19) urging countries with the disease to begin addressing worms with diligence, particularly hookworm and soil helminths. The strategy for controlling soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control the disease through regular treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas."

translations.source: Watu walio katika hatari ni: watoto wa shule ya mapema/ chekechea watoto wa umri wa shule wanawake wa umri wa uzazi (ikiwa ni pamoja na wanawake wajawazito katika trimester ya pili na ya tatu na wanawake wanaonyonyesha); watu wazima katika kazi fulani hatarishi kama vile wachumaji chai au wachimbaji.
translations.reference: People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"People at risk include: early childhood/preschool children, school-age children, women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women); adults in certain hazardous occupations such as tea pickers or miners."

translations.source: Matibabu Dawa zinazopendekezwa na WHO -albendazole (400 mg) na mebendazole (miligramu 500) - ni nzuri, bei nafuu na ni rahisi kusimamiwa na wafanyikazi wasio wa matibabu (k.m. walimu). Zimepitia majaribio ya kina ya usalama na zimetumika kwa mamilioni ya watu wenye athari chache na ndogo. Albendazole na mebendazole zote mbili zimetolewa kwa wizara za afya za kitaifa kupitia WHO katika nchi zote zilizo na ugonjwa huo kwa matibabu ya watoto wote wa umri wa kwenda shule. Ivermectin kwa udhibiti wa S. stercoralis inatarajiwa kupatikana kwa bei nafuu kuanzia 2021.
translations.reference: TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Recommended medicines for treatment by WHO – albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, affordable, and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g., teachers). They have undergone extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole have been provided to national ministries of health through WHO in all countries with the disease for the treatment of all school-aged children. Ivermectin for the control of *S. stercoralis* is expected to become available at an affordable price starting in 2021.

translations.source: watoto wanazaliwa wakiwa wamekufa Muhtasari: Mtoto anayefariki dunia baada ya wiki 28 za ujauzito, lakini kabla au wakati wa kuzaliwa, huainishwa kuwa mfu. Kuna karibu watoto milioni 2 wanaozaliwa kila mwaka - mmoja kila baada ya sekunde 16. Zaidi ya 40% ya watoto wote wanao kufa kabla ya kuzaliwa hutokea wakati wa leba - hasara ambayo inaweza kuepukwa kwa kuboreshwa kwa huduma bora na yenye heshima wakati wa kujifungua ikiwa ni pamoja na ufuatiliaji wa kawaida na upatikanaji wa huduma ya dharura ya uzazi inapohitajika. Kupata mtoto aliyekufa wakati wa ujauzito au kuzaa ni janga ambalo halijashughulikiwa vya kutosha katika ajenda za kimataifa, sera na programu zinazofadhiliwa. Kuna gharama za kisaikolojia, hasa kwa wanawake na familia zao, kama vile unyogovu wa uzazi, athari za kifedha na athari za kiuchumi, pamoja na unyanyapaa na mwiko. Mnamo mwaka wa 2014, Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha Mpango wa Utekelezaji wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (ENAP), ambao unajumuisha lengo la kimataifa la watoto 12 au pungufu wa trimester ya tatu (marehemu) wanaojifungua kwa jumla ya watoto 1,000 wanaozaliwa katika kila nchi ifikapo 2030.
translations.reference: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Stillbirths: A Summary**

A child who dies before or during birth, after 28 weeks of gestation, is classified as stillborn. Approximately 2 million babies are stillborn each year – one every 16 seconds. More than 40% of all stillbirths occur during labor – a loss that could be prevented by improving respectful and quality maternity care, including routine monitoring and access to emergency obstetric care when needed. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy that has not been adequately addressed in international agendas, policies, and funded programs. There are psychological costs, especially for women and their families, such as postpartum depression, financial and economic consequences, as well as stigma and blame. In 2014, the World Health Organization endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes an international target of a reduction of 12 or more stillbirths per 1,000 live births by 2030.

translations.source: Mnamo 2021, nchi 139 hasa zenye kipato cha juu na za juu za kipato cha kati zilifikia lengo hili, lakini nchi 56 hazitafikia lengo la ENAP kufikia 2030 ikiwa juhudi zaidi hazitafanywa. Ikiwa mwelekeo wa sasa utaendelea, watoto milioni 15.9 watazaliwa wamekufa; karibu nusu ya hizi (milioni 7.7, 48%) zitatokea katika Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara. Sababu: Kukiwa na huduma bora za afya wakati wote wa ujauzito na kujifungua, watoto wengi wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa wanaweza kuzuilika. Sababu kuu za uzazi ni pamoja na matatizo ya ujauzito na uzazi, ujauzito wa muda mrefu, maambukizi ya uzazi kama vile malaria, kaswende na VVU, hali ya uzazi hasa shinikizo la damu, kisukari, na kizuizi cha ukuaji wa fetasi (wakati mtoto ambaye hajazaliwa hawezi kufikia uwezo wake wa ukuaji na hivyo kuwa mdogo kuliko inavyopaswa kuwa). Matatizo ya kuzaliwa nayo yanasababisha chini ya 10% ya watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamefariki walioripotiwa kitaifa katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na idadi kamili katika mazingira ya kipato cha chini haijulikani kwa sababu ya mapungufu katika uchunguzi.
translations.reference: In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

In 2021, 139 countries, particularly those with high and upper-middle income, reached this goal, but 56 countries will not achieve the ENAP target by 2030 if greater efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Reason: With better health services throughout pregnancy and childbirth, many stillbirths can be prevented. Key causes of stillbirth include complications of pregnancy and childbirth, prolonged pregnancy, reproductive infections such as malaria, syphilis, and HIV, maternal conditions especially high blood pressure, diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when a baby before birth fails to reach its growth potential and is therefore smaller than it should be). Birth defects cause less than 10% of reported stillbirths in high-income countries, and the full number in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in surveillance.

translations.source: Mambo yanayohusiana na umri wa mama na kufanya mazoezi ya kuvuta sigara yanaweza pia kuongeza hatari ya ugonjwa wa uzazi na vifo vya kabla ya kuzaliwa. Kujifungua kunaweza kuzuiwa kwa njia ya kupanga uzazi ili kuepuka mimba zisizotarajiwa, afya bora na lishe bora kabla na wakati wa ujauzito, huduma bora na yenye heshima katika ujauzito na uzazi ikijumuisha wafanyakazi wa afya wenye ujuzi wa kutosha wakiwemo wakunga. Matibabu ya kaswende kwa wajauzito inaweza kuzuia watoto wanaokadiriwa kufikia 200,000 wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa, wakati ufuatiliaji wa mapigo ya moyo wa fetasi na ufuatiliaji wa leba na uingiliaji wa haraka inapohitajika ni muhimu kwa kuzuia uzazi 832,000 ndani ya uzazi na kupunguza vifo vya watoto wachanga. Watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa huhusishwa sana na viashirio vya kijamii na kiuchumi vya kiafya. Kinga na utunzaji sikivu unahitaji kuunganishwa katika mwendelezo wa huduma ya afya ya uzazi, na zaidi.
translations.reference: Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Factors related to a mother's age and smoking habits can also increase the risk of reproductive disease and stillbirths. Childbirth can be prevented through family planning to avoid unintended pregnancies, good health and proper nutrition before and during pregnancy, and quality, respectful care during pregnancy and childbirth, including skilled healthcare workers such as midwives. Treatment for pre-eclampsia in pregnant women can prevent an estimated 200,000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and umbilical cord monitoring with prompt intervention when needed is essential to prevent 832,000 intrapartum stillbirths and reduce neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly associated with social and economic health indicators. Immunization and attentive care need to be integrated into the continuum of reproductive health care, and more."

translations.source: Hii ni pamoja na utunzaji wa heshima na msaada katika tukio la kifo. Kusikiliza uzoefu na sauti za wanawake na jumuiya zao ni muhimu ili kusaidia kushughulikia masuala ya unyanyapaa unaohusishwa na kifo cha kabla. Jibu la WHO: Mengi zaidi yanahitajika kufanywa ili kujumuisha uzuiaji wa mimba mfu ndani ya ajenda za kimataifa na kitaifa za huduma ya afya ya hali ya juu kwa wanawake, vijana na watoto. Kupitia Mkakati wa Kimataifa wa Afya ya Wanawake, Watoto na Vijana 2016–2030 na kutekeleza mapendekezo ya Mpango Kazi wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (2014), WHO inajitahidi kukomesha uzazi unaoweza kuzuilika, kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuboresha afya ya uzazi, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana. Watoto wengi waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa hawapati cheti cha kuzaliwa na hawajasajiliwa.
translations.reference: This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"This includes dignified care and support in the event of death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of mistreatment associated with stillbirth. WHO’s response: More needs to be done to include prevention of stillbirth within international and national agendas for high-quality health services for women, adolescents, and children. Through the Global Strategy for Women’s, Children’s and Adolescents’ Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO strives to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve reproductive health, newborns, children, and adolescents. Many babies born stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered."

translations.source: Kuboresha mifumo ya kuripoti kuzaliwa na vifo vya watoto wachanga ni suala la haki za binadamu na sharti la kupunguza watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa. Aidha, kuna haja ya kuimarisha mifumo ya kawaida ya taarifa za afya ili kunasa na kuripoti kwa usahihi watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa ajili ya ufuatiliaji na kuboresha ubora wa huduma. WHO hutoa zana kusaidia nchi kuboresha data zao kuhusu watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa na vifo vya watoto wachanga. WHO pia ina mwongozo wa kusaidia nchi kukagua na kuchunguza vifo vya watu binafsi ili ziweze kupendekeza na kutekeleza masuluhisho ya kuzuia vile vile katika siku zijazo na mfumo wa kawaida wa uainishaji wa vifo wakati wa kujifungua (ICD-PM). WHO na UNICEF wanaunga mkono wito wa kuchukua hatua za pamoja kukomesha watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa njia zifuatazo:
translations.reference: Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by:
translations.hypothesis: Improving systems for reporting on the birth and death of newborns is a human rights issue and a requirement for reducing the number of babies born dead. Furthermore, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report on babies born dead for monitoring and improving service quality. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so that they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent them in the future, and a standard classification of death during childbirth (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF support the call to take joint action to end stillbirths through the following ways:

translations.source: kuongeza uelewa na kupunguza unyanyapaa kusaidia wanawake na familia zilizofiwa kuimarisha mifumo ya afya ili kutoa huduma bora kutaifisha na kuainisha malengo ya uzazi kuboresha upimaji wa watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa ili kuongeza ushahidi na maarifa.
translations.reference: increasing awareness and reducing stigma supporting bereaved women and families strengthening health systems to provide high-quality care nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets improving measurement of stillbirths to enhance evidence and knowledge.

"To increase understanding and reduce injustice, to help women and families who have lost loved ones, to strengthen health systems in order to provide better services, to institutionalize and specify reproductive goals, to improve the assessment of stillborn babies, in order to increase evidence and knowledge."
