To achieve this end, the program collaborates with specialists and organizations to implement key research activities, identify and quantify health risks from UV radiation, develop reliable predictions of the health and environmental outcomes of changes in UV exposure and stratospheric ozone depletion, and develop practical methods for monitoring changes in health effects resulting from UV over time. The program provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism, and occupational health.

translations.source: Chanjo ya afya kwa wote Muhtasari: Huduma ya afya kwa wote (UHC) ina maana kwamba watu wote wanapata huduma kamili za ubora wa afya wanazohitaji, wakati na mahali wanapozihitaji, bila matatizo ya kifedha. Inashughulikia mwendelezo kamili wa huduma muhimu za afya, kutoka kwa uhamasishaji wa afya hadi kinga, matibabu, urekebishaji na utunzaji wa uponyaji. Ili kutimiza ahadi hii, nchi zinahitaji kuwa na mifumo thabiti ya afya, yenye ufanisi na yenye usawa ambayo imejikita katika jumuiya zinazohudumia. Huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC) ndiyo njia bora na ya gharama nafuu ya kufika hapo.
translations.reference: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Health Coverage for All**

**Summary:** Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without suffering financial hardship. It covers the entire continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation, and palliative care. To fulfill this promise, countries need to have robust, efficient, and equitable health systems that are community-oriented. Primary health care (PHC) is the best and most cost-effective way to achieve this.

translations.source: Kila nchi ina njia tofauti ya kufikia UHC na kuamua nini cha kushughulikia kulingana na mahitaji ya idadi ya watu na rasilimali zilizopo. Kuwekeza katika PHC huhakikisha kwamba mahitaji hayo yote yanatambuliwa, yanapewa kipaumbele na kushughulikiwa kwa njia jumuishi; kwamba kuna nguvu kazi ya afya na utunzaji imara na yenye vifaa; na kwamba sekta zote za jamii zinachangia katika kukabiliana na mambo ya kimazingira na kijamii na kiuchumi yanayoathiri afya na ustawi, ikiwa ni pamoja na kujiandaa kwa ajili ya, kukabiliana na hali ya dharura. Jibu la WHO: Kama msingi wa UHC, WHO inapendekeza kuelekeza upya mifumo ya afya kwa kutumia mbinu ya huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC). Kufikia UHC ni kipaumbele cha kimkakati cha WHO, kukiwa na lengo la watu bilioni 1 zaidi kunufaika na huduma ya afya kwa wote ifikapo 2025. tunazingatia usaidizi wa kiufundi ili kujenga taasisi za kitaifa na utoaji wa huduma ili kujaza nafasi muhimu wakati wa dharura.
translations.reference: Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Every country has different ways of achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) and determining what to address based on the needs of the population and available resources. Investing in Primary Health Care (PHC) ensures that all those needs are recognized, prioritized, and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a strong and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to addressing the environmental, social, and economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, and responding to, emergencies. WHO’s Response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a Primary Health Care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a strategic priority for WHO, with the goal of an additional one billion people benefiting from universal health coverage by 2025. We focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps during emergencies."

translations.source: Katika mipangilio thabiti zaidi ya mfumo wa afya, tunasukuma athari za afya ya umma kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote kupitia mazungumzo ya sera ya mifumo ya siku zijazo na usaidizi wa kimkakati ili kuboresha utendaji kazi. Kazi hii inaungwa mkono na mwongozo na makubaliano ya kawaida; na uongozi katika nyanja za diplomasia, utetezi, usawa wa kijinsia, usawa wa afya na haki za binadamu, hatua za sekta mbalimbali, na fedha. Ajenda ya 2030 ya SDGs Kazi ya WHO inawiana na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu (SDG) lengo la 3.8, ambalo linalenga kufikia bima ya afya kwa wote, ikiwa ni pamoja na ulinzi wa hatari za kifedha, upatikanaji wa huduma bora za afya na gharama nafuu za dawa muhimu na chanjo za bei nafuu kwa wote. Ulimwenguni, maendeleo kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote ni changamoto. Maendeleo katika utoaji wa huduma yamekwama huku idadi ya watu wanaokabiliwa na matumizi mabaya ya afya nje ya mfukoni ikiongezeka kila mara.
translations.reference: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

"In the most stable health system settings, we are driving public health impacts towards health for all through policy dialogues on future systems and strategic support to improve performance. This work is underpinned by guidance and common ground; and leadership in areas of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, cross-sectoral actions, and financing. The 2030 SDG Agenda WHO’s work aligns with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which aims to achieve universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality health services and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards health for all is challenging. Advances in service delivery have stalled while the number of people facing catastrophic health expenditure outside of their pockets is constantly increasing."

translations.source: WHO hufuatilia maendeleo na kuchapisha ripoti za kimataifa ili kufichua ushahidi wa hivi punde kila baada ya miaka miwili. Tazama ripoti hapa na utembelee karatasi ya ukweli ya UHC kwa habari zaidi.
translations.reference: WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

WHO monitors progress and publishes international reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the report here and visit the UHC factsheet for more information.

translations.source: Afya ya mjini Muhtasari: Ukuaji wa miji ni mojawapo ya mitindo inayoongoza duniani ya karne ya 21 ambayo ina athari kubwa kwa afya. Zaidi ya 55% ya watu duniani wanaishi mijini - idadi ambayo inatarajiwa kuongezeka hadi 68% ifikapo 2050. Kwa vile ukuaji mkubwa wa miji katika siku zijazo utafanyika katika nchi zinazoendelea, dunia leo ina fursa ya kipekee ya kuongoza ukuaji wa miji na mwelekeo mwingine mkuu wa maendeleo ya miji kwa njia ambayo inalinda na kukuza afya. Hii ni muhimu, sio kwa sababu afya na ustawi wa raia labda ndio mali muhimu zaidi ya jiji.
translations.reference: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Urban Health Summary:** The growth of cities is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century with significant implications for health. More than 55% of the world's population lives in urban areas – a number expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most urban growth in the future will occur in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urban growth and another major trend in urban development in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is arguably the most valuable asset of a city.

translations.source: Hata hivyo, wengi kati ya watu bilioni 4.2 wanaoishi mijini wanakabiliana na ukosefu wa makazi na usafiri, usafi wa mazingira na usimamizi duni wa taka, na ubora wa hewa ambao unakiuka miongozo ya WHO. Aina zingine za uchafuzi wa mazingira, kama vile kelele, uchafuzi wa maji na udongo, kinachojulikana kama visiwa vya joto mijini, na ukosefu wa nafasi ya kutembea, kuendesha baiskeli na kuishi hai huchanganyika zaidi na kufanya miji kuwa kitovu cha janga la magonjwa yasiyoambukiza na vichochezi vya mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa. Hazina ya WHO kwa Afya ya Mjini Athari: Takriban 40% ya ukuaji wa miji uko katika makazi duni ambayo hayana maji salama na vyoo, na 91% ya watu katika maeneo ya mijini wanapumua hewa chafu. Linapokuja suala la lishe bora, ukuaji wa miji huongeza umbali kutoka shamba hadi uma, na kusababisha mahitaji ya vyakula visivyo na afya, vilivyochakatwa.
translations.reference: However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"However, many of the 4.2 billion people living in urban areas face a lack of housing and transportation, poor environmental sanitation and waste management, and air quality that violates WHO guidelines. Other forms of environmental pollution, such as noise, water and soil pollution, what is known as urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling, and active living, combine to make cities a hub for non-communicable diseases and drivers of climate change. WHO's Urban Health Treasure: Impact: Approximately 40% of urban growth occurs in substandard settlements that lack safe water and toilets, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to good nutrition, urban growth increases the distance from farm to table, leading to a demand for unhealthy, processed foods."

translations.source: Wakazi wa mijini pia wako katika hatari kubwa ya kuathiriwa na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa kwa sababu ya utegemezi wao wa nishati ya mafuta kwa usafiri, kupikia na kupasha joto. Miji huchukua zaidi ya theluthi mbili ya nishati ya ulimwengu na hutoa 60% ya gesi chafuzi, na zile za ndani zinaweza kukumbwa na halijoto ya 3–5º C juu kuliko maeneo ya vijijini yanayozunguka kwa sababu ya upana wake mkubwa wa saruji na nafasi finyu za kijani kibichi. Haya yote yanahatarisha afya ya wakazi wa mijini. Sababu 10 kuu za vifo zinahusiana kwa karibu na ukuaji wa miji wa haraka na usio na mpango, na muundo mbaya wa mijini na mipango. Miji inakabiliwa na mzigo wa kiafya mara tatu wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza kama vile VVU/UKIMWI, kifua kikuu, nimonia, dengi na kuhara;
translations.reference: Urban dwellers are also highly vulnerable to the effects of climate change because of their dependence on fossil fuels for transport, cooking and heating. Cities account for over two thirds of the world’s energy and emit 60% of greenhouse gases, and those inland may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas because of their large expanses of concrete and limited open green spaces. All of this puts urban dwellers’ health at risk. Most the top 10 causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face the triple health burden of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, dengue and diarrhoea;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

"Urban residents are also at high risk of being affected by climate change due to their dependence on fossil fuels for transportation, cooking, and heating. Cities consume more than two-thirds of the world's energy and produce 60% of polluting gases, and those within them can experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas due to their large concrete expanse and limited green spaces. All of this endangers the health of urban residents. The 10 leading causes of death are closely linked to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face a threefold health burden of infectious diseases such as HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, malaria, and diarrhea."

translations.source: magonjwa yasiyo ya kuambukiza kama vile ugonjwa wa moyo, kiharusi, pumu, saratani, kisukari na unyogovu; na vurugu na majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na majeraha ya barabarani. Jibu la WHO: Licha ya changamoto zao, miji inaweza kuunda fursa kwa afya bora, mazingira safi na hatua za hali ya hewa. Sera madhubuti za mijini lazima zitangulize afya, kwani ni muhimu kwa ajili ya kuendeleza maisha bora ya mijini, kujenga nguvu kazi yenye tija, kuunda jamii zenye uthabiti na uchangamfu, kuwezesha uhamaji, kukuza mwingiliano wa kijamii na kulinda idadi ya watu walio hatarini. Mtazamo wa WHO kuhusu afya ya mijini unazingatia ubora bora wa hewa, maji na usafi wa mazingira, na viashirio vingine vya mazingira; mipango ya miji yenye afya; mazingira bora na yasiyo na moshi; uhamaji salama na kiafya; kuzuia vurugu na majeraha; kukuza mifumo ya chakula na lishe bora; usimamizi wa mazingira ya magonjwa yanayoenezwa na vekta.na kujiandaa kwa miji kwa dharura.
translations.reference: noncommunicable diseases like heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments and climate action. Strong urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for fostering good urban livelihoods, building a productive workforce, creating resilient and vibrant communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on better air quality, water and sanitation, and other environmental determinants; healthy urban planning; healthier and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and diets; environmental management of vector-borne diseases; and urban preparedness for emergencies.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Non-communicable diseases such as heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes, and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO’s response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments, and climate action. Robust urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for developing better urban lives, building a productive workforce, creating stable and resilient communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction, and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s perspective on urban health focuses on the best possible quality of air, water, and environmental sanitation, and other environmental indicators; healthy city planning; clean and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and nutrition; environmental management of vector-borne diseases, and preparing cities for emergencies."

translations.source: Kufanya kazi katika sekta zote na kuhakikisha uwiano wa sera katika maeneo mbalimbali ni muhimu katika kuunda mazingira ya kusaidia na kuwezesha afya, ambayo yanahakikisha kwamba masuala ya afya na usawa yanaunganishwa katika mchakato wa kupanga, uwekezaji na maamuzi ya sera katika ngazi ya ndani. Ili kusaidia Nchi Wanachama kushughulikia vipaumbele vilivyo hapo juu, WHO inafanya kazi ili kuimarisha msingi wa ushahidi ili kusaidia watunga sera kufanya maamuzi sahihi wanaposhughulikia hatari za kiafya. Inatoa zana na mwongozo juu ya kile kinachofanya kazi, na inasaidia ufuatiliaji wa viashiria muhimu vinavyohusiana na afya. WHO inaongoza na kushiriki katika kuhimiza mabadilishano ya jiji hadi jiji na kusaidia kuandaa mifumo ya kitaasisi na sera ya utawala bora wa miji kwa afya na ustawi katika miji.
translations.reference: Working across sectors and ensuring the coherence of policies across different areas is key to creating supportive and enabling environments for health, which ensures that health and equity considerations are integrated throughout the planning process, investments and policy decisions at the local level. To help Member States address the above priorities, WHO works to strengthen the evidence base to help policy-makers make informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports monitoring of key health-related indicators. WHO leads and engages in fostering city-to-city exchanges and helps develop institutional and policy frameworks for good urban governance for health and well-being in cities.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Working across all sectors and ensuring policy coherence in various areas is crucial in creating a supportive and enabling environment for health, which ensures that health and equity issues are integrated into the planning, investment, and policy-making process at the local level. To help Member States address the priorities mentioned above, WHO is working to strengthen the evidence base to support policymakers in making informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports the monitoring of key health-related indicators. WHO leads and participates in promoting city-to-city exchanges and helps to establish institutional frameworks and policies for better urban governance for health and well-being in cities."

translations.source: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto Muhtasari: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto unajumuisha aina zote za ukatili dhidi ya watu walio chini ya umri wa miaka 18. Kwa watoto wachanga na watoto wadogo, unyanyasaji unahusisha hasa unyanyasaji wa watoto (yaani unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kingono na kihisia na kutelekezwa) mikononi mwa wazazi na watu wengine wenye mamlaka. Wavulana na wasichana wako katika hatari sawa ya unyanyasaji wa kimwili na kihisia na kutelekezwa, na wasichana wako katika hatari kubwa ya unyanyasaji wa kijinsia. Watoto wanapobalehe, unyanyasaji wa rika na ukatili wa marafiki wa karibu, pamoja na unyanyasaji wa watoto, huenea sana.
translations.reference: Violence against children Overview: Violence against children includes all forms of violence against people under 18 years old. For infants and younger children, violence mainly involves child maltreatment (i.e. physical, sexual and emotional abuse and neglect) at the hands of parents and other authority figures. Boys and girls are at equal risk of physical and emotional abuse and neglect, and girls are at greater risk of sexual abuse. As children reach adolescence, peer violence and intimate partner violence, in addition to child maltreatment, become highly prevalent.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

**Child Abuse: Summary**

Child abuse encompasses all forms of violence against individuals under the age of 18. For infants and young children, abuse primarily involves child maltreatment (i.e., physical, sexual, and emotional abuse and neglect) in the hands of parents and other authority figures. Boys and girls are at equal risk of physical, emotional abuse, and neglect, while girls are at greater risk of sexual abuse. As children grow older, peer abuse and bullying, along with child abuse, become increasingly prevalent.

translations.source: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto unaweza kuzuiwa. Kuzuia na kukabiliana na unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto kunahitaji jitihada za kushughulikia hatari na ulinzi katika viwango vyote vinne vya hatari (mtu binafsi, uhusiano, jumuiya, jamii). Azimio la Mei 2016 la Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha mpango wa kwanza wa utekelezaji wa WHO wa Kimataifa wa kuimarisha jukumu la mfumo wa afya ndani ya mwitikio wa kitaifa wa sekta mbalimbali kushughulikia unyanyasaji wa watu binafsi, hasa dhidi ya wanawake na wasichana, na dhidi ya watoto. A Kulingana na mpango huu, WHO kwa kushirikiana na Nchi Wanachama na washirika wengine, imejitolea: Kufuatilia ukubwa wa kimataifa na sifa za ukatili dhidi ya watoto na kuunga mkono juhudi za nchi kuweka kumbukumbu na kupima ukatili huo. Kudumisha mfumo wa taarifa za kielektroniki unaotoa muhtasari wa data ya kisayansi kuhusu mzigo, sababu za hatari na matokeo ya unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto, na ushahidi wa kuzuilika kwake.
translations.reference: Violence against children can be prevented. Preventing and responding to violence against children requires that efforts systematically address risk and protective factors at all four interrelated levels of risk (individual, relationship, community, society). May 2016 World Health Assembly resolution endorsed the first ever WHO Global plan of action on strengthening the role of the health system within a national multisectoral response to address interpersonal violence, in particular against women and girls, and against children. A According to this plan, WHO in collaboration with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global magnitude and characteristics of violence against children and supporting country efforts to document and measure such violence. Maintaining an electronic information system that summarizes the scientific data on the burden, risk factors and consequences of violence against children, and the evidence for its preventability.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

Child abuse can be prevented. Preventing and addressing child abuse requires efforts to address risks and protection at all four levels of risk (individual, relationship, community, society). A May 2016 resolution of the World Health Assembly endorsed the WHO’s first global implementation plan to strengthen the role of the health system within the national multi-sectoral response to address violence against individuals, particularly against women and girls, and against children. Accordingly, WHO, in collaboration with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global extent and characteristics of child abuse and supporting countries’ efforts to record and measure it. Maintaining a system of electronic information providing a summary of scientific data on the burden, risk factors, and consequences of child abuse, and evidence of its preventability.

translations.source: Kutengeneza na kusambaza hati za mwongozo wa kiufundi, kanuni na viwango vya kuzuia na kukabiliana na ukatili dhidi ya watoto kulingana na ushahidi. Kuchapisha mara kwa mara ripoti za hali ya kimataifa kuhusu juhudi za nchi kushughulikia unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto kupitia sera za kitaifa na mipango ya utekelezaji, sheria, programu za kuzuia na huduma za kukabiliana. Nchi zinazounga mkono na washirika katika kutekeleza mikakati ya uzuiaji na majibu yenye msingi wa ushahidi, kama ile iliyojumuishwa katika INSPIRE: Mikakati saba ya kukomesha ukatili dhidi ya watoto. Kushirikiana na mashirika na mashirika ya kimataifa ili kupunguza na kukomesha ukatili dhidi ya watoto duniani kote, kupitia mipango kama vile Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa wa Kutokomeza Ukatili dhidi ya Watoto, Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa wa Kutokomeza Ukatili dhidi ya Watoto,
translations.reference: Developing and disseminating evidence-based technical guidance documents, norms and standards for preventing and responding to violence against children. Regularly publishing global status reports on country efforts to address violence against children through national policies and action plans, laws, prevention programmes and response services. Supporting countries and partners in implementing evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those included in INSPIRE: Seven strategies for ending violence against children. Collaborating with international agencies and organizations to reduce and eliminate violence against children globally, through initiatives such as the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children,
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"To develop and disseminate technical guidance documents, policies, and standards for preventing and responding to violence against children based on evidence. To publish regularly reports on the global situation regarding countries' efforts to address child abuse through national policies and implementation plans, laws, prevention programs, and response services. To support countries and partners in implementing evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those included in INSPIRE: Seven Strategies for Ending Violence Against Children. To collaborate with organizations and international agencies to reduce and eliminate violence against children worldwide, through initiatives such as the Global Partnership to End Violence Against Children."

translations.source: kwa pamoja kwa Wasichana na Muungano wa Kuzuia Ghasia. Unyanyasaji wa watoto Unyanyasaji wa watoto ni unyanyasaji na utelekezwaji unaotokea kwa watoto chini ya umri wa miaka 18. Inajumuisha aina zote za unyanyasaji wa kimwili na/au kihisia, unyanyasaji wa kijinsia, kutelekezwa, uzembe na unyonyaji wa kibiashara au mwingine, unaosababisha madhara halisi au yanayoweza kutokea kwa afya, maisha, ukuaji au utu wa mtoto katika muktadha wa uhusiano wa mtoto wajibu, uaminifu au madaraka. Mfiduo wa unyanyasaji wa rafiki wa karibu wakati mwingine hujumuishwa kama unyanyasaji wa watoto.
translations.reference: Together for Girls and the Violence Prevention Alliance. Child maltreatment Child maltreatment is the abuse and neglect that occurs to children under 18 years of age. It includes all types of physical and/or emotional ill-treatment, sexual abuse, neglect, negligence and commercial or other exploitation, which results in actual or potential harm to the child’s health, survival, development or dignity in the context of a relationship of responsibility, trust or power. Exposure to intimate partner violence is also sometimes included as a form of child maltreatment.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

**Together for Girls and the Alliance to Prevent Violence. Child Abuse Child abuse is abuse and neglect occurring to children under the age of 18. It includes all forms of physical and/or emotional abuse, sexual abuse, abandonment, negligence and exploitation of a commercial or other nature, causing actual or potential harm to a child's health, life, development or dignity in the context of a relationship where the child is dependent, trusted or has authority. Exposure to peer abuse is sometimes included as child abuse.**

translations.source: Unyanyasaji wa watoto una madhara makubwa sana ya papo hapo na ya muda mrefu. Zaidi ya kifo, jeraha la kimwili na ulemavu, vurugu inaweza kusababisha dhiki ambayo huharibu ukuaji wa ubongo na kuharibu mifumo ya neva na kinga. Hii nayo inahusishwa na kucheleweshwa kwa maendeleo ya utambuzi, utendaji duni wa shule na kuacha shule, matatizo ya afya ya akili, majaribio ya kujiua, kuongezeka kwa tabia zinazohatarisha afya, urekebishaji na maandalizi ya unyanyasaji. Habari njema ni kwamba unyanyasaji wa watoto unaweza kuzuiwa kupitia hatua zinazosaidia wazazi na walezi, kukuza kanuni na maadili yasiyo ya ukatili, kutoa elimu na mafunzo ya stadi za maisha, kuimarisha mapato ya familia na usalama wa kiuchumi, kutoa mwitikio wa hali ya juu na huduma za usaidizi, kuunda na kudumisha mazingira salama kwa watoto, na kutekeleza na kutekeleza sheria dhidi ya unyanyasaji wa watoto. WHO, kwa kushirikiana na washirika kadhaa:
translations.reference: Child maltreatment has enormous immediate and long-term repercussions. Beyond death, physical injury and disability, violence can lead to stress that impairs brain development and damages the nervous and immune systems. This in turn is associated with delayed cognitive development, poor school performance and dropout, mental health problems, suicide attempts, increased health-risk behaviours, revictimization and the perpetration of violence. The good news is that child maltreatment can be prevented through interventions that support parents and caregivers, promote non-violent norms and values, provide education and life skills training, strengthen families’ income and economic security, offer high quality response and support services, create and sustain safe environments for children, and implement and enforce laws against child maltreatment. WHO, in collaboration with a number of partners:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

"Child abuse has very serious immediate and long-term consequences. Beyond death, physical injury, and disability, violence can cause distress that damages brain development and disrupts nervous and immune systems. This is also associated with delayed cognitive development, poor school performance and dropping out of school, mental health problems, attempted suicide, increased risky behaviors, perpetration and preparation for abuse. The good news is that child abuse can be prevented through actions that support parents and caregivers, promote non-violent principles and values, provide education and life skills training, strengthen family incomes and economic security, provide high-quality and support services, create and maintain safe environments for children, and enact and enforce laws against child abuse. WHO, in collaboration with several partners:"

translations.source: hutoa mwongozo wa kiufundi na wa kawaida kwa kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi; inatetea ongezeko la usaidizi wa kimataifa kwa uwekezaji katika kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi; hutoa usaidizi wa kiufundi kwa programu za kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi katika nchi kadhaa za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Vurugu za vijana Vurugu za vijana ni tatizo la afya ya umma duniani.
translations.reference: provides technical and normative guidance for evidence-based child maltreatment prevention; advocates for increased international support for and investment in evidence-based child maltreatment prevention; provides technical support for evidence-based child maltreatment prevention programmes in several low- and middle-income countries. Youth violence Youth violence is a global public health problem.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"It provides technical and practical guidance for preventing child abuse based on evidence; it advocates for increased international support for investment in preventing child abuse based on evidence; it provides technical assistance to programs preventing child abuse based on evidence in several low- and middle-income countries. Youth Violence Youth violence is a global public health problem."

translations.source: Inajumuisha aina mbalimbali za vitendo kutoka kwa uonevu na mapigano ya kimwili, hadi unyanyasaji mkali zaidi wa kingono na kimwili hadi mauaji. Vurugu za vijana huongeza kwa kiasi kikubwa gharama za afya, ustawi na haki ya jinai; hupunguza tija; na kwa ujumla hudhoofisha muundo wa jamii. Zaidi ya vifo, majeraha na madhara ya kisaikolojia, unyanyasaji wa vijana unaweza kusababisha kuongezeka kwa tabia hatari za kiafya kama vile kuvuta sigara, matumizi mabaya ya dawa za kulevya, ngono isiyo salama na vurugu zaidi. Haya nayo yanahusishwa na magonjwa sugu ya kupumua, saratani, magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa, kisukari, mimba za utotoni na magonjwa ya zinaa. Mikakati madhubuti ya kuzuia na kukabiliana nayo ni pamoja na ile inayokuza uzazi na maendeleo ya utotoni;
translations.reference: It includes a range of acts from bullying and physical fighting, to more severe sexual and physical assault to homicide. Youth violence dramatically increases health, welfare and criminal justice costs; reduces productivity; and generally undermines the fabric of society. Beyond deaths, injuries and psychological harm, youth violence can lead to increased health risk behaviours such as smoking, substance abuse, unsafe sex, and further violence. These in turn are associated with chronic respiratory diseases, cancer, cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, early pregnancy and sexually transmitted diseases. Effective prevention and response strategies include those that promote parenting and early childhood development;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"It encompasses a wide range of actions from petty theft and physical fights, to the most severe sexual and physical abuse, to murder. Youth violence significantly increases the costs of health, well-being, and criminal justice; it reduces productivity; and generally weakens the fabric of society. Beyond deaths, injuries, and psychological trauma, mistreatment of young people can lead to an increase in risky health behaviors such as smoking, substance abuse, unsafe sex, and further violence. These are also associated with chronic respiratory diseases, cancer, heart and vascular diseases, diabetes, teenage pregnancies, and sexually transmitted infections. Effective prevention and response strategies include those that promote child development and early childhood development;"

translations.source: programu za kuzuia uonevu shuleni, kitaaluma, na maisha na ujuzi wa kijamii; mbinu za kimatibabu na watu walio katika hatari kubwa, na mbinu za ngazi ya jamii na jamii kama vile kupunguza ufikiaji na matumizi mabaya ya pombe na silaha za moto, maeneo yenye matatizo makubwa na polisi wenye matatizo, uboreshaji wa miji na kupunguza umaskini. WHO na washirika hupunguza unyanyasaji wa vijana kupitia mipango inayosaidia kutambua, kuhesabu na kukabiliana na tatizo, hizi ni pamoja na: kuandaa kifurushi cha programu za kuzuia unyanyasaji shuleni; kuzingatia ukubwa wa vurugu za vijana na haja ya kuzuia;
translations.reference: school-based bullying prevention, academic, and life and social skills development programmes; therapeutic approaches with high-risk individuals, and community- and society-level approaches like reducing access to and misuse of alcohol and firearms, hotspots and problem-oriented policing, urban upgrading, and poverty de-concentration. WHO and partners decrease youth violence through initiatives that help to identify, quantify and respond to the problem, these include: developing a package for schools-based violence prevention programmes; drawing attention to the magnitude of youth violence and the need for prevention;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Programs to prevent bullying at school, academically, and in life, and social skills; medical approaches and those at high risk, and community and societal level approaches such as reducing access to and misuse of alcohol and firearms, troubled areas and problematic police, urban improvement and poverty reduction. WHO and partners reduce youth violence through programs that help identify, count, and address the problem. These include: developing a package of programs to prevent bullying at school; considering the scale of youth violence and the need to prevent it."

translations.source: kujenga ushahidi juu ya upeo na aina za vurugu katika mazingira tofauti; kuendeleza miongozo kwa Nchi Wanachama na sekta zote zinazohusika ili kuzuia vurugu za vijana na kuimarisha majibu yake; kuunga mkono juhudi za kitaifa za kuzuia ghasia za vijana; na kushirikiana na mashirika na mashirika ya kimataifa ili kuzuia unyanyasaji wa vijana duniani kote.
translations.reference: building evidence on the scope and types of violence in different settings; developing guidance for Member States and all relevant sectors to prevent youth violence and strengthen responses to it; supporting national efforts to prevent youth violence; and collaborating with international agencies and organizations to prevent youth violence globally.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"To build evidence on the scope and types of violence in different settings; to develop guidelines for Member States and all sectors involved to prevent youth violence and strengthen responses to it; to support national efforts to prevent youth unrest; and to collaborate with national and international organizations to prevent youth abuse worldwide."

translations.source: Ukatili dhidi ya wanawake Muhtasari: Ukatili dhidi ya wanawake - hasa unyanyasaji wa mpenzi wa karibu na unyanyasaji wa kijinsia - ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma na kiafya na ukiukaji wa haki za binadamu za wanawake. Umejikita katika na kuendeleza ukosefu wa usawa wa kijinsia.Ulimwenguni 1 kati ya wanawake 3 hupata unyanyasaji wa kimwili na/au kingono maishani mwao, hasa na mwenzi wa karibu. Hiki ni ukumbusho kamili wa ukubwa wa usawa wa kijinsia na ubaguzi dhidi ya wanawake. Umoja wa Mataifa unafafanua unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake kama "kitendo chochote cha unyanyasaji wa kijinsia ambacho husababisha, au kinaweza kusababisha madhara ya kimwili, kingono, au kiakili au mateso kwa wanawake, ikiwa ni pamoja na vitisho vya vitendo kama hivyo, kulazimishwa au kunyimwa uhuru kiholela, iwe unafanyika hadharani au katika maisha ya kibinafsi.
translations.reference: Violence against women Overview: Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public and clinical health problem and a violation of women's human rights. It is rooted in and perpetuates gender inequalities.Globally 1 in 3 women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, mostly by an intimate partner. This is a stark reminder of the scale of gender inequality and discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as "any act of gender-based violence that results in, or is likely to result in, physical, sexual, or mental harm or suffering to women, including threats of such acts, coercion or arbitrary deprivation of liberty, whether occurring in public or in private life.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Violence Against Women**

**Summary:** Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public health and social problem and a violation of women's human rights. It is rooted in and perpetuates gender inequality. Globally, 1 in 3 women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, mostly by an intimate partner. This is a stark reminder of the extent of gender inequality and discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as "any act of gender-based violence that results in, or is likely to result in, physical, sexual, or psychological harm or suffering to women, including threats of such acts, coercion or arbitrary deprivation of liberty, whether occurring in public or in private life."

translations.source: Vurugu za mpenzi wa karibu hurejelea tabia ya mshirika wa karibu au mpenzi wa zamani ambayo husababisha madhara ya kimwili, kingono au kisaikolojia, ikiwa ni pamoja na unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kulazimishwa kingono, unyanyasaji wa kisaikolojia na kudhibiti tabia. Unyanyasaji wa kijinsia ni "tendo lolote la ngono, jaribio la kupata tendo la ngono, au kitendo kingine kinachoelekezwa dhidi ya ujinsia wa mtu kwa kulazimishwa, na mtu yeyote bila kujali uhusiano wake na mwathiriwa, katika mazingira yoyote. Inajumuisha ubakaji, unaofafanuliwa kama kupenya kwa kulazimishwa au vinginevyo kwa lazima kwa uke au mkundu kwa kutumia uume, sehemu nyingine ya mwili au kitu." Athari: Unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake ni tatizo la kimataifa la uwiano wa janga. Inasababisha uharibifu, madhara kwa maisha ya wanawake na ya watoto wao.
translations.reference: Intimate partner violence refers to behaviour by an intimate partner or ex-partner that causes physical, sexual or psychological harm, including physical aggression, sexual coercion, psychological abuse and controlling behaviours. Sexual violence is "any sexual act, attempt to obtain a sexual act, or other act directed against a person’s sexuality using coercion, by any person regardless of their relationship to the victim, in any setting. It includes rape, defined as the physically forced or otherwise coerced penetration of the vulva or anus with a penis, other body part or object." Impact: Violence against women is a global problem of pandemic proportions. It causes devastating, harm to women’s lives and that of their children.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Intimate partner violence refers to the behavior of a close associate or former partner that causes physical, sexual, or psychological harm, including physical abuse, sexual coercion, psychological abuse, and controlling behavior. Sexual violence is defined as "any sexual act, attempt to obtain a sexual act, or other act directed against a person's sexuality by coercion, by anyone regardless of their relationship with the victim, in any setting. It includes rape, defined as the forced or otherwise compulsory penetration of the vagina or anus with a penis, another part of the body, or an object." Impact: Violence against women is a global problem of epidemic proportions. It causes destruction and harm to the lives of women and their children."

translations.source: Pia inadhuru afya ya kiuchumi na kijamii ya familia, jumuiya na nchi zao. Wakati baadhi ya wanawake wako katika hatari zaidi kuliko wengine, unyanyasaji unaweza kutokea kwa mwanamke yeyote, katika nchi yoyote - bila kujali tamaduni, dini au hali ya kiuchumi. Ukosefu wa usawa wa kijinsia ikiwa ni pamoja na kanuni hatari za kijinsia ni vichochezi muhimu vya unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake. Ukatili unaweza kuathiri vibaya afya ya wanawake kimwili, kiakili, kingono na uzazi. Inahusishwa na hatari kubwa ya majeraha, huzuni, matatizo ya wasiwasi, mimba zisizopangwa, magonjwa ya zinaa, VVU na matatizo mengine mengi ya afya.
translations.reference: It also hurts the economic and social health of their families, communities, and countries. While some women are more at risk than others, violence can happen to any woman, in any country – regardless of culture, religion or economic status. Gender inequality including harmful gender norms are key drivers of violence against women. Violence can negatively affect women’s physical, mental, sexual, and reproductive health. It is associated with increased risk of injuries, depression, anxiety disorders, unplanned pregnancies, sexually transmitted infections, HIV and many other health problems.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"It also affects the economic and social well-being of families, communities, and their countries. While some women are at greater risk than others, abuse can happen to any woman, in any country – regardless of culture, religion, or economic status. Gender inequality, including harmful gender norms, is a key driver of violence against women. Violence can severely affect women's physical, mental, sexual, and reproductive health. It is associated with a high risk of injuries, depression, anxiety disorders, unintended pregnancies, sexually transmitted infections, HIV, and many other health problems."

translations.source: Watoto wanaolelewa katika familia ambazo kuna jeuri wanaweza kukumbwa na misukosuko mbalimbali ya kitabia na kihisia. Haya yanaweza kusababisha matatizo ya kiakili na ukuaji na pia kuhusishwa na kufanya au kukumbana na ukatili baadaye maishani. Jibu la WHO: WHO ina jukumu muhimu katika kuleta umakini na kujibu unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake kama suala la afya ya umma na usawa wa kijinsia kupitia: Utafiti na kujenga ushahidi ili kuangazia ukubwa wa unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake, sababu zake za hatari na matokeo yake, na kutambua afua madhubuti za kuzuia na kukabiliana nazo. Kutengeneza miongozo na zana, kuweka kanuni na viwango vya mwitikio madhubuti wa kiafya kwa ukatili dhidi ya wanawake.
translations.reference: Children who grow up in families where there is violence may suffer a range of behavioural and emotional disturbances. These can lead to mental and development problems and also be associated with perpetrating or experiencing violence later in life. WHO response: WHO plays a key role in bringing attention to and responding to violenceagainst women as a public health and gender equality issue through: Research and evidence-building to highlight the magnitude of violence against women, its risk factors and consequences, and to identify effective interventions for prevention and response. Developing guidelines and tools, setting norms and standards for an effective health response to violence against women.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Children raised in families where there is violence can experience various psychological and emotional disturbances. These can lead to mental health problems and developmental issues, and can also be associated with perpetrating or experiencing violence later in life. WHO's Response: WHO has a crucial role in raising awareness and responding to violence against women as a public health and gender equality issue through: Research and building evidence to highlight the magnitude of violence against women, its risk factors and consequences, and identifying effective interventions to prevent and address it. Developing guidelines and tools, establishing principles and standards for a robust health response to violence against women."

translations.source: Kuimarisha uwezo wa nchi wa mifumo ya afya na watoa huduma ili kuzuia na kukabiliana na unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake. Kuhimiza uongozi katika mifumo ya afya na kujenga utashi wa kisiasa wa kushughulikia unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake kupitia utetezi na ushirikiano. Mpango Kazi Mkuu wa 13 wa WHO (GPW) unajumuisha unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake kama sehemu ya mchango wake katika Lengo la Maendeleo Endelevu (SDG) 5 kuhusu usawa wa kijinsia na uwezeshaji wa wanawake (haswa, SDG 5.2 juu ya kutokomeza unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake na wasichana). Katika Mkutano wa Afya Ulimwenguni mnamo Mei 2016, Nchi Wanachama ziliidhinisha mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa juu ya kuimarisha jukumu la mifumo ya afya katika kushughulikia unyanyasaji wa watu binafsi, haswa dhidi ya wanawake na wasichana na dhidi ya watoto.
translations.reference: Strengthening country capacity of health systems and providers to prevent and respond to violence against women. Encouraging leadership in health systems and building political will to address violence against women through advocacy and partnerships. The WHO 13th General Programme of Work (GPW) includes violence against women as part of its contribution to Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 5 on gender equality and women’s empowerment (specifically, SDG 5.2 on the elimination of violence against women and girls). At the World Health Assembly in May 2016, Member States endorsed a global plan of action on strengthening the role of the health systems in addressing interpersonal violence, in particular against women and girls and against children.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Strengthening the capacity of countries and health systems and service providers to prevent and address violence against women. Encouraging leadership within health systems and building political will to address violence against women through advocacy and collaboration. WHO’s 13th General Programme of Work (GPW) includes violence against women as part of its contribution to Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 5 on gender equality and women’s empowerment (specifically, SDG 5.2 on eliminating all forms of violence against women and girls). At the World Health Assembly in May 2016, Member States endorsed a global plan of action to strengthen the role of health systems in addressing violence against individuals, particularly against women and girls and against children."

translations.source: Moto wa nyika Muhtasari: Moto wa nyika unaongezeka kote ulimwenguni katika mzunguko, ukali na muda, na hivyo kuongeza hitaji la kuelewa athari za kiafya za mfiduo wa moto wa nyikani. Hatari ya moto wa nyikani hukua katika hali ya ukame sana, kama vile ukame, mawimbi ya joto na wakati wa upepo mkali. Moshi wa moto wa mwituni ni mchanganyiko wa vichafuzi hatari vya hewa, kama vile PM2.5, NO2, ozoni, hidrokaboni zenye kunukia, au risasi. Mbali na kuchafua hewa kwa vichafuzi vyenye sumu, moto wa mwituni pia huathiri hali ya hewa wakati huo huo kwa kutoa kiasi kikubwa cha kaboni dioksidi na gesi zingine chafu kwenye angahewa. Huku mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa yakisababisha hali ya joto na ukame na kuongezeka kwa miji ya maeneo ya vijijini, msimu wa moto unaanza mapema na kumalizika kwa kuchelewa.
translations.reference: Wildfires Overview: Wildfires are increasing around the globe in frequency, severity and duration, heightening the need to understand the health effects of wildfire exposure. The risk of wildfires grows in extremely dry conditions, such as drought, heat waves and during high winds.Wildfire smoke is a mixture of hazardous air pollutants, such PM2.5, NO2, ozone, aromatic hydrocarbons, or lead. In addition to contaminating the air with toxic pollutants, wildfires also simultaneously impact the climate by releasing large quantities of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. With climate change leading to warmer temperatures and drier conditions and the increasing urbanization of rural areas, the fire season is starting earlier and ending later.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Wildfire Summary:** Wildfires are increasing globally in frequency, intensity, and duration, thus increasing the need to understand the health effects of exposure to wildfire smoke. The risk of wildfires grows in extremely dry conditions, such as drought, heat waves, and windy periods. Wildfire smoke is a mixture of hazardous air pollutants, such as PM2.5, NO2, ozone, volatile organic compounds, or lead. In addition to air pollution with toxic pollutants, wildfires also affect the climate at the same time by releasing large amounts of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. As climate change causes hotter conditions and drought and the growth of urban areas in rural areas, the wildfire season starts earlier and ends later.

translations.source: Matukio ya moto wa porini yanazidi kukithiri katika suala la ekari kuteketezwa, muda na ukubwa, na yanaweza kutatiza usafiri, mawasiliano, usambazaji wa maji, na huduma za umeme na gesi. Athari: Moto wa nyika unaowaka karibu na maeneo yenye watu wengi unaweza kuwa na athari kubwa kwa mazingira, mali, mifugo na vifo vya binadamu na maradhi kulingana na ukubwa, kasi na ukaribu wa moto huo, na kama idadi ya watu ina onyo la juu la kuhama. Moshi wa moto wa nyikani ni mchanganyiko wa vichafuzi vya hewa ambavyo chembechembe zake (PM) ndio tishio kuu la afya ya umma. PM2.5 kutoka kwa moshi wa moto wa mwituni huhusishwa na vifo vya mapema kwa idadi ya watu, na inaweza kusababisha na kuzidisha magonjwa ya mapafu, moyo, ubongo/mfumo wa neva, ngozi, utumbo, figo, macho, pua na ini. Pia imeonyeshwa kusababisha uharibifu wa utambuzi na kupoteza kumbukumbu.
translations.reference: Wildfire events are getting more extreme in terms of acres burned, duration and intensity, and they can disrupt transportation, communications, water supply, and power and gas services. Impact: Wildfires that burn near populated areas can have significant impact on the environment, property, livestock and human mortality and morbidity depending on the size, speed and proximity to the fire, and whether the population has advanced warning to evacuate. Wildfire smoke is a mixture of air pollutants of which particulate matter (PM) is the principal public health threat. PM2.5 from wildfire smoke is associated with premature deaths in the general population, and can cause and exacerbate diseases of the lungs, heart, brain/nervous system, skin, gut, kidney, eyes, nose and liver. It has also been shown to lead to cognitive impairment and memory loss.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Incidents of wildfires are increasing in terms of acres burned, duration, and size, and can disrupt transportation, communication, water supply, and electricity and gas services. Impacts: Wildfires burning near populated areas can have significant impacts on the environment, property, livestock, and human deaths and illnesses, depending on the size, speed, and proximity of the fire, and whether the population has a high evacuation alert. Smoke from wildfires is a mixture of air pollutants, with its particulate matter (PM) being the main threat to public health. PM2.5 from wildfire smoke is associated with premature deaths in the population and can cause and exacerbate diseases of the lungs, heart, brain/nervous system, skin, intestines, kidneys, eyes, nose, and liver. It has also been shown to cause cognitive impairment and memory loss."

translations.source: Wazima moto na wafanyakazi wa kukabiliana na dharura pia huathiriwa sana na majeraha, moto na kuvuta pumzi ya moshi, hasa katika viwango vya juu. Utafiti zaidi wa taaluma mbalimbali unathibitishwa ili kuelewa madhara ya kiafya yaliyofichika na ya muda mrefu ya mfiduo wa moto wa nyikani kwa watu walio hatarini (watoto, wazee, wajawazito, wagonjwa sugu), haswa kwa maeneo ya kijiografia yanayostahimili mfiduo wa mara kwa mara na wa mzunguko kwa matukio haya ya moto wa porini. Majibu ya WHO: Ukubwa wa gharama halisi za kibinadamu kutokana na moto wa nyika unaweza kupunguzwa ikiwa hatua za kutosha za kuzuia dharura, kujiandaa, kukabiliana na uokoaji zitatekelezwa kwa njia endelevu na kwa wakati. Kwa kufanya kazi na Nchi Wanachama, WHO husaidia kujenga mifumo ya afya thabiti na tendaji ambayo inaweza kutarajia mahitaji na changamoto wakati wa dharura, ili tuweze kuwa na uwezekano mkubwa wa kupunguza hatari na kujibu kwa ufanisi inapohitajika. WHO inafanya kazi na washirika katika kuandaa, kuzuia, kugundua, kujibu na kupona kutokana na dharura na majanga, ikiwa ni pamoja na kufichua mazingira, kama vile kuwezesha mifumo ya tahadhari ya mapema na kutoa ushauri wa afya na ubora wa hewa;
translations.reference: Firefighters and emergency response workers are also greatly impacted by injuries, burns and smoke inhalation, particularly at high concentrations. More interdisciplinary research is warranted to understand the latent and long-term health effects of wildfire exposure on vulnerable populations (children, older people, pregnant people, chronically ill people), particularly for geographic areas enduring repeated and cyclical exposure to these wildﬁre events. WHO Response: The magnitude of the physical and human costs from wildfires can be reduced if adequate emergency prevention, preparedness, response and recovery measures are implemented in a sustainable and timely manner. Working with Member States, WHO helps to build resilient and proactive health systems that can anticipate the needs and challenges during emergencies, so that we can be more likely to reduce risks and respond effectively when needed. WHO works with partners in preparing, preventing, detecting, responding and recovering from emergencies and disasters, including environmental exposures, such asimplementing early warning systems and issuing health and air quality advisories;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Firefighters and emergency responders are also significantly affected by injuries, burns, and inhaling smoke, especially at high levels. Further research across various professions is warranted to understand the hidden and long-term health effects of wildfire exposure on vulnerable populations (children, the elderly, pregnant women, people with chronic illnesses), particularly in geographic areas that experience repeated and cyclical exposure to these wildfires. WHO Response: The true human cost of wildfires can be reduced if sufficient measures to prevent emergencies, prepare, respond, and rescue are implemented sustainably and in a timely manner. Working with Member States, WHO helps build resilient and functional health systems that can anticipate needs and challenges during emergencies, so that we can maximize risk reduction and respond effectively when needed. WHO works with partners to prepare for, prevent, detect, respond to, and recover from emergencies and disasters, including environmental exposures, such as facilitating early warning systems and providing health and air quality advice."

translations.source: zenye kutolewa kwa vifaa vya hatari; kutathmini mahitaji ya afya ya jamii na uharibifu wa miundombinu; kurejesha huduma za msingi, kama vile chanjo, afya ya mtoto na uzazi, na afya ya akili; kuimarisha rasilimali watu kwa ajili ya usimamizi wa maafa; kukusanya, kuchambua na kusambaza taarifa zinazohusiana na dharura na maafa yanayoweza kutokea katika eneo; kuanzisha na kusimamia hifadhi ya vifaa vya misaada;
translations.reference: containing the release of hazardous materials; assessing health needs of the community and infrastructure damage; restoring primary care services, like immunization, child and maternal health, and mental health; strengthening human resources for disaster management; collecting, analysing and disseminating information related to emergencies and disasters that are likely to occur in a region; establishing and managing stocks of relief supplies and equipment;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Dealing with the release of dangerous materials; assessing community health needs and infrastructure damage; restoring essential services, such as immunization, child and reproductive health, and mental health; strengthening human resources for disaster management; collecting, analyzing, and disseminating information related to potential emergencies and disasters in the area; establishing and managing a relief supplies reserve;"

translations.source: wito wa ufadhili wa dharura kusaidia hatua za afya; na kuandaa sera za kitaifa, mapendekezo na mipango ya kitaifa ya kukabiliana na dharura.
translations.reference: calling for emergency funding to support health action; and developing national policies, recommendations and national emergency response plans.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili text:

**Call for emergency funding to support health measures; and to prepare national policies, recommendations, and national plans to respond to emergencies.**

translations.source: Afya ya wanawake Muhtasari: Kuwa mwanamume au mwanamke kuna athari kubwa kwa afya, kama matokeo ya tofauti za kibayolojia na zinazohusiana na jinsia. Afya ya wanawake na wasichana inatia wasiwasi hasa kwa sababu, katika jamii nyingi, wanatatizwa na ubaguzi unaotokana na mambo ya kitamaduni. Kwa mfano, wanawake na wasichana wanakabiliwa na ongezeko la hatari ya VVU/UKIMWI. Baadhi ya mambo ya kitamaduni yanayozuia wanawake na wasichana kufaidika na huduma bora za afya na kufikia kiwango bora zaidi cha afya ni pamoja na:
translations.reference: Women's health Overview: Being a man or a woman has a significant impact on health, as a result of both biological and gender-related differences. The health of women and girls is of particular concern because, in many societies, they are disadvantaged by discrimination rooted in sociocultural factors. For example, women and girls face increased vulnerability to HIV/AIDS. Some of the sociocultural factors that prevent women and girls to benefit from quality health services and attaining the best possible level of health include:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

**Women's Health Summary:** Being male or female has a significant impact on health, as a result of biological and gender-related differences. Women's and girls' health is of particular concern because, in many societies, they are disadvantaged by cultural factors. For example, women and girls face an increased risk of HIV/AIDS. Some cultural factors that prevent women and girls from benefiting from quality health services and achieving the highest possible level of health include:

translations.source: uhusiano usio sawa wa uwezo kati ya wanaume na wanawake; kanuni za kijamii zinazopunguza elimu na fursa za ajira za kulipwa; mtazamo wa kipekee juu ya majukumu ya uzazi ya wanawake; na uwezekano au uzoefu halisi wa unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kingono na kihisia. Ingawa umaskini ni kikwazo muhimu kwa matokeo chanya ya afya kwa wanaume na wanawake, umaskini unaelekea kutoa mzigo mkubwa kwa afya ya wanawake na wasichana kutokana na, kwa mfano, mazoea ya kulisha (utapiamlo) na matumizi ya nishati zisizo salama za kupikia (COPD).
translations.reference: unequal power relationships between men and women; social norms that decrease education and paid employment opportunities; an exclusive focus on women’s reproductive roles; and potential or actual experience of physical, sexual and emotional violence. While poverty is an important barrier to positive health outcomes for both men and women, poverty tends to yield a higher burden on women and girls’ health due to, for example, feeding practices (malnutrition) and use of unsafe cooking fuels (COPD).
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"An unequal power relationship between men and women; social norms that limit women's education and paid employment opportunities; a singular view of women's reproductive roles; and the possibility or actual experience of physical, sexual, and emotional abuse. While poverty is a significant barrier to positive health outcomes for both men and women, poverty tends to disproportionately burden the health of women and girls due to, for example, feeding practices (malnutrition) and the use of unsafe cooking energy (COPD)."



**Explanation of some key translation choices:**

*   **uhusiano usio sawa wa uwezo:** Translated as "An unequal power relationship" to capture the nuance of imbalance.
*   **kanuni za kijamii:** Translated as "social norms" - a common and accurate equivalent.
*   **mtazamo wa kipekee:** Translated as "a singular view" to convey the idea of a limited or narrow perspective.
*   **COPD:** Left as COPD as it's a common medical abbreviation and likely refers to Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease, often linked to unsafe cooking practices.

translations.source: Yaws (Endemic treponematoses) Muhtasari: Miyao ni ugonjwa sugu wa ngozi unaojulikana na papillomas (uvimbe usio na kansa) na vidonda. Husababishwa na bakteria aina ndogo ya Treponema pallidum pertenue, ambayo ni ya kundi moja la bakteria wanaosababisha kaswende ya venereal. Miayo kimsingi huathiri hasa watoto walio na umri wa chini ya miaka 15 ambao wanaishi katika jamii maskini katika maeneo ya misitu yenye joto, unyevu na ya kitropiki ya Afrika, Asia, Amerika ya Kusini na visiwa vya Pasifiki. Idadi kubwa ya watu walioathirika wanaishi vijijini, mbali na huduma za afya.
translations.reference: Yaws (Endemic treponematoses) Overview: Yaws is a chronic skin infection characterized by papillomas (noncancerous lumps) and ulcers. It is caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pertenue, which belongs to the same group of bacteria that causes venereal syphilis. Yaws primarily affects children aged under 15 years who live in poor communities in warm, humid and tropical forested areas of Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Pacific islands. The majority of affected populations live rural areas, far from health services.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Yaws (Endemic treponematoses) Summary: Yaws is a chronic skin disease characterized by papillomas (non-cancerous growths) and ulcers. It is caused by a subspecies of bacteria, *Treponema pallidum pertenue*, which belongs to the same group of bacteria that cause venereal syphilis. Yaws primarily affects children under the age of 15 who live in poor communities in the hot, humid, tropical forests of Africa, Asia, South America, and the Pacific Islands. A large number of those affected live in rural areas, far from healthcare services.

translations.source: Umaskini, hali duni za kijamii na kiuchumi na usafi duni wa kibinafsi huwezesha kuenea kwa miayo. Ingawa kuna visa zaidi ya 80,000 vya miayo kila mwaka, wataalam wanaamini ugonjwa huo unaweza kudhibitiwa na hatimaye kutokomezwa kwa sababu kadhaa. Kwanza, hutokea tu kwa wanadamu, si wanyama, na kufanya udhibiti iwe rahisi zaidi. Pia ni rahisi kutibu kwa dawa zinazopatikana kwa urahisi na tayari imeondolewa katika baadhi ya nchi, ikiwa ni pamoja na India. Mifuko iliyobaki ya miayoyanayoweza kuathiri, ingawa kwa kawaida katika maeneo ya mbali, inamaanisha uwezekano wa kuenea zaidi kuna uwezekano mdogo kwa ufuatiliaji na udhibiti sahihi.
translations.reference: Poverty, low socio-economic conditions and poor personal hygiene facilitate the spread of yaws. Although there are over 80 000 cases of yaws each year, experts believe the disease can be controlled and ultimately eradicated for several reasons. First, it only occurs in humans, not animals, making control much easier. It is also easy to treat with readily available drugs and has already been eliminated in some countries, including India. The remaining pockets of yaws infection, although usually in remote places, means further spread is less likely with proper surveillance and control measures.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

Poverty, poor social and economic conditions, and poor personal hygiene facilitate the spread of leprosy. Although there are over 80,000 cases of leprosy each year, experts believe the disease can be controlled and eventually eradicated for several reasons. First, it only occurs in humans, not animals, making control easier. It is also easy to treat with readily available medication and has already been eliminated in some countries, including India. Remaining pockets of leprosy that can affect, although usually in remote areas, mean the possibility of further spread is less likely with proper monitoring and control.

translations.source: Dalili: Kuna hatua mbili za maambukizi ya yaws. Hatua ya awali ni wakati mgonjwa anaambukiza na anaweza kusambaza ugonjwa huo kwa wengine. Katika hatua hii, papule (uvimbe usio na kansa, unaokua nje) hukua kwenye sehemu ya maambukizi. Papuli hii imejaa viumbe na inaweza kudumu kwa miezi 3-6 ikifuatiwa na uponyaji wa asili. Matibabu isipokuwepo, hii inafuatiwa na vidonda vya ngozi vinavyoenea juu ya mwili.
translations.reference: Symptoms: There are two stages of yaws infection. The early stage is when the patient is infectious and can spread the disease to others. In this stage, a papule (a noncancerous, outward-growing lump) develops at the site of infection. This papule is full of the organisms and may persist for 3–6 months followed by natural healing. Without treatment, this is followed by disseminated skin lesions over the body.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Symptoms:** There are two stages of yaws infection. The initial stage is when a patient is infectious and can spread the disease to others. In this stage, papules (non-cancerous growths that grow outwards) develop on the site of infection. This papule is full of organisms and can last for 3-6 months followed by natural healing. Without treatment, this is followed by skin lesions that spread across the body.

translations.source: Maumivu ya mifupa na vidonda vya mfupa vinaweza pia kutokea. Hatua ya pili sio ya kuambukiza na kawaida huonekana miaka mitano baada ya kuambukizwa. Imetawaliwa na sifa ya ulemavu wa pua, mifupa na hyperkeratosis ya mitende / mimea (unene). Baada ya uchunguzi wa eneo kulingana na dalili hizi, ugonjwa huo unaweza kuthibitishwa kwa kuchunguza sampuli kutoka kwa kikosi cha ngozi chini ya aina maalum ya darubini (uchunguzi wa giza). Vipimo vya damu havitumiwi kwa kawaida kwa sababu miayo inahusiana kwa karibu na bakteria inayosababisha kaswende na magonjwa hayo mawili yataonyesha matokeo sawa.
translations.reference: Bone pain and bone lesions may also occur. The second stage is noninfectious and typically appears five years after infection. It is characterized by disabling consequences of the nose, bones and palmar/plantar hyperkeratosis (thickening). After a field diagnosis based on these symptoms, the disease can be confirmed by examining a sample from a skin legion under a special type of microscope (darkfield examination). Blood tests are not typically used because yaws is closely related to the bacterium that causes syphilis and the two diseases will show the same result.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Pain in the bones and bone lesions can also occur. The second stage is not infectious and usually appears five years after infection. It is characterized by disability of the nose, bones, and hyperkeratosis of the palms/soles (thickening). After examination of the area based on these symptoms, the disease can be confirmed by examining a sample from the skin lesion under a special type of microscope (dark examination). Blood tests are not usually used because the rays are closely related to the bacteria that causes yaws, and those two diseases would show similar results."

translations.source: Matibabu: Kwa sasa hakuna chanjo ya miayo. Elimu ya afya na uboreshaji wa usafi wa kibinafsi ni mbinu muhimu zaidi za kuzuia. Antibiotiki mbili zinaweza kutumika kutibu miayo. Dozi moja ya mdomo ya azithromycin (30 mg/kg, upeo wa 2 g) ndiyo tiba inayopendekezwa. Benzathine penicillin (dozi moja ya ndani ya misuli) yenye vitengo milioni 0.6 (watoto chini ya miaka 10) na vitengo milioni 1.2 (watu walio na umri wa zaidi ya miaka 10) inaweza kutumika kwa wagonjwa wanaoshukiwa kushindwa kwa matibabu baada ya azithromycin, au wagonjwa ambao hawawezi kutibiwa na azithromycin.
translations.reference: Treatment: There is currently no vaccine for yaws. Health education and improved personal hygiene are the most important tactics for prevention. Two antibiotics can be used to treat yaws. A single oral dose of azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g) is the recommended treatment. Benzathine penicillin (single intramuscular dose) at 0.6 million units (children aged under 10 years) and 1.2 million units (people aged over 10 years) can be used for patients with suspected clinical treatment failure after azithromycin, or patients who cannot be treated with azithromycin.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Treatment:** Currently, there is no vaccine for scabies. Health education and improvement of personal hygiene are the most important methods of prevention. Two antibiotics can be used to treat scabies. A single oral dose of azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g) is the recommended treatment. Benzathine penicillin (a single intramuscular injection) with 0.6 million units (children under 10 years) and 1.2 million units (people over 10 years) can be used for patients suspected of failing treatment after azithromycin, or patients who cannot be treated with azithromycin.

translations.source: Matibabu haya yatasababisha uponyaji kamili wa kliniki katika 95% ya kesi lakini wagonjwa wanapaswa kuchunguzwa tena wiki 4 baada ya kupokea antibiotiki. Mapitio ya hati za kihistoria za miaka ya 1950 yanaonyesha kuwa zaidi ya nchi na wilaya 90 zilipatikana kwa miayo. Nchi na maeneo 14 pekee kati ya 90 ndizo zilizo na data ya hivi majuzi kuhusu miayo kulingana na mfumo wa kawaida wa ufuatiliaji; hata hivyo, takwimu hizi zinaweza tu kuwa dalili ya kuwepo kwa ugonjwa huo na sio kiwango chake kamili. Ghana, Papua New Guinea na Visiwa vya Solomon huripoti zaidi ya kesi 10,000 kwa mwaka. Ecuador na India zinaonekana kuwa zimekatiza usambazaji.
translations.reference: This treatment will result in complete clinical healing in 95% of cases but patients should be re-examined 4 weeks after receiving the antibiotics. A review of the historic documents from 1950s shows that over 90 countries and territories were endemic for yaws. Only 14 out of the 90 countries and territories have recent data on yaws based on the routine surveillance system; however, these figures may just be an indication of the presence of the disease and not its full extent. Ghana, Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands report over 10 000 cases per year. Ecuador and India appear to have interrupted transmission.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"This treatment will result in complete clinical healing in 95% of cases, but patients should be re-examined 4 weeks after receiving antibiotics. Review of historical records from the 1950s shows that over 90 countries and districts were found to be affected by filaria. Only 14 of these 90 countries and areas have recent data on filaria according to a standard monitoring system; however, these figures can only be an indication of the disease's presence and not its full extent. Ghana, Papua New Guinea, and the Solomon Islands report more than 10,000 cases per year. Ecuador and India appear to have interrupted transmission."

translations.source: Homa ya manjano Muhtasari: Homa ya manjano ni ugonjwa wa virusi ambao hupitishwa kwa wanadamu kwa kuumwa na mbu walioambukizwa. Inakabiliwa na magonjwa ya milipuko na inaweza kuzuilika kwa chanjo. Mbu hawa wanaong'ata mchana huzaliana karibu na nyumba (ndani), katika misitu au (mwituni), au katika makazi yote mawili (nusu-ndani). Homa ya manjano ni ugonjwa wenye athari kubwa, hatari kubwa, na hatari ya kuenea kimataifa, ikiwakilisha tishio linalowezekana kwa usalama wa afya ulimwenguni.
translations.reference: Yellow fever Overview: Yellow fever is a viral disease that is transmitted to humans by the bites of infected mosquitoes. It is prone to epidemics and is preventable with a vaccine. These day-biting mosquitoes breed around houses (domestic), in forests or jungles (wild), or in both habitats (semi-domestic). Yellow fever is a high-impact, high-threat disease, with a risk of international spread, representing a potential threat to global health security.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Yellow Fever Summary: Yellow fever is a viral disease transmitted to humans through the bite of infected mosquitoes. It is prone to outbreaks and can be prevented by vaccination. These mosquitoes bite during the day and breed close to homes (indoors), in forests or (in the countryside), or in both semi-indoor environments. Yellow fever is a highly impactful disease, with a high mortality rate and a risk of international spread, representing a potential threat to global health security.

translations.source: Kuna aina 3 za mizunguko ya maambukizi: Homa ya manjano ya Sylvatic (au jungle) huenea nyani wanapoumwa na mbu wa mwituni ambao hupitisha virusi kwa nyani wengine na wanadamu. Homa ya manjano ya kati huenea wakati mbu wa nusu-fuge wanaambukiza nyani na watu. Hii ndiyo aina ya mlipuko wa kawaida zaidi barani Afrika. Homa ya manjano ya mijini inaweza kusababisha magonjwa makubwa ya milipuko wakati watu walioambukizwa wanapoanzisha virusi katika maeneo yenye wakazi wengi wenye msongamano mkubwa wa mbu na ambapo watu wana kinga kidogo. Mara kwa mara, wasafiri walioambukizwa wamesafirisha kesi kwa nchi ambazo hazina homa ya manjano.
translations.reference: There are 3 types of transmission cycles: Sylvatic (or jungle) yellow fever spreads when monkeys are bitten by wild mosquitoes that pass the virus on to other monkeys and humans. Intermediate yellow fever spreads when semi-domestic mosquitoes infect both monkeys and people. This is the most common type of outbreak in Africa.
