Urban yellow fever can cause large epidemics when infected people introduce the virus into heavily populated areas with high mosquito density and where people have little immunity. Occasionally, infected travellers have exported cases to countries that are free of yellow fever.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

There are 3 types of transmission cycles: Sylvatic (or jungle) yellow fever spreads when monkeys are bitten by forest mosquitoes that transmit the virus to other monkeys and humans. Intermediate yellow fever spreads when semi-forest mosquitoes infect monkeys and people. This is the most common type of outbreak in Africa. Urban yellow fever can cause large outbreaks of disease when infected people introduce the virus into densely populated areas with a high mosquito population and where people have little immunity. Occasionally, infected travelers have introduced cases to countries that are free of yellow fever.

translations.source: Hata hivyo, ugonjwa huo unaweza kuenea kwa urahisi katika nchi mpya ikiwa kuna spishi za mbu wanaoweza kuusambaza, hali mahususi ya hali ya hewa, na hifadhi ya wanyama inayohitajika kuutunza. Dalili: Homa ya manjano inaweza kujitokeza ikiwa na dalili mbalimbali na ukali. Mara baada ya kuambukizwa, virusi vya homa ya manjano huingia ndani ya mwili kwa siku 3-6. Watu wengi hawaoni dalili, lakini dalili hizi zinapotokea, zinazojulikana zaidi ni homa, maumivu ya misuli na maumivu ya mgongo, maumivu ya kichwa, kukosa hamu ya kula, na kichefuchefu au kutapika. Katika hali nyingi, dalili hupotea baada ya siku 3-4.
translations.reference: However, the disease can only spread easily to a new country if there are mosquito species able to transmit it, specific climatic conditions, and the animal reservoir needed to maintain it. Symptoms: Yellow fever can present with a wide range of symptoms and severity. Once contracted, the yellow fever virus incubates in the body for 3–6 days. Many people do not experience symptoms, but when these do occur, the most common are fever, muscle pain with prominent backache, headache, loss of appetite, and nausea or vomiting. In most cases, symptoms disappear after 3–4 days.
translations.hypothesis: However, the disease can easily spread to new countries if there are mosquito species capable of transmitting it, specific climatic conditions, and the necessary wildlife reservoir to sustain it. Symptoms: Yellow fever can manifest with a variety of symptoms and severity. After infection, the yellow fever virus enters the body within 3-6 days. Many people do not experience symptoms, but when they do occur, the most common are fever, muscle pain and back pain, headache, loss of appetite, and nausea or vomiting. In most cases, symptoms disappear after 3-4 days.

translations.source: Asilimia ndogo ya wagonjwa huingia katika awamu ya pili, yenye sumu zaidi ndani ya saa 24 baada ya kupona dalili za awali. Homa kali inarudi na mifumo kadhaa ya mwili huathiriwa, kwa kawaida ini na figo. Katika awamu hii, watu wanaweza kupata homa ya manjano (njano ya ngozi na macho, hivyo basi kuitwa homa ya manjano), mkojo mweusi, na maumivu ya tumbo na kutapika. Kutokwa na damu kunaweza kutokea kwa mdomo, pua, macho au tumbo. Nusu ya wagonjwa hawa hufa ndani ya siku 7-10. Homa ya njano ni vigumu kutambua, hasa wakati wa hatua za mwanzo.
translations.reference: A small percentage of patients enter a second, more toxic phase within 24 hours of recovering from initial symptoms. High fever returns and several body systems are affected, usually the liver and kidneys. In this phase, people are likely to develop jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes, hence the name yellow fever), dark urine, and abdominal pain with vomiting. Bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes, or stomach. Half of these patients die within 7–10 days.Yellow fever is difficult to diagnose, especially during the early stages.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"A small percentage of patients enter a second phase, which is more toxic within 24 hours after recovering from initial symptoms. A high fever returns and several bodily systems are affected, typically the liver and kidneys. In this phase, people may experience jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes, hence the name jaundice), dark urine, and abdominal pain and vomiting. Bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes, or stomach. Half of these patients die within 7-10 days. Jaundice is difficult to recognize, especially during the early stages."

translations.source: Kesi kali zaidi zinaweza kuchanganyikiwa na malaria kali, leptospirosis, homa ya ini ya virusi (hasa aina za fulminant), homa nyingine za kutokwa na damu, kuambukizwa na flavivirusi zingine (kama vile homa ya dengue haemorrhagic) na sumu. Uchunguzi wa mnyororo wa polymerase (PCR) katika damu na mkojo wakati mwingine unaweza kugundua virusi katika hatua za mwanzo za ugonjwa. Katika hatua za baadaye, uchunguzi wa kutambua kingamwili unahitajika (IgM). Matibabu: Maambukizi ya homa ya manjano yanaweza kusababisha ugonjwa mbaya na inaweza kusababisha kifo katika hali mbaya. Matibabu ya mapema katika hospitali husaidia huboresha viwango vya maisha.
translations.reference: More severe cases can be confused with severe malaria, leptospirosis, viral hepatitis (especially fulminant forms), other haemorrhagic fevers, infection with other flaviviruses (such as dengue haemorrhagic fever) and poisoning. Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing in blood and urine can sometimes detect the virus in early stages of the disease. In later stages, testing to identify antibodies is needed (IgM). Treatment: Yellow fever infections can cause serious illness and can be fatal in severe cases. Early supportive treatment in hospitals improves survival rates.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Severe cases can be confused with severe malaria, leptospirosis, viral hepatitis (especially fulminant forms), other haemorrhagic fevers, other flavivirus infections (such as dengue haemorrhagic fever), and poisoning. Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing in blood and urine can sometimes detect the virus in the early stages of the disease. In later stages, antibody identification testing is required (IgM). Treatment: Yellow fever infection can cause serious illness and can lead to death in severe cases. Early treatment in hospital helps improve survival rates."

translations.source: Kwa sasa hakuna dawa maalum ya kuzuia virusi kwa homa ya manjano, lakini utunzaji maalum wa kutibu upungufu wa maji mwilini, homa, na kushindwa kwa ini na figo huboresha matokeo. Maambukizi ya bakteria yanayohusiana yanaweza kutibiwa na antibiotiki. Wagonjwa wanahitaji kukaa chini ya vyandarua wakati wa mchana ili kupunguza hatari ya kuenea kwa wengine kwa kuumwa na mbu. Chanjo ni kipimo muhimu zaidi cha kuzuia homa ya manjano. Uzuiaji wa milipuko unaweza kupatikana tu ikiwa idadi kubwa ya watu wamechanjwa.
translations.reference: There is currently no specific anti-viral drug for yellow fever, but specific care to treat dehydration, fever, and liver and kidney failure improves outcomes. Associated bacterial infections can be treated with antibiotics. Patients need to stay under mosquito nets during the day to limit the risk of spreading to others through mosquito bites. Vaccination is the single most important measure for preventing yellow fever. The prevention of outbreaks can only be achieved if the majority of the population is immunized.
translations.hypothesis: Currently, there is no specific medication to prevent yellow fever virus, but supportive care to treat dehydration, fever, and liver and kidney failure improves outcomes. Related bacterial infections can be treated with antibiotics. Patients need to stay under mosquito nets during the day to reduce the risk of spreading the disease to others through mosquito bites. Vaccination is the most important preventative measure against yellow fever. Prevention of outbreaks can only be achieved if a large proportion of the population is vaccinated.

translations.source: Chanjo ya homa ya manjano ni salama na ya bei nafuu, na dozi moja hutoa kinga ya maisha dhidi ya ugonjwa huo. Watu wanaopona kutokana na maambukizi ya homa ya manjano pia wanalindwa maisha yote. Soma zaidi kuhusu chanjo ya homa ya manjano. Udhibiti wa mbu pia unaweza kusaidia kuzuia homa ya manjano na ni muhimu katika hali ambapo chanjo ni ndogo au chanjo haipatikani mara moja. Udhibiti wa mbu ni pamoja na kuondoa maeneo ambayo mbu wanaweza kuzaliana na kuua mbu na mabuu wakubwa kwa kutumia dawa za kuua wadudu katika maeneo yenye msongamano mkubwa wa mbu.
translations.reference: The yellow fever vaccine is safe and affordable, and a single dose provides life-long immunity against the disease. People who recover from yellow fever infection are also protected for life. Read more about the yellow fever vaccine. Mosquito control can also help to prevent yellow fever and is vital in situations where vaccination coverage is low or the vaccine is not immediately available. Mosquito control includes eliminating sites where mosquitoes can breed and killing adult mosquitoes and larvae by using insecticides in areas with high mosquito density.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Yellow fever vaccination is safe and affordable, and a single dose provides lifelong protection against the disease. People who recover from yellow fever infection are also protected for life. Read more about yellow fever vaccination. Mosquito control can also help prevent yellow fever and is important in situations where vaccination is limited or vaccination is not immediately available. Mosquito control includes eliminating areas where mosquitoes can breed and killing mosquitoes and larvae using insecticides in areas with high mosquito density."

translations.source: Ushiriki wa jamii kupitia shughuli, kama vile kusafisha mifereji ya maji ya kaya na kufunika vyombo vya maji ambapo mbu wanaweza kuzaliana ni njia muhimu na nzuri ya kudhibiti mbu.
translations.reference: Community involvement through activities, such as cleaning household drains and covering water containers where mosquitoes can breed is an important and effective way to control mosquitoes.
translations.hypothesis: Community participation through activities such as cleaning household drainage channels and covering water containers where mosquitoes can breed is a vital and effective way to control mosquitoes.

translations.source: Watu wenye mahitaji ya matibabu "wameachwa nyuma katika maumivu" inaonyesha ripoti mpya. Shirika la afya Duniani (WHO) limechapisha ripoti mpya kuhusu upatikanaji wa Morphine kwa ajili ya matumizi ya matibabu, ambayo inaeleza jinsi mgawanyo wa kimataifa wa Morphine, kama dawa muhimu ya maumivu, si sawa na haki dhidi ya mahitaji ya matibabu. Ripoti yenye anuwani "Kuachwa nyuma katika maumivu", inaonyesha matatizo katika upatikanaji wa dawa hii muhimu na inatoa hatua za kuboresha upatikanaji salama kupitia sera ya dawa. Licha ya maombi ya Morphine kuwa dawa yenye ufanisi na ya gharama nafuu ya kupunguza maumivu makali, iliyoorodheshwa tangu 1977 katika toleo la kwanza la orodha ya Dawa muhimu ya mfano ya WHO, tofauti ya upatikanaji wa Dawa katika nchi mbalimbali ni mkubwa. Kuna tofauti ya mara 5 hadi 63 katika makadirio ya wastani ya matumizi ya Mofine kati ya nchi za kipato cha juu na nchi za kipato cha chini.
translations.reference: People with medical needs are “left behind in pain” reveals new report The World Health Organization (WHO) has published a new report on access to morphine for medical use, which describes how the global distribution of morphine, as a vital pain medicine, is unequal and does not fulfil the medical need. The report, titled “Left behind in pain”, highlights the problems in access to this essential medicine and offers actions to improve safe access through balanced policy. Despite morphine being an effective and relatively low-cost medicine for relieving strong pain, listed since 1977 in the first edition of the WHO Model list of Essential Medicines, the disparity in access across countries is stark. There is a 5- to 63-fold difference in the estimated median consumption of morphine between high-income countries and lower income countries.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"People with medical needs 'left behind in pain,' a new report shows. The World Health Organization (WHO) has published a new report on the availability of Morphine for medical use, which explains how the international distribution of Morphine, as an essential pain medication, is unequal and unfair in relation to medical needs. The report titled "Left Behind in Pain," highlights problems in the availability of this essential medicine and offers steps to improve safe access through drug policy. Despite Morphine requests being an effective and affordable medicine for reducing severe pain, listed since 1977 in the first edition of the WHO’s Model List of Essential Medicines, the difference in drug availability across countries is significant. There is a 5 to 63 times difference in the average estimates of Morphine use between high-income countries and low-income countries."

translations.source: Mtindo wa matumizi hutofautiana kwa kiasi kibwa katika nchi nzenye utajiri sawa. Hailingani na hitaji la matibabu, kama inavyoonyeshwa na idadi inayokadiriwa ya siku ambazo watu wana maumivu au wanashida ya kupumua kwa watu walio na ugonjwa mbaya. Takwimu hii inalingana na ile ya Tume ya Lancet ya 2018 ambayo ilieleza ukosefu wa upatikanaji wa dawa za kutuliza maumivu kama 'moja wapo ya ukosefu wa usawa mbaya zaidi, uliofichwa katika afya ya kimataifa' huku nchi tajiri zaidi zikiwa na Morphine 10% na 90% ya Opioid sawa na Morphine. "Kuwaacha watu katika maumivu wakati Dawa zinazofaa zinapatikana kwa ajili ya kudhibiti maumivu, hasa katika muktadha wa huduma ya mwisho wa maisha, inapaswa kuwa sababu ya wasiwasi mkubwa kwa watunga sera", alisema Dk Yukiko Nakatani, mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa WHO Dawa na Bidhaa za afya. "Lazima tutee kwa haraka upatikanaji salama na kwa wakati wa Morphine kwa wale walio na mahitaji ya matibabu kupitia sera iliyosasishwa, kila mahali"
translations.reference: Consumption pattern varies significantly across countries of similar wealth; it does not correspond to medical need, as indicated by the estimated number of days people are in pain or suffer with severe shortness of breath for people with a terminal illness. This data echoes that of the 2018 Lancet Commission which described the lack of access to pain relief medication as ‘one of the most heinous, hidden inequities in global health’ with the richest 10% of countries possessing 90% of distributed morphine-equivalent opioids. “Leaving people in pain when effective medicines are available for pain management, especially in the context of end-of-life care, should be a cause of serious concern for policy-makers,” says Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General for Medicines and Health Products. “We must urgently advocate for safe and timely access to morphine for those in medical need through balanced policy, everywhere.”
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Patterns of use vary greatly in countries with similar wealth. This does not align with the medical need, as demonstrated by the estimated number of days people experience pain or difficulty breathing for those with serious illnesses. This figure is consistent with the 2018 Lancet Commission, which described the lack of access to pain medication as 'one of the most egregious inequities, hidden in global health,' with wealthier countries having 10% and 90% of the equivalent of Morphine Opioids. "Leaving people in pain when effective medicines are available to control pain, especially in the context of end-of-life care, should be a major concern for policymakers," said Dr. Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General for Drugs and Health Products. "We must urgently scale up the safe and timely availability of Morphine for those with medical needs through updated policies, everywhere."

translations.source: Sababu za kutofautiana. Upatikanaji wa Morphine kwa matumizi ya matibabu huathiriwa na mambo mengi yanayoingiliana ikiwemo viwezeshaji vinavyohusiana na utawala bora, michakato ya kuaminika/ufanisi ya ununuzi na ugavi, upatikanaji wa rasilimali na shughuli za kujenga uwezo, pamoja na vikwazo vinavyohusiana na sheria na sera zenye vikwazo, utoaji wa huduma duni, na mitazamo na muono usio sahihi. Kutokana na miuktadha tofauti ya nchi, viwezeshaji, vikwazo na vipaumbele vya utekelezaji vinatofautiana; uchunguzi wa wadau uliowasilishwa katika ripoti hii, na wahojiwa kutoka nchi 105 wanachama wa WHO, unathibitisha tofauti hii. Kwa mfano, utoaji usio wa kawaida wa Morphine na Opioids nyingine bora katika vituo vya afya kutokana na ufadhili mdogo ulibainishwa kama kikwazo katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati ikilinganishwa na nchi za kipato cha juu. Zaidi ya theluthi moja ya waliohojiwa katika kanda zote na pia walibainisha vikwazo vinavyotokana na vipengele vya sheria na udhibiti huku wakitambua umuhimu wa mfumo wa kisheria na udhibiti katika kufikia ufikiaji salama.
translations.reference: Reasons for the disparity Access to morphine for medical use is influenced by many interacting factors including enablers related to good governance, reliable/efficient procurement and supply processes, resource availability and capacity-building activities, along with barriers related to overly restrictive legislation and policies, inadequate service provision, and misinformed attitudes and perception. Given different country contexts, enablers, barriers and priorities for action vary; a stakeholder survey presented in this report, with respondents from 105 WHO Member States, confirms this variation. For example, irregular supply of morphine and other strong opioids at health facilities due to limited financing were commonly noted as a barrier in low- and middle-income countries compared to high-income countries. More than a third of respondents across all regions also noted barriers arising from legislative and regulatory factors while recognizing the importance of a legislative and regulatory framework in achieving safe access.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Swahili document into English:

**Reasons for Variation.** Access to Morphine for medical use is affected by many interacting factors, including enablers related to good governance, reliable/efficient procurement and supply processes, availability of resources and capacity-building activities, as well as barriers related to restrictive laws and policies, poor service delivery, and inaccurate perceptions and vision. Due to different country contexts, enablers, barriers, and implementation priorities vary; stakeholder feedback presented in this report, and interviews with respondents from 105 WHO member countries, confirm this variation. For example, irregular supply of Morphine and other essential Opioids in health facilities due to limited funding was identified as a barrier in low- and middle-income countries compared to high-income countries. More than a third of respondents across all regions also identified barriers stemming from legal and regulatory aspects, while recognizing the importance of a legal and regulatory framework in achieving safe access.

translations.source: Mahitaji ya vikwazo visivyofaa vinaweza kuzuia ufikiaji wa magonjwa kwa sababu yanazuia mtiririko wa usambazaji au kufanya kuagiza na kutoa kuwa ngumu kwa wataalamu wa afya. Ni muhimu kutambua kwamba wasiwasi kuhusu madhara yanayoweza kusababishwa na afyuni ni halali, kama vile uwezo wao wa kusababisha matatizo ya matumizi ya Opioid. Kwa sababu hii, kiakisi fulani cha tahadhari kuhusu madhara yanayoweza kutokea kutokana na matumizi ya Opioid (m.f. hutumika katika maumivu ya muda mrefu yasiyo ya kansa) ni muhimu kwa afya ya umma kwa vile matumizi yake yana taarifa za kutosha na yanalingana na hatari. Wasiwasi kama huo haupaswi kudhoofisha manufaa ya matumizi ya Opioid inapoonyeshwa kimatibabu na inapotumiwa kwa usalama na inapotumiwa kwa usalama na wataalamu waliofunzwa. Fursa za kuchukua hatua ripoti inawasilisha seti inayosaidiana ya maeneo ya kuchukuliwa hatua ambayo yanalenga kuboresha ufikiaji salama wa Morphine.
translations.reference: Unduly restrictive requirements could hamper patient access because they impede the flow of supply or make prescribing and dispensing difficult for health-care professionals. It is important to note that concerns about the potential harmful effects of opioids are valid, such as their potential to lead to opioid use disorder. For this reason, a certain amount of caution about the potential harms of opioid use (e.g. use in chronic non-cancer pain) is important for public health insofar as its use is well-informed and proportionate to risks. Such concerns should not undermine the benefits of opioid use when clinically indicated and when used safely by trained professionals. Opportunities for actionThe report presents a complementary set of areas for action that aim to improve safe access to morphine.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Swahili document into English:

"Unnecessary restrictions can hinder access to medication because they obstruct the supply chain or make importing and exporting difficult for healthcare professionals. It is important to recognize that concerns about the harms that can be caused by opioids are legitimate, such as their potential to cause opioid use disorder. For this reason, some level of caution regarding the potential harms from opioid use (e.g., used in long-term non-cancer pain) is important for public health because its use is well-informed and proportionate to the risk. Such concerns should not undermine the benefits of opioid use when medically indicated and when used safely by trained professionals. Opportunities for action: The report presents a coordinated set of areas to be addressed that aim to improve the safe access to Morphine."

translations.source: Haya ni pamoja na kutekeleza programu ndogo ndogo za kikanda au nchi nzima juu ya kuboresha upatikanaji wa Morphine kwa matumizi ya matibabu na kifurushi cha huduma muhimu na bidhaa kwa ajili ya matibabu shufaa ambayo imeundwa kulingana na orodha ya Dawa muhimu ya WHO na kifurushi muhimu cha WHO cha Palliative Care. pia ni pamoja na kuboresha usimamizi, kurahisisha michakato ya ununuzi na ugavi ili kushughulikia uzembe wowote, kuboresha rasilimali kama vile ufadhili thabiti, kuimarisha seti za ujuzi wa wahudumu wa afya, na kuongeza ufahamu kuhusu manufaa na madhara yanaweza kusababishwa na matumizi ya Opioid. Mafanikio ya hatua hizi yanategemea ushirikiano na mshikamano miongoni mwa wadau wote katika ngazi ya kitaifa, kikanda na kimataifa.
translations.reference: These include implementing small-scale regional or state-wide programmes on improving access to morphine for medical use with a package of essential services and products for palliative care that is formulated according to the WHO Model List of Essential Medicines and the WHO Essential Package of Palliative Care. They also include improving governance, streamlining procurement and supply processes to address any inefficiency, improving resourcing such as stable funding, enhancing competent skill sets of the health workforce, and raising awareness about both the benefits and potential harms of opioid use. The success of these actions will hinge on collaboration and cooperation among all stakeholders at national, regional, and global levels.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"This includes implementing small-scale or nationwide programs to improve the accessibility of Morphine for medical use and a package of essential services and products for palliative care, designed based on the WHO's List of Essential Medicines and the WHO's Essential Palliative Care package. It also includes improving management, streamlining procurement and supply processes to address any inefficiencies, improving resources such as stable funding, strengthening the skill sets of healthcare providers, and increasing awareness about the benefits and harms that can be caused by the use of Opioids. The success of these steps depends on collaboration and solidarity among all stakeholders at the national, regional, and international levels."

translations.source: Visiwa vidogo vinakubali mpango shupavu wa utekelezaji kuhusu magonjwa yasiyoambukiza na afya ya akili. Leo, Azimio la Bridgetown la 2023 kuhusu magonjwa yasiyoambukiza (NCDs) na afya ya akili lilizinduliwa, kushughulikia baadhi ya magonjwa hatari Duniani katika nchi zinazoendelea za visiwa vidogo (SIDS), ambavyo vipo hatarini zaidi. Azimio hilo ni matokeo muhimu ya mkutano wa mawaziri wa SIDS wa magonjwa yasiyo ambukiza (NCDs) na afya ya akili, unaoanza tarehe 14 hadi 16 Juni, ulioandaliwa kwa pamoja na shirika la afya Duniani (WHO), shirika la afya la Pan Amerika (PAHO) na serikali ya Barbados. SIDS zinaathiriwa kwa kiasi kikubwa na NCDs- ambayo husababisha 74% ya vifo vyote Ulimwenguni- kutokana na kutegemea chakula kutoka nje, athari za kibiashara na mgogoro wa hali ya hewa. Ripoti mpya ya WHO inaonyesha kuwa nchi 8 kati ya 15 zilizo na hatari zaidi ya 30% ya vifo vya mapema kutokana na magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa, saratani, kisukari, au ugonjwa wa kupumua kwa muda mrefu ni SIDS.
translations.reference: Small islands agree bold plan of action on noncommunicable diseases and mental health Today, the 2023 Bridgetown Declaration on Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs) and Mental Health was launched, to address some of the world’s deadliest diseases in Small Island Developing States (SIDS), which are especially at risk. The Declaration is a key outcome of the SIDS Ministerial Conference on Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs) and Mental Health, running from 14 to 16 June, co-hosted by the World Health Organization (WHO), the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) and the Government of Barbados. SIDS are disproportionately impacted by NCDs – which cause 74% of all deaths globally – due to their reliance on imported food, commercial influences and the climate crisis. A new WHO report shows that 8 of the 15 countries with more than a 30% risk of premature death from cardiovascular disease, cancer, diabetes, or chronic respiratory disease are SIDS.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Small island developing states (SIDS) have endorsed a robust implementation plan regarding non-communicable diseases and mental health. Today, the Bridgetown Initiative 2023 on non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and mental health was launched, addressing some of the most dangerous diseases globally in developing island nations (SIDS), which are at greater risk. The resolution is a key outcome of the SIDS ministerial meeting on non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and mental health, taking place from June 14 to 16, co-organized by the World Health Organization (WHO), the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO), and the government of Barbados. SIDS are disproportionately affected by NCDs – which account for 74% of all deaths worldwide – due to reliance on imported food, trade impacts, and the climate crisis. A new WHO report shows that 8 of the 15 countries with the highest risk (over 30% of premature deaths) from cardiovascular diseases, cancer, diabetes, or chronic respiratory diseases are SIDS.

translations.source: Nchi 10 zilizo na viwango vya juu vya unene wa kupindukia Duniani zote ni SIDS katika pasifiki, ambapo zaidi ya 45% ya watu wazima wanaishi na unene uliokithiri. Hali ya afya ya akili ni ya kawaida katika nchi za SIDS, na kuathiri wastani wa 15.2% ya wakazi huko Karibiani na 11.2% ya wakazi katika Pasifiki. Watu walio na afya ya akili wanakabiliwa na hatari kubwa ya vifo vya mapema, ikiwemo kutokana na hali ya afya ya kimwili ambayo haijashughulikiwa na kutokana na kujiua. Unyanyapaa, uhaba wa wafanyakazi maalumu na athari za mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa huchangia hali yenye changamoto katika SIDS ambayo inahitaji uangalizi wa haraka. Azimio la Bridgetown la 2023 linaonyesha hatua thabiti za kushughulikia masuala mbalimbali ya kijamii, kimazingira, kiuchumi na kibiashara ambayo husababisha NCDs na hali ya afya ya akili.
translations.reference: The 10 countries with the highest obesity rates globally are all SIDS in the Pacific, where over 45% of adults live with obesity. Mental health conditions are common in SIDS countries, affecting an estimated 15.2% of the population in the Caribbean and 11.2% of the population in the Pacific. People with mental health conditions face a higher risk of premature mortality, including from unaddressed physical health conditions and from suicide. Stigma, specialized staff shortages and the impact of climate change contribute to a challenging situation in SIDS that requires immediate attention. The 2023 Bridgetown Declaration outlines bold steps to address the range of social, environmental, economic and commercial issues that lead to NCDs and mental health conditions.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"The 10 countries with the highest rates of obesity worldwide are SIDS in the Pacific, where over 45% of adults live with extreme obesity. Mental health conditions are prevalent in SIDS countries, affecting an average of 15.2% of the population in the Caribbean and 11.2% of the population in the Pacific. People with mental health conditions face a higher risk of premature death, including from unaddressed physical health conditions and from suicide. Abuse, a shortage of specialized personnel, and the effects of climate change contribute to the challenging situation in SIDS which requires urgent attention. The Bridgetown Initiative of 2023 outlines concrete steps to address various social, environmental, economic, and trade issues that contribute to NCDs and mental health conditions."

translations.source: Kuendelezwa kupitia mchakato jumuishi unaoongozwa 'kwa' na 'na' SIDs, tamko hilo linaangazia kwamba NCDs na hali ya Afya ya akili haiwezi kushughulikiwa ipasavyo bila kukabiliana na mgogoro wa hali ya hewa. "Hatua ya ujasiri kwa hali ya hewa, afya njema, na ustawi wetu inategemea kurekebisha na kupanga upya ufadhili wa kimataifa ili kufungua mabilioni ya uwekezaji, na kuifanya iwe chini ya adhabu kwa nchi zinazoendelea kulipa madeni yao", alisema Mheshimiwa Mia Amor Mottley, waziri mkuu wa Barbados. "Ufadhili wa kukabiliana na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa na kukabiliana na mabadiliko ya Tabianchi zilizo hatarini zaidi pia ni muhimu, huku magonjwa yasiyo ambukiza na afya ya akili yakichangia" "Changamoto zinazokabili nchi zinazoendelea za visiwa vidogo zimeunganishwa na zina pande nyingi. Mabadiliko ya Tabianchi, uharibifu wa mazingira, kukosekana kwa usawa wa kijamii na kiuchuni, na vigezo vya kibiashara vya afya vinazidisha mzigo wa NCDs na hali ya afya ya akili", alisema DK Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO.
translations.reference: Developed through an inclusive process led ‘for’ and ‘by’ SIDS, the declaration highlights that NCDs and mental health conditions cannot be properly addressed without responding to the climate crisis. “Bold action for our climate, good health, and well-being relies on redressing and reorganizing global financing to unlock billions in investment, while making it less punishing for developing countries to pay their debts,” said Her Excellency Mia Amor Mottley, Prime Minister of Barbados. “Funding for climate change adaptation and mitigation in the most vulnerable countries is also key, with noncommunicable diseases and mental health accounted for.” "The challenges faced by Small Island Developing States are interconnected and multifaceted. Climate change, environmental degradation, social and economic inequalities, and the commercial determinants of health exacerbate the burden of NCDs and mental health conditions," said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Developed through a collaborative process led by 'for' and 'with' SIDs, the statement highlights that NCDs (Non-Communicable Diseases) and mental health conditions cannot be adequately addressed without tackling the climate crisis. "A bold step for climate action, good health, and our well-being depends on reallocating and restructuring international financing to unlock trillions of investment, and penalizing countries that continue to pay their debts," said the Honorable Mia Amor Mottley, Prime Minister of Barbados. "Financing to address climate change and respond to climate-vulnerable countries is also critical, alongside non-communicable diseases and mental health," she added. "The challenges facing developing small island countries are interconnected and multifaceted. Climate change, environmental degradation, lack of social and economic equity, and trade determinants of health exacerbate the burden of NCDs and mental health conditions," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General.

translations.source: "Licha ya rasilimali zao chache na vikwazo vya kijografia, mataifa haya yameonyesha ujasiri wa ajabu katika kikabiliana na shida. Kwa pamoja, lazima tutengeneze njia mbayo msingi wake ni usawa, uthabiti na uendelevu. Ni lazima tuimarishe mifumo ya afya, tuimarishe hatua za kuzuia na kugundua mapema, na kuweka kipaumbele katika ujumuishaji wa NCD na huduma ya afya ya akili katika huduma ya afya ya msingi. "Mkurugenzi mkuu pia aliahidi kwamba WHO itafanya kazi kuhamasisha rasilimali za kifedha ili kuendeleza vituo vya afya vinavyostahimili hali ya hewa, mazingira endelevu ndani ya SIDS. WHO pia itaendelea kutetea 'hasara na uharibifu' ufadhili wa kukabiliana na mabadiliko ya Tabianchi na uwekezaji wa kupunguza katika nchi za kipato cha chini.
translations.reference: "Despite their limited resources and geographical constraints, these nations have shown remarkable resilience in the face of adversity. Together, we must forge a path that is based on equity, resilience, and sustainability. We must strengthen health systems, enhance prevention and early detection measures, and prioritize the integration of NCD and mental health services into primary health care. "The Director-General also pledged that WHO would work to mobilize financial resources to develop climate resilient, environmentally sustainable health-care facilities in the SIDS. WHO will also continue to advocate for ‘loss and damage’ funding for climate change adaptation and mitigation investments in lower income countries.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Despite their limited resources and geographical challenges, these nations have demonstrated remarkable resilience in facing adversity. Together, we must create pathways based on equity, stability, and sustainability. We must strengthen health systems, reinforce prevention and early detection measures, and prioritize the integration of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) and mental health services into primary healthcare. The Director-General also pledged that WHO will work to mobilize financial resources to develop climate-resilient health facilities and sustainable environments within Small Island Developing States (SIDS). WHO will also continue to advocate for ‘loss and damage’ funding to address climate change and investment in mitigation in low-income countries."

translations.source: Kama sehemu ya kukabiliana na Azimio, SIDS ilijidhatiti kuchukua hatua kadhaa, ikijumuisha maeneo kama vile kushirikisha jamii yote, kuharakisha utekelezaji, kupata uwekezaji, kuoanisha serikali zote na uhasibu kwa maendeleo. Pia mkutano huo ulionyesha ahadi maalumu za SIDS kuhusu NCDs na afya ya akili, ikiwemo kuboresha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya ya akili katika vituo vya huduma za msingi, kusasisha sheria ya udhibiti wa Tumbaku ili kufidia sigara za kielektroniki na mvuke, na kupunguza ulaji wa chumvi na sukari. Ahadi hizi zinapatana na 'manunuzi bora' ya WHO- seti ya afua za gharama nafuu, zenye msingi wa ushahidi ili kukabiliana na NCDs. Orodha ya manunuzi bora ilisasishwa hivi karibuni katika Mkutano wa afya Ulimwenguni mnamo Mei ili kujumuisha kinga ya pili ya Homa ya Rheumatic, udhibiti wa papo hapo na wa muda mrefu wa ugonjwa wa pumu na ugonjwa sugu wa mapafu vile vile afua kadhaa za mlango wa kizazi, matiti, utumbo mpana na Ini, Saratani ya watoto, na matibabu ya kina ya saratani kwa wale wanaoishi na VVU. "SIDS katika bara la Amerika inasalia kuwa hatarini kwa NCDs.
translations.reference: As part of agreeing the Declaration, SIDS signed up to undertake a suite of actions, covering areas such as engaging all of society, accelerating implementation, securing investments, aligning across governments and accounting for progress. The conference also showcased specific commitments by SIDS on NCDs and mental health, including to improve access to mental health care in primary care facilities, update tobacco control legislation to cover e-cigarettes and vaping, and reduce populations’ salt and sugar intake. These commitments align with WHO’s ‘best buys’ – a set of cost-effective, evidence-based interventions to tackle NCDs. The list of best buys was recently updated at the World Health Assembly in May to include secondary prevention for rheumatic fever, acute and long-term management of asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease as well as several interventions for cervical, breast, colorectal, liver and childhood cancer, and the comprehensive treatment of cancer for those living with HIV. “SIDS in the Americas remain overwhelmingly vulnerable to NCDs.
translations.hypothesis: As part of addressing the Declaration, SIDS committed to taking several actions, including areas such as engaging the entire community, accelerating implementation, securing investment, aligning all governments, and accountability for development. The meeting also highlighted specific SIDS commitments regarding NCDs and mental health, including improving access to mental health services in primary health facilities, updating tobacco control legislation to cover electronic cigarettes and vapor, and reducing salt and sugar intake. These commitments align with the WHO's ‘best buys’ – a set of cost-effective, evidence-based interventions to address NCDs. The list of best buys was recently updated at the World Health Assembly in May to include secondary prevention of Rheumatic Fever, immediate and long-term management of asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, as well as interventions for childhood cancers, breast, colorectal and liver cancers, and comprehensive cancer treatment for those living with HIV. "SIDS in the Americas region remains vulnerable to NCDs.

translations.source: Mkutano huu wa mawaziri unakuja katika wakati wa kipekee na utakuwa ufunguo wa kupaza sauti na mahitaji ya SIDS, na kuleta NCDs na afya ya akili mbele ya orodha yetu ya kipaumbele", alisema Dk Jarbas Barbosa, mkurugenzi wa PAHO, na mkurugenzi wa WHO wa kanda ya Amerika. "Ili kuendeleza kasi hii, lazima tuchukue hatua sasa. PAHO ipo tayari kusaidia utekelezaji wa kasi wa afua katika kanda yetu, ikiwa ni pamoja na kujumuisha utunzaji wa hali hizi katika huduma za afya ya msingi na kutumia fursa ya ubunifu kama vile zana za afya za kidijitali". Nchi za SIDS zina rekodi ya kuendesha maamuzi ya kimataifa; mikataba yao ya njia ya bahari ya uhispania na SAMOA ilifungua njia kwa Azimio la kwanza la kisiasa la umoja wa mataifa kuhusu NCDs mnamo 2018. Mkutano huo na Azimio zitafahamisha na kuunda mikutano ya ngazi ya juu ya Baraza kuu la umoja wa mataifa inayokuja kuhusu huduma ya afya kwa wote mwezi Sepemba 2023 na kuhusu NCDs mnamo 2025.
translations.reference: This Ministerial Conference comes at a unique time and will be key to amplifying the voice and needs of SIDS, bringing NCDs and mental health to the forefront of our priority list,” said Dr Jarbas Barbosa, PAHO Director, and WHO Regional Director for the Americas. “To build on this momentum, we must act now. PAHO is ready to support accelerated implementation of interventions in our region, including mainstreaming care for these conditions into primary health services and taking advantage of innovations such as digital health tools.” SIDS countries have a track record of driving global decision-making; their Port of Spain and SAMOA pathway agreements paved the way for the first-ever UN political declaration on NCDs in 2018. The conference and the Declaration will inform and shape forthcoming UN General Assembly High-Level Meetings on universal health coverage in September 2023 and on NCDs in 2025.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"This meeting of ministers comes at a unique time and will be key to amplifying the voices and needs of SIDS, and bringing NCDs and mental health to the forefront of our priority list," said Dr. Jarbas Barbosa, Director of PAHO, and Director of the WHO’s Americas region. "To maintain this momentum, we must take action now. PAHO is ready to support the rapid acceleration of efforts in our region, including incorporating care for these conditions into primary health services and leveraging innovation such as digital health tools." SIDS countries have a track record of driving international decisions; their Spanish maritime route and SAMOA agreements paved the way for the first political declaration of the United Nations on NCDs in 2018. The meeting and the declaration will inform and shape upcoming high-level United Nations General Assembly meetings on universal health coverage in September 2023 and on NCDs in 2025.

translations.source: Mkutano wa washirika wa mpango wa magonjwa ya Kitropiki yaliyosahaulika wa WHO unamalizika kwa wito wa kusisimua wa kuchukua hatua Duniani kote. Wajumbe na wawakilishi waliacha washirika wa mpango wa magonjwa ya Kitropiki yaliyopuuzwa, mkutano na mwito wa kuchukua hatua kutoka kwa mkurugenzi wake Dk Ibrahima Soce Fall. Kama mkutano wa kimataifa, ulihudhuriwa na baadhi ya washirika muhimu 300, ulifikia tamati huko Jeniva Jumanne 13 Juni, Dk Fall aliwataka washiriki kuchukua ujumbe kwamba, "kama hatujipi changamoto sisi wenyewe, hatuwezi kubadilika na kuboresha". Katika hafla kubwa iliyofanyika kukagua maendeleo dhidi ya mpango kabambe wa WHO wa magonjwa ya Kitropiki yaliyosahaulika 2024-2030 na kutoa uelewa mzuri wa shughuli za kimataifa za NTD kutoka kwa mitazamo mahususi ya magonjwa na mtambuko, washirika, wafadhili, washirika wa kiufundi, taasisi na wawakilishi wa nchi waliimarisha dhamira yao ya kufikia lengo kuu: Ulimwengu usio na magonjwa ya Kitropiki yaliyosahaulika ifikapo 2030. Mada kuu ya mkutano ilijikita katika maono ya "jumuia moja inayotoa mpango mmoja wa kimataifa wa NTD".
translations.reference: WHO’s Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting ends with stirring call for worldwide action Delegates and representatives left the Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting with a stirring call to action from its director Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall. As the multinational meeting, attended by some 300 key partners, came to an end in Geneva on Tuesday 13 June, Dr Fall urged participants to take on board the message that, “if we do not challenge ourselves, we cannot evolve and improve”. In a wide-ranging event held to review progress against the WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 and generate a better understanding of global NTD operations from both disease-specific and cross-cutting perspectives, partners, donors, technical partners, institutions and country representatives reinforced their commitment to achieving an ambitious target: a world free from neglected tropical diseases by 2030. The meeting’s primary theme centred around the vision of “One community delivering one Global NTD Programme”.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"The meeting of partners of the WHO’s neglected tropical diseases program concluded with a compelling call to action worldwide. Delegates and representatives left the meeting of the neglected tropical diseases program with a call to action from its director, Dr. Ibrahima Soce Fall. As an international meeting, attended by some 300 key partners, it reached its conclusion in Geneva on Tuesday, June 13. Dr. Fall urged participants to take the message that, "if we don't challenge ourselves, we cannot transform and improve." The major event, held to review progress against the WHO’s ambitious 2024-2030 plan for neglected tropical diseases and to provide a clear understanding of global NTD activities from specific disease and transmission perspectives, saw partners, donors, technical partners, institutions, and country representatives reaffirm their commitment to achieving the ultimate goal: a world free of neglected tropical diseases by 2030. The main theme of the meeting focused on the vision of "one community delivering one global NTD plan."

translations.source: Kuchukua vidokezo vyao kutoka kwa mabadiliko matatu muhimu ya kimkakati yaliyopendekezwa kwenye muongozo- umiliki mkubwa wa nchi, kupima athari si mchakato, na kujitolea kwa shughuli mtambuka na ushirikiano wa ufanisi zaidi- mijadala ilikuwa ya kisayansi, yenye kuona wazi na iliyoshinikizwa pia na haja ya usawa wa afya na haki ili kukabiliana na NTDs. Ni mtazamo kama huo pekee, mkutano ulisikika, unaweza kuleta mabadiliko ambayo yanahitajika kwa baadhi ya jamii maskini zaidi Duniani kufikia malengo ya maendeleo endelevu ya umoja wa mataifa 2030. Katika mkutano wa kwanza kama huu wa kimataifa tangu kuzinduliwa kwa mpango kabambe mnamo Januari 2021, wajumbe walisikika pia kutoka kwa mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Akiwakaribisha washiriki mwanzoni mwa kongamano la siku mbili, Dk Tedros alisema, "mkusanyiko huu unaonyesha kile ambacho WHO inahusu- washirika kuja pamoja ili kuongeza nguvu zao za pamoja kushughulikia changamoto za afya za kimataifa ambazo hata mmoja wetu angeweza kushughulikia peke yake. "Changamoto yenye mambo mengi kama vile magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyopuuzwa inachukua mtazamo wa pande nyingi, na hilo ndilo linalotuleta, pamoja".
translations.reference: Taking their cues from the three key strategic shifts advocated in the road map – greater country ownership, measuring impacts not process, and a commitment to cross-cutting activities and collaboration for greater efficiency – discussions were pragmatic, clear-sighted and suffused also with the need for health equity and justice to tackle NTDs. Only such an approach, the meeting heard, can bring about the change that is needed by some of the world’s poorest communities to achieve the United Nations 2030 Sustainable Development Goals. In the first such global meeting since the launch of the road map in January 2021, delegates heard also from WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Welcoming participants at the beginning of the two-day symposium, Dr Tedros said, “This gathering illustrates what WHO is all about – partners coming together to leverage their collective strength to address global health challenges that none of us could address alone. “A multifaceted challenge like neglected tropical diseases takes a multifaceted approach, and that is what we bring, together”.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Drawing on notes from three key strategic shifts proposed in the guideline – greater country ownership, measuring impact is not a process, and commitment to decentralized activities and more effective collaboration – discussions were scientific, insightful, and also driven by the need for health equity and fairness to address Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs). It was such a perspective alone, the meeting heard, that could bring about the changes needed for some of the world’s poorest communities to achieve the United Nations’ 2030 Sustainable Development Goals. In the first international meeting of its kind since the launch of the Roadmap in January 2021, participants also heard from the WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Welcoming participants at the start of the two-day congress, Dr Tedros said, "This gathering demonstrates what WHO is about – partners coming together to amplify their collective strengths to tackle global health challenges that none of us could handle alone." "A complex challenge such as neglected tropical diseases takes a multi-faceted approach, and that’s what brings us together.”

translations.source: Wajumbe na washiriki walitumia siku mbili mfululizo kubainisha mapungufu na changamoto kuu za kufikia malengo ya hali ya juu na changamoto za ndani, za msingi. Changamoto hizo za msingi ni pamoja na kuhakikisha kwamba watu walioathirika moja kwa moja na NTDs, na wale wanaohusika moja kwa moja na kusimamia afua dhidi ya NTDs, wanaombwa na kutambuliwa, hususani katika suala la mawasiliano na ushirikishwaji wa jamii. Pia mkutano ulibainisha umuhimu wa kuendeleza jamii za mazoezi, ili kujenga uwezo na imani ya wafanyakazi wa kimataifa wa NTD. Katika majadiliano zaidi, wajumbe walikubaliana kuhusu kanuni na hatua za kivitendo za kutekeleza hatua kuelekea malengo yaliyotajwa na kupangwa pamoja kwa mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa wa 2025-2030. Hatua hiyo muhimu na maamuzi yaliyotokana na mkutano huo yalijumuisha mapendekezo ya kuimarisha na kutekeleza majukwaa ya uratibu wa kitaifa ili kuhakikisha uratibu wa mipango ya pamoja na ufuatiliaji na tathmini, na kuhakikisha usimamizi bora wa ugavi ndani ya mifumo ya kitaifa.
translations.reference: Delegates and participants spent the subsequent two days identifying the major gaps and challenges to achieving high-level goals and local, on-the-ground challenges. Those on-the-ground challenges include ensuring that people directly affected by NTDs, and those directly responsible for administering interventions against NTDs, are solicited and acknowledged, particularly in terms of communication and community engagement. The meeting noted also the importance of developing communities of practice, in order to build the capacity and confidence of the global NTD workforce. In further discussions, delegates agreed on principles and practical steps to implement action towards the stated targets and planned together for a Global Action Plan for 2025–2030. Key actions and decisions arising from the meeting included recommendations for strengthening and operationalizing national coordination platforms to ensure coordination for joint planning and for monitoring and evaluation, and to ensure more efficient supply chain management within national systems.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Swahili document into English:

"Delegates and participants spent two consecutive days identifying key gaps and challenges in achieving ambitious goals and fundamental internal challenges. These fundamental challenges include ensuring that people directly affected by NTDs (Neglected Tropical Diseases), and those directly involved in managing the fight against NTDs, are heard and recognized, particularly in terms of communication and community engagement. The meeting also highlighted the importance of developing communities of practice to build the capacity and confidence of the international NTD workforce. In further discussions, delegates agreed on principles and practical steps to implement actions towards the aforementioned goals and jointly developed a plan for the 2025-2030 international implementation plan. Key actions and decisions resulting from the meeting included recommendations to strengthen and implement national coordination platforms to ensure the coordination of joint programs and monitoring and evaluation, and to ensure better supply chain management within national systems."

translations.source: Mkutano wa washirika ulifanyika pia mwishoni mwa mchakato mkubwa wa mapitio ya utendaji ndani ya idara ya WHO ya udhibiti wa magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyopuuzwa. Katika kipindi cha miezi minne iliyopita, WHO imekuwa ikisikiliza michango na ushuhuda kutoka kwa washirika na washikadau wake wote, ili kuhakikisha kwamba yenyewe inajumuisha maadili ya uwajibikaji, ukweli na uwazi ambayo inaunga mkono na kudai. Pia mabadiliko hayo ni pamoja na kuhakikisha kuwa NTDs zimewekwa upya katika usanifu wa kimataifa wa afya. Washiriki wa mkutano walikaribisha msisitizo juu ya majukumu muhimu ambayo jumuiya ya NTD inaweza na ni lazima kutekeleza katika kuchangia changamoto kubwa kama vile huduma ya afya kwa wote, afya moja na ongezeko la tishio la kilimwengu la mabadiliko ya Tabianchi. Wajumbe waliohudhuria ana kwa ana pia waliweza kufurahia maonyesho yanayohusu magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika katika makao makuu ya WHO.
translations.reference: The Partners’ Meeting took place also at the end of a major functional review process within WHO’s Department of Control of Neglected Tropical Diseases. Over the course of the past four months, WHO has been hearing contributions and testimony from all its partners and stakeholders, in order to ensure that it itself embodies the values of accountability, transparency and equity that it espouses and demands. Those values also include ensuring that NTDs are repositioned in the global health architecture. Meeting participants welcomed the emphasis on the key roles that the NTD community can and must play in contributing to wider challenges such as Universal Health Coverage, One Health and the increasing threat of global climate change. Delegates attending in person were also able to enjoy an exhibition dedicated to neglected tropical diseases at WHO headquarters.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"A partner meeting was also held at the end of a major performance review process within the WHO's department for neglected tropical diseases control. Over the past four months, WHO has been listening to contributions and testimonies from all its partners and stakeholders to ensure that it incorporates values of accountability, integrity, and transparency that support and demand them. These changes also include ensuring that neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) are renewed within the international health architecture. Meeting participants welcomed the emphasis on the critical roles that the NTD community can and must fulfill in contributing to major challenges such as health for all, One Health, and the increasing global threat of climate change. Attendees also had the opportunity to enjoy presentations regarding neglected tropical diseases at WHO headquarters."

translations.source: Seti ya paneli ya chuma iliagizwa ili kuonyesha kanuni 20 zinazounda kikundi cha NTD, na hizi ziliambatana na picha na taarifa zinazoonyesha hatua ambazo zimewekwa katika kipindi cha miongo mingi kukabilian nazo.
translations.reference: A set of metal panels was commissioned to illustrate the 20 conditions that make up the NTD grouping, and these were accompanied by photographs and information illustrating the actions that have been put in place over the course of many decades to tackle them.
translations.hypothesis: A set of steel panels was ordered to display the 20 principles that form the NTD group, and these were accompanied by pictures and information showing the steps that have been established over decades to address them.

translations.source: Mkutano wa washirika wa mpango wa magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyopuuzwa, 12-13 Juni 2023. Wawakilishi wa jumuia ya kimataifa ya NTD watakutana Jeniva wiki ijayo ili kushiriki katika mkutano wa washirika wa mpango wa magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyopuuzwa Duniani, ambao utafanyika katika ukumbi wa makao makuu tarehe 12-13 Juni 2023. Malengo ya mkutano huo ni kukagua maendeleo ya muongozo wa WHO wa magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika 2021-2030 na kutoa uelewa mzuri wa operesheni za kimataifa za NTD kutoka kwa mitazamo mahususi ya magonjwa na mtambuka, pamoja na kushiriki katika nyanja mbali mbali mijadala kuhusu maono ya One WHO inayotoa mpango mmoja wa Global NTD. Wakati mikutano ya awali ya kimataifa ya washirika wa NTD iliitishwa na WHO mwaka 2007 na 2017, tukio hili la tatu lijalo ni la kwanza tangu kuzinduliwa kwa mpango kabambe mnamo Januari 2021. Mpango kabambe wa WHO wa magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika 2021-2030 ni mfumo mpana unaoainisha malengo, taratibu za kupatanisha malengo ya maendeleo endelevu na ajenda nyingine za kimataifa na kutoa wito wa mabadiliko ya kimkakati katika mbinu za NTD kwa lengo la kukomesha jenga la magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika ifikapo 2030.
translations.reference: Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting, 12–13 June 2023 Representatives of the global NTD community will gather in Geneva next week to participate in the Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting, which takes place in headquarters’ auditorium on 12-13 June 2023. The objectives of the meeting are to review progress on the WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 and generate a better understanding of global NTD operations from both disease-specific and cross-cutting perspectives, as well as to engage in wide-ranging discussions around the vision for One WHO delivering on one Global NTD Programme. While previous global meetings of NTD partners were convened by WHO in 2007 and 2017, this third upcoming event is the first since the launch of the road map in January 2021. The WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 is a comprehensive framework that outlines targets, mechanisms for alignment with the Sustainable Development Goals and other global agendas and calls for strategic shifts in NTD approaches with the aim of ending the epidemic of neglected tropical diseases by 2030.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Meeting of Partners of the Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme, 12-13 June 2023.** Representatives of the international NTD community will meet in Geneva next week to participate in a meeting of partners of the global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme, which will take place at the organization's headquarters on 12-13 June 2023. The goals of the meeting are to review the progress of the WHO's neglected tropical diseases guidance 2021-2030 and to provide a better understanding of international NTD operations from specific disease and epidemiological perspectives, as well as to participate in various discussions regarding the One WHO vision providing a single Global NTD plan. While previous international partner meetings of NTD were convened by WHO in 2007 and 2017, this third event is the first since the launch of the roadmap in January 2021. The WHO's neglected tropical diseases roadmap 2021-2030 is a broad framework that outlines goals, aligns procedures with the Sustainable Development Goals and other international agendas, and calls for strategic changes in NTD approaches with the aim of ending the cycle of neglected tropical diseases by 2030.

translations.source: Mkutano utazingatia: Kukagua maendeleo ya sasa ya kufikia malengo ya 2030; Kubainisha mapungufu na changamoto kubwa za kufikia malengo hayo; Kukubaliana na kanuni na hatua za kiutendaji za jinsi ya kutekeleza hatua kuelekea malengo; Kuimarisha msaada na kujitolea kutoka kwa washirika wa NTD kutekeleza mpango kabambe na kufikia malengo yake; na
translations.reference: The meeting will focus on: reviewing current progress to achieving the 2030 targets; identifying the major gaps and challenges to reaching those targets; agreeing principles and practical steps for how to implement action towards the targets; galvanizing support and commitment from NTD partners to operationalize the shifts advocated in the road map and to achieve its targets; and

The meeting will focus on: Reviewing the current progress in achieving the 2030 goals; Identifying gaps and major challenges in reaching those goals; Agreeing on principles and practical steps on how to implement actions towards the goals; Strengthening support and commitment from NTD partners to implement the comprehensive plan and achieve its goals; and

translations.source: Kupanga pamoja kwa mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa wa 2025-2030. Zaidi ya washirika 300 katika mkutano wanatarajiwa kuhudhuria, ana kwa ana au mtandaoni. Watajumuisha wawakilishi wa jumuiya ya kitaifa ya NTD, kutoka nchi na wafadhili hadi watekelezaji na washirika wa kiufundi, kutoka mafunzoni hadi vyama vya wagonjwa na vyombo vyengine vyote na taasisi zinazohusika katika mapambano dhidi ya NTDs. Maonyesho ya magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyopuuzwa yatafanyika katika Mezzanine ya jengo B, juu kidogo ya mgahawa. Seti ya paneli za chuma itaonyesha kanuni 20 zilizowekwa chini ya mwamvuli wa NTD na kutoa taarifa kuhusu hatua zinazotekelezwa ili kukabiliana na mzigo wao.
translations.reference: planning together for a Global Action Plan 2025–2030. Over 300 participants in the meeting are expected to attend, either in-person or online. They will include representatives of the global NTD community, from countries and donors to implementing and technical partners, from academia to patients’ associations and all other entities and institutions involved in the fight against NTDs. An exhibition on neglected tropical diseases will take place in the mezzanine of the B building, just above the cafeteria. A set of metal panels will illustrate the 20 conditions grouped under the NTD umbrella and provide information on actions implemented to tackle their burden.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Joint planning for the 2025-2030 international implementation plan. More than 300 partners are expected to attend the meeting, in person or online. They will include representatives of the national NTD community, from countries and donors to implementers and technical partners, from schools to patient organizations and all other bodies and institutions involved in the fight against NTDs. Displays of neglected tropical diseases will take place in the Mezzanine of Building B, just above the restaurant. A set of steel panels will showcase the 20 principles set out under the NTD umbrella and provide information about the measures being taken to address their burden."



**Explanation of some key translation choices:**

*   **NTD:** I've kept "NTD" as it is, as it's a standard abbreviation for "Neglected Tropical Diseases" in this context.
*   **Mwamvuli wa NTD:** Translated as "the NTD umbrella" to convey the idea of a framework or collective effort.
*   **Maonyesho:** Translated as "Displays" to accurately reflect the presentation of information.

translations.source: WHO inatangaza washindi wa Tamasha la 4 la Afya kwa wote 7 kushinda filamu fupi, 4 kutajwa maalumu kutoka Jury Shirika la afya Duniani limetangaza uteuzi rasmi rasmi wa filamu zilizoshinda mwaka huu katika tamasha lake la 4 la filamu la afya kwa wote, lililofanyika makao makuu ya WHO, Jeniva. Hafla hiyo, ilihudhuriwa ana kwa ana na mtandaoni na waigizaji, watayarishani na watu mashuhuri, ilishuhudia filamu zilizoshinda zikitangazwa kwa kategoria 7 tofauti, huku filamu 4 zikitajwa maalumu kutoka Jury.
translations.reference: WHO announces winners of the 4th Health for All Film Festival 7 winning short films, 4 special mentions from the jury The World Health Organization has announced the official selection of this year’s winning films at its 4th Annual Health for All Film Festival, held at WHO Headquarters, Geneva. The event, attended in person and online by actors, producers and public figures, saw winning films announced for 7 different categories, while 4 films received special mentions from the jury.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

WHO announces winners of the 4th Health for All Film Festival – 7 films win short films, 4 specially mentioned by the Jury. The World Health Organization has officially announced the selection of the winning films this year at its 4th Health for All Film Festival, held at WHO headquarters in Geneva. The event, attended in person and online by actors, producers, and celebrities, saw the winning films announced in 7 different categories, with 4 films specially mentioned by the Jury.

translations.source: Huu ni mwaka wa nne wa tamasha la filamu na ushindani ulikuwa mkali sana, na baadhi ya filamu 93 ziliorodheshwa zilizingatia masuala kuanzia wasiwasi hadi unyogovu hadi athari za mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa kwa afya, vile vile changamoto za afya za watu wenye ulemavu. Filamu zilihukumiwa na jopo la watalamu mashuhuri, wasanii na wanaharakati, wakiwemo waigizaji mashuhuri Sharon Stone na Alfonso Herrera, mwandishi wa densi, Sherrie Silver, mwanaharakati wa hali ya hewa, Sophia kianni na mtunzi wa vyombo vya habari, Adelle Onyango. Walijumuika na mafisa wakuu wa umoja wa mataifa na wafanyakazi wa WHO. "Tamasha la afya kwa wote la filamu huleta sura ya kibinadamu kwa kazi ya kisayansi ya WHO", mkurugenzi wa WHO, Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus alisema. "Kusikiliza sauti za watu walioathiriwa na masuala ya afya ni njia nzuri ya kuongeza ufahamu na kuboresha uelewa wetu wa uzoefu wa watu na hii inaweza kutusaidia kusonga mbele kuelekea afya kwa wote"
translations.reference: This is the fourth year of the film festival and competition was no less fierce, with some 93 shortlisted films covering issues ranging from anxiety and depression through to the effects of climate change on health, as well as the health challenges of people with disabilities. The films were judged by a panel of distinguished professionals, artists and activists, including the renown actors, Sharon Stone and Alfonso Herrera, dance choreographer, Sherrie Silver, climate activist, Sophia Kianni and media personality, Adelle Onyango. They were joined by senior United Nations officials and WHO staff. “The Health for All Film Festival brings a human face to WHO’s scientific work,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “Listening to the voices of people affected by health issues is a powerful way to raise awareness and improve our understanding of people’s experiences and this can help us advance towards health for all.”
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"This is the fourth year of the film festival and the competition was very fierce, with some of the 93 films listed addressing issues ranging from anxiety to depression to the effects of climate change on health, as well as health challenges faced by people with disabilities. The films were judged by a panel of prominent experts, artists, and activists, including renowned actors Sharon Stone and Alfonso Herrera, choreographer Sherrie Silver, climate activist Sophia Kianni, and media personality Adelle Onyango. They were joined by senior officials from the United Nations and WHO staff. "The Film for Health Festival brings a human face to the WHO's scientific work," said WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Listening to the voices of people affected by health issues is a great way to raise awareness and improve our understanding of people's experiences, and this can help us move forward towards health for all."

translations.source: Kutoka kwa uteuzi rasmi "GRAND PRIX" inahusishwa kwa kila moja ya kategoria kuu tatu za mashindano: Huduma ya afya kwa wote, dharura za afya, na afya bora na ustawi, ambayo inalingana na malengo ya bilioni tatu za WHO. Sharon Stone, Golden Globe na mwigizaji aliyeshinda tuzo ya Emmy kutoka Marekani, mtayarishaji na mwanaharakati alisema; "Nimefurahi kuwa sehemu ya tamasha la filamu la afya kwa wote. Hii ni kuhusu kujenga ufahamu bora juu ya hatua muhimu zinahitajika ili kufikia hali bora ya maisha Duniani kote. Simulizi zilizochaguliwa zinazungumza nasi kuhusu thamani ya asili ya afya bora na ufikiaji wake, na zinateteta huduma ya afya kwa wote.
translations.reference: From the official selection one “GRAND PRIX” is attributed for each of the three main competition categories: Universal Health Coverage, Health emergencies, and Better health and well-being, which align with WHO’s Triple Billion Targets. Sharon Stone, Golden Globe and Emmy Award-winning actress from the United States of America, producer and activist for health and humanitarian causes said: "I am delighted to be part of the Health for All Film Festival. This is about creating better awareness on crucial actions needed for reaching healthier living conditions around the world. The stories selected talk to us about the intrinsic value of good health and its access, and they advocate for universal health coverage.

translations.source: Huduma ya afya ya wote ni haki muhimu sana, ni haki ya Binadamu kwa kila mtu Duniani kote. Mada ya afya ya akili iliangaziwa sana katika maingizo yaliyoshinda mwaka huu, ikijumuisha filamu fupi yenye nguvu na ya kusisimua kutoka Swideni kuhusu wasiwasi na mfadhaiko. Filamu hiyo, ikiwa na sauti ya kusisimua, iliangazia mfululizo wa waigizaji wakieleza hatua mbalimbali za wasiwasi na jinsi inavyoweza kujidhihirisha. Ujumbe wa jumla ulikuwa kwamba haupo peke yako katika uzoefu wa mawazo hasi na kwamba ni sawa kukiri hisia kama hizo.
