Notes to editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with approximately 125 million people living in areas needing prevention against the disease.

translations.source: Trakoma hupatikana hasa katika maeneo ya maskini zaidi na ya vijijini zaidi ya Afrika, Amerika ya kati na Kusini, Asia, Pasifiki ya Magharibi na Mashariki ya kati. WHO kanda ya Afrika imeathiriwa kwa kiasi kikubwa na Trakoma huku watu milioni 105 wakiishi katika maeneo hatarishi, ambayo inawakilisha 84% ya wingi wa Trakoma Duniani. Trakoma ni chanzo kikuu cha maradhi ya upofu Duniani kote. Ugonjwa husababishwa kwa kuambukizwa na bakteria Clamydia Trachomatis. Maambukizi huama kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kupitia vidole vilivyochafuliwa, Fomites na nzi ambao wamegusana na uchafu kutoka katika macho au pua ya mtu aliyeambukizwa.
translations.reference: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Trachoma is primarily found in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western and Eastern Pacific, and the Middle East. The WHO African region is most affected by trachoma, with 105 million people living in endemic areas, representing 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacteria Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites (inanimate objects), and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person.

translations.source: Mambo hatarishi ya kimazingira kwa maambukizi ya Trakoma ni pamoja na hali duni ya usafi, kaya zenye msongamano mkubwa wa watu, kutopata maji ya kutosha au matumizi ya vifaa vya usafi wa mazingira. Mnamo 1996, WHO ilizindua muungano wa WHO wa kutokomeza Trakoma Duniani kufikia mwaka 2020 (GET 2020). Pamoja na washirika wengine katika muungano, WHO inaunga mkono utekelezaji wa mkakati SAFE nchini na uimarishaji wa uwezo wa kitaifa kupitia tathmini ya magonjwa, usimamiaji, ufuatiliaji, tathmini ya mradi na uhamasishaji wa rasilimali unaochangia kutokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. Muongozo, ulioidhinishwa na baraza la afya Duniani mwaka 2020 kupitia uamuzi wake wa 73(33), inaweka 2030 kama tarehe mpya inayolengwa ya kutokomezwa Duniani.
translations.reference: Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

"Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, lack of access to sufficient water, or use of inadequate environmental sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by 2020 (GET 2020). Together with other partners in the alliance, WHO supports the implementation of the SAFE strategy nationally and the strengthening of national capacity through disease assessment, management, monitoring, project evaluation, and resource mobilization contributing to the elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The guidelines, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its resolution 73(33), set 2030 as the new target date for global elimination."

translations.source: Watoto milioni 152 walizaliwa njiti katika muongo uliopita Kati ya watoto 10 wanaozaliwa, 1 ni njiti - na kila sekunde 40, mtoto 1 kati ya hao hufariki Viwango vya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati havijabadilika katika muongo mmoja uliopita katika eneo lolote la Dunia. Athari za migogoro, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, UVIKO-19 zinaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali. Hatua zinahitajika haraka ili kuboresha uzuiaji wa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, pamoja na utunzaji bora kwa watoto walioathiriwa na familia zao.
translations.reference: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"152 million children were born prematurely in the last decade. Of every 10 babies born, 1 is premature - and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies. Rates of preterm birth have not changed in the last decade in any region of the world. The effects of conflict, climate change, and COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and children everywhere. Urgent action is needed to improve prevention of preterm birth, as well as better care for affected children and their families."

translations.source: Inakadiriwa watoto milioni 13.4 walizaliwa Njiti mwaka 2020, na karibu milioni 1 wakifa kutokana na matatizo ya unjiti, kulingana na ripoti mpya iliyotolewa leo na mashirika ya umoja wa mataifa. Hii ni sawa na mtoto 1 kati ya 10 aliyezaliwa mapema (kabla ya wiki 37 za ujauzito) Duniani kote. Alizaliwa hivi karibuni: Muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, iliyotolewa na shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) na shirika la Umoja wa mataifa la kuhudumia watoto (UNICEF) pamoja na PMNCH - muungano mkubwa zaidi Duniani wa wanawake, watoto, na vijana, inapiga kengele kwenye "Dharura ya kimya kimya" ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, ambayo haijatambuliwa kwa muda mrefu katika kiwango na ukali wake, ambayo inazuia maendeleo katika kuboresha afya na maisha ya watoto. Ripoti hiyo inajumuisha makadirio yaliyosasishwa kutoka WHO na UNICEF, iliyotayarishwa na shule ya London ya usafi na madawa ya kitropiki, kuhusu kuenea kwa watoto wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati. Kwa ujumla, ripoti inagundua kuwa viwango vya kuzaliwa Njiti havijabadilika katika eneo lolote Duniani katika muongo mmoja uliopita, na watoto milioni 152 walio katika mazingira magumu walizaliwa hivi karibuni kutoka 2010 hadi 2020.
translations.reference: An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

An estimated 13.4 million children were born in preterm in 2020, and nearly 1 million died from preterm complications, according to a new report released today by United Nations agencies. This is equivalent to 1 in 10 babies born prematurely (before 37 weeks of gestation) worldwide. Recently released: A Decade of Action on Preterm Birth, published by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) along with PMNCH - the largest global alliance of women, children, and youth - rings the alarm on a "silent emergency" of preterm birth, which has long been underestimated in scale and severity, hindering progress in improving the health and lives of children. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared by the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, the report finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region of the world in the past decade, and 152 million children in vulnerable situations were born preterm between 2010 and 2020.

translations.source: Kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati sasa ndio sababu kuu ya vifo vya watoto, ikichukua zaidi ya 1 kati ya vifo 5 vya watoto vinavyotekea kabla ya miaka 5 ya kuzaliwa. Waathirika wa kuzaliwa Njiti hukabiliwa na matokeo ya afya ya maisha yote, na kuongezeka uwezekano wa ulemavu na uchelewaji wa maendeleo. Mapengo ya kuishi kwa kanda, kipato, rangi Kutokana na ripoti ya kihistoria kuhusu mada mnamo 2012, ripoti hii mpya ya "Muongozo" wa kuzaliwa hivi karibuni inatoa muhtasari wa kina wa kuenea kwa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati na athari zake kwa wanawake, familia, jamii na uchumi. Mara nyingi mno, mahali ambapo watoto huzaliwa huamua kama wataishi.
translations.reference: Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Being born prematurely is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 child deaths occurring before the age of 5. Preterm survivors face lifelong health consequences, with an increased risk of disability and developmental delay. Disparities in survival exist by region, income, and ethnicity. Following a historical report on the topic in 2012, this new "Guideline" report on preterm birth provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its impact on women, families, communities, and the economy. Far too often, where a child is born determines whether they will survive.

translations.source: Ripoti hiyo inabainisha kuwa ni mtoto 1 kati ya 10 wanaozaliwa mapema kabla ya wakati (chini ya wiki 28) wanaishi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini, ikilinganishwa na zaidi ya 9 kati ya 10 katika nchi zenye kipato cha juu. Pengo la ukosefu wa usawa kuhusiana na rangi, kabila, mapato, ufikiaji wa huduma bora huamua uwezekano wa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, kifo, na ulemavu, hata katika nchi zenye kipato cha juu. Kusini mwa Asia na Afrika kusini mwa jangwa la Sahara ndizo zinazo viwango vya juu zaidi vya kuzaliwa Njiti, na watoto wachanga katika maeneo haya wanakabiliwa na hatari kubwa zaidi ya vifo. Kwa pamoja, maeneo haya mawili yanachaangia zaidi ya 65% ya watoto wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati wao kukamilika Duniani. Pia ripoti hiyo inaangazia kwamba athari za migogoro, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, na uharibifu wa mazingira, UVIKO-19, na kupanda kwa gharama za maisha kunaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali.
translations.reference: The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"The report indicates that 1 in 10 babies born prematurely (before 28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality services determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. South Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm births, and newborns in these regions face a greater risk of mortality. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of babies born prematurely worldwide. The report also highlights that the effects of conflicts, climate change, environmental degradation, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and children everywhere."

translations.source: Kwa mfano, uchafuzi wa hewa unakadiriwa kuchangia watoto milioni 6 wanaozaliwa Njiti kila mwaka. Karibu mtoto 1 kati ya 10 anayezaliwa kabla ya wakati analiwa katika nchi 10 dhaifu zaidi zilizoathiriwa na majanga ya kibinadamu, kulingana na uchambuzi mpya katika ripoti hiyo. Hatari za afya ya Uzazi, kama vile mimba za utotoni na pre-eclampsia, zinahusishwa kwa karibu na kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati. Hii inasisitiza haya ya kuhakikisha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya ya ngono na uzazi, ikiwa ni pamoja na upangaji uzazi bora, na utunzaji wa hali ya juu wakati wa ujauzito na wakati wa kujifungua. Ajenda ya utekelezaji: uwekezaji zaidi wa nchi na harakati zinazoongozwa na wazazi
translations.reference: For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million babies born prematurely each year in Africa. Nearly 1 in 10 babies born before term are affected in the 10 countries most impacted by humanitarian crises, according to new analysis in the report. Health risks related to reproductive health, such as teenage pregnancies and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to premature births. This emphasizes the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including quality family planning, and high-quality care during pregnancy and childbirth. Implementation agenda: increased country investment and parent-led initiatives."

translations.source: Pia muuongo uliopita umeshuhudia ukuaji wa harakati wa jamii juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati na kuzuia uzazi, unaoendeshwa na mitandao ya wazazi, wataalamu wa afya, wasomi, Asasi za kiraia, na wengine. Kote ulimwenguni, Makundi ya familia zilizoathiriwa za kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati wao wamekuwa mstari wa mbele kutetea upatikanaji wa matunzo bora na mabadiliko ya sera na kusaidia familia nyingine. Kuelekea kongamano la kimataifa la afya ya mama wajawazito, huko Cape Town, Afrika Kusini, kuanzia tarehe 8-11 Mei, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA na PMMNCH wanatoa wito wa kuchukua hatua zifuatazo ili kuboresha huduma kwa wanawake na watoto wachanga na kupunguza hatari zinazotokana na kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati: Ongezeko la uwekezaji: Kuhamasisha rasilimali za kimataifa na za ndani ili kuboresha afya ya uzazi na watoto wachanga, kuhakikisha utunzaji wa hali ya juu unatolewa wakati na mahali unapohitajika. Kuharakisha utekelezaji: Kufikia malengo ya nchi kwa ajili ya maendeleo kupitia sera zilizowekwa za kitaifa za utunzaji wa uzazi na watoto wachanga.
translations.reference: The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"The past two decades have witnessed the growth of community movements on preterm birth and preventing stillbirths, driven by networks of parents, healthcare professionals, academics, civil society organizations, and others. Worldwide, groups of families affected by preterm birth have been at the forefront advocating for access to quality care, policy changes, and supporting other families. Ahead of the International Conference on Maternal and Newborn Health in Cape Town, South Africa, from May 8-11, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA, and PMMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve services for women and newborns and reduce risks associated with preterm birth: Increased investment: Mobilize international and domestic resources to improve maternal and newborn health, ensuring quality care is provided in a timely and appropriate manner. Accelerate implementation: Achieve country targets for development through national policies on maternal and newborn care."

translations.source: Ushirikiano katika sekta zote: Kukuza elimu kupitia mzunguko wa maisha; kusaidia uwekezaji mzuri wa kiuchumi; ufadhili wa pamoja katika sekta zote; kuimarisha majibu ya kukabiliana na hali ya hewa katika kipindi chote cha maisha; na kuendeleza uratibu na uthabiti wa mifumo ya dharura. Uvumbuzi unaoendeshwa ndani ya nchi: Kuwekeza katika uvumbuzi na utafiti unaoongozwa na ndani ili kusaidia uboreshaji wa ubora wa utunzaji na usawa katika ufikiaji. Nukuu: Profesa Joy Lawn, Shule ya London ya usafi na Tiba ya kitropiki Kiongozi mwenza, Alizaliwa hivi karibuni: Muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati (2023) na kuzaliwa hivi karibuni (2012) "Ripoti hii mpya inaonyesha kuwa gharama ya kutochukua hatua katika muongo uliopita ilikuwa watoto milioni 152 walizaliwa mapema sana.
translations.reference: Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

**Collaboration across all sectors:** Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting sound economic investment; joint funding across all sectors; strengthening climate change response mechanisms throughout the life cycle; and developing coordination and resilience of emergency systems. **Country-driven innovation:** Investing in country-led innovation and research to support improvements in the quality of care and equity in access. **Quote:** Professor Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine Co-Lead, Born Too Soon: A Decade of Action on Preterm Birth (2023) and Early Childhood (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the past decade was 152 million children born too soon."

translations.source: Wakati baadhi ya maeneo yameathiriwa zaidi, uzazi wa mapema unatishia maendeleo ya kiafya katika kila nchi. Uwekezaji mkubwa katika utunzaji wa watoto wachanga walio katika mazingira magumu unaweza kuokoa mamilioni ya familia kutokana na huzuni. Pia kazi zaidi inahitajika ili kuzuia kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, ambayo pia itaboresha maendeleo katika kupunguza vifo vya uzazi na vifo vya watoto wachanga. Kwa pamoja kauli mbizi hizi za kuzuia na kutunzaa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati zitazalisha watu binafsi na jamii zenye afya zaidi ili kuleta maendeleo ya kiuchumi na kijamii. Kizazi chetu kijacho kinatutegemea sisi sote kuchukua hatua sasa -- uwekezaji unaweza usiwe mdogo lakini faida ya uwekezaji huu itakuwa kubwa kwa nchi"
translations.reference: While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country"
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"While some areas are affected more than others, early childhood threatens health development in every country. Significant investment in the care of young children in difficult circumstances can save millions of families from grief. Also, more work is needed to prevent premature births, which will also improve progress in reducing maternal mortality and infant mortality. Together, these two strategies of preventing and caring for premature births will produce healthier individuals and communities to bring about economic and social development. Our next generation depends on all of us to take action now -- the investment may not be small, but the benefit of this investment will be great for the country."

translations.source: Dk Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon mwandishi mwenza na kiongozi wa kitaifa wa Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) "Sasa hakuna kisingizio cha kunyamaza. Sasa tuna mikononi mwetu vifaa na maarifa ya kubadilisha matokeo kwa watoto wetu wachanga walio katika mazingira magumu zaidi. Najivunia kuona serikali yangu sasa inawekeza na kujitolea utekelezaji hata maeneo ya vijijini. Muongo ujao uanaweza na lazima uwe tofauti kwa wale wanaokabiliwa na kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati hapa na kila mahali"
translations.reference: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) “There is now no excuse for silence. We now have in our hands the devices and the knowledge to change outcomes for our most vulnerable newborns. I am proud to see my government now investing and committed to implementation across even rural areas. The next decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere.”
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

Dr. Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon (co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360)) "There is no excuse for silence now. We now have the tools and knowledge to change outcomes for our newborns in the most challenging environments. I am proud to see my government now investing and committing to implementation even in rural areas. The coming decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere."

translations.source: Anshu Banerjee, mkurugenzi wa afya ya uzazi, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana ndani ya WHO "Kuhakikisha huduma bora kwa watoto hawa wadogo zaidi, walio katika mazingira magumu zaidi na familia zao ni muhimu sana kwa kuboresha afya na maisha ya mtoto. Pia maendeleo yanahitajika ili kusaidia kuzuia uzazi kabla ya wakati -- hii inamaana kwamba kila mwanamke lazima aweze kupata huduma bora za afya kabla na wakati wa ujauzito ili kutambua na kudhibiti hatari" Steven Lauwerier, mkurugenzi wa afya (a.i.), UNICEF "Baada ya kila kifo cha mapema ni njia ya kupotez na kuvunjika moyo.
translations.reference: Dr Anshu Banerjee, Director for Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing at WHO “Ensuring quality care for these tiniest, most vulnerable babies and their families is absolutely imperative for improving child health and survival. Progress is also needed to help prevent preterm births -- this means every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks.” Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF “After every preterm death is a trail of loss and heartbreak.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"Anshu Banerjee, Director of Reproductive Health, Newborns, Children and Adolescents within WHO, "Ensuring quality care for these smallest children, who are in the most difficult environments and their families is extremely important for improving child health and life. Development is also needed to help prevent early pregnancies – this means that every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks." Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF "After every early death is a path of loss and heartbreak."

translations.source: Licha ya maendele mengi ambayo ulimwengu umefanya katika muongo mmoja uliopita, hataujafanya maendeleo yoyote katika kupunguza idadi ya watoto wadogo wanaozaliwa mapema au kuepusha hatari ya kifo chao. Uhuru ni mbaya. Ni wakati wa kuboresha upatikanaji wa huduma kwa mama wajawazito na watoto wachanga wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati na kuhakikisha kila mtoto anapata mwanzo mzuri na kustawi maishani" Helga Fogstad, mkurugenzi mtendaji, PMNCH "Kuzaliwa mapema kunasisitiza kwa nini ni lazima kuongeza uwekezaji na uwajibikaji kwa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, sababu kubwa zaidi ya vifo vya watoto chini ya umri wa miaka mitano, Duniani.
translations.reference: Despite the many advances the world has made in the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of small babies born too soon or averting the risk of their death. The toll is devasting. It’s time we improve access to care for pregnant mothers and preterm infants and ensure every child gets a healthy start and thrives in life.” Helga Fogstad, Executive Director, PMNCH “Born too soon underlines why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth – the world’s largest cause of death of children under the age of five.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Despite the many advances the world has made in the last decade, there has been no progress in reducing the number of babies born prematurely or preventing the risk of their death. This is unacceptable. It is time to improve access to services for pregnant women and newborns born prematurely and to ensure every child gets a good start and thrives in life." Helga Fogstad, CEO, PMNCH "Preterm birth underscores why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth, the leading cause of death for children under five years of age, globally."

translations.source: Maendeleo yanadorora kwa afya ya uzazi na watoto wachanga, vilevile kuzuia watoto wanaokufa, na sasa yamerejeshwa nyuma zaidi kupitia mchanganyiko mbaya wa UVIKO_19, mabadiliko ya hewa, migogoro inayoongezeka na kupanda kwa gharama za maisha. "Kwa kufanya kazi pamoja kwa ushirikiano- serikali, wafadhili, sekta binafsi, asasi za kiraia, wazazi, na wataalamu wa afya - tunaweza kupinga kengele kuhusu "Dharura hii ya kimya", na kuleta jitihada za kuzuia na matunzo mbele ya afya ya kitaifa na juhudi za maendeleo, kujenga mtaji wa watu kwa kusaidia familia, jamii na uchumi kila mahali". "Gabriela na Jerome Foster, Las Vegaz, Marekani: wazazi wa Janel Foster, na waanzilishi wa Wakafu wa Jalen Gift. "Wakfu wetu ulianzishwa baada ya kufiwa na mtoto wetu wa kiume, Jalen Foster, ambae alijifungua mtoto Njiti akiwa na wiki 27 kutokana na kutokea kwa Bakteria wanaojulikana kama Streptococcus kundi B. Kumpoteza mtoto wetu wa kiume kulituumiza sana, na hakukuwa na usaidizi katika jamii na ndipo tulipoamua kutoa mahali pa faraja na matumaini kwa familia ambazo zimepoteza mtoto.
translations.reference: Progress is flatlining for maternal and newborn health, as well as the prevention of stillbirths, and is now pushed back further through the devastating combination of COVID-19, climate change, expanding conflicts and rising living costs. “By working together in partnership – governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can sound the alarm about this “silent emergency”, and bring preterm prevention and care efforts to the forefront of national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, societies and economies everywhere. ”Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, and founders of Jalen’s Gift Foundation “Our foundation was founded after the loss of our son, Jalen Foster, who was a preterm stillbirth at 27 weeks due to prenatal onset of a bacteria known as group B streptococcus. Losing our son was devastating, and there was no support in the community and that’s when we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a baby.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Progress in maternal and child health, as well as preventing child deaths, is now being reversed due to a toxic mix of COVID-19, climate change, increasing conflicts, and rising cost of living. "By working together collaboratively – government, donors, the private sector, civil society organizations, parents, and health professionals – we can push back against the alarm bells about this "silent emergency," and bring prevention and care efforts forward in national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, communities, and economies everywhere." "Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, USA: parents of Janel Foster, and founders of the Jalen Gift Foundation. "Our foundation was established after the passing of our son, Jalen Foster, who delivered a baby prematurely at 27 weeks due to a Streptococcus Group B bacteria. Losing our son was deeply painful, and there was no support in the community, which is why we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a child."

translations.source: Hatukutaka familia nyingine zijisikie kama sisi- peke yao. Kwa hiyo, tulianzisha Wakfu wa Jalen Gift ili kusaidia huduma nyingi bila malipo, kama vile picha, usaidizi wa huzuni na usaidizi kuelekea huduma za ukumbusho" *** Pakua Born too Soon rasilimali za media
translations.reference: We didn’t want other families to feel like we did – alone. So, we started Jalen’s Gift Foundation to help other families going through a loss, to provide multiple free services, such as photograph, grief support and help towards memorial services.” ***Download the Born too soon media resources
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"We didn't want another family to feel like we did – alone. That's why we established the Jalen Gift Foundation to help numerous services free of charge, such as photography, grief support, and guidance towards memorial services." *** Download Born too Soon media resources.

translations.source: UAE na WHO zinapeleka vifaa muhimu vya matibabu nchini sudani Umoja wa falme za kiarabu (UAE) na shirika la afya Duniani zimewasilisha tani 30 za vifaa vya dharura vya matibabu nchini sudani leo. Ndege iliyobeba vifaa vya matibabu ya majeraha, upasuaji wa dharura, dawa muhimu iliwasili katika uwanja wa ndege wa Port sudan mapema leo asubuhi. Shehena hiyo, yenye thamani ya Dola za kimarekani 444,000, ni ya kwanza ambayo WHO imeweza kuwasilisha kwa ndege hadi Sudani tangu kuzuka kwa mzozo huo. WHO ilisambaza vifaa kwa vituo vya afya kabla ya kuongezeka kwa mgogoro.
translations.reference: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

**UAE and WHO Deliver Essential Medical Supplies to Sudan**

The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization (WHO) have delivered 30 tons of emergency medical supplies to Sudan today. An aircraft carrying medical supplies for injuries, emergency surgery, and essential medicines arrived at Port Sudan airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$444,000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to health centers before the escalation of the crisis.

translations.source: Viliishiwa baada ya siku chache kutokana idadi ya waliojeruhiwa. Kutumwa kwa ndege hiyo kunakuja kama sehemu ya juhudi za umoja wa falme za kiarabu kuwaunga mkono watu wa Sudani na kuakisi uhusiano wake wa kina na Sudani, hasa kwa kuzingatia hali ya sasa ya nchi hiyo. Pia misaada hiyo ni upanuzi wa maono ya kibinadamu ya UAE na inasisitiza uhusiano wake na nchi nyingie na kulingana na udugu wa kibinadamu na kujitolea kwake kusaidia wengine wakati wa majanga na hali za Dharura. Dk Reem binti Ebrahim Al hashimy, waziri wa nchi na ushirikiano wa kimataifa, alisema kuwa UAE "inaendelea kufanya kazi kwa karibu na shirika la afya Duniani ili kuhakikisha kupelekwa kwa shughuli muhimu za usafirishaji kutuma msaada wa haraka wa matibabu na chakula kusaisaidia kukabiliana na mzozo wa sasa nchini sudani. Sambamba na kutoa wito kwa pande zote za mzozo wa sasa kusitisha mara moja uhasama na kupunguza hali inayosababisha mateso yasiyoelezeka kwa watu wa Sudani, UAE iko imara katika ahadi yake ya kutoa misaada ya usaidizi kwa nchi wakati wa mahitaji.
translations.reference: These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Swahili document into English:

"They were evacuated after a few days due to the number of injuries. The sending of the plane came as part of the United Arab Emirates' efforts to support the people of Sudan and reflects its deep relationship with Sudan, especially considering the current situation in the country. This aid is also an extension of the UAE's humanitarian vision and emphasizes its relationship with other countries based on human brotherhood and its commitment to helping others during disasters and emergencies. Dr. Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely with the World Health Organization to ensure the delivery of essential logistical operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help them cope with the current crisis in Sudan." Alongside calling on all parties to the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate the situation causing unspeakable suffering to the people of Sudan, the UAE remains steadfast in its commitment to providing assistance to the country during times of need."

translations.source: Umoja wa falme za kiarabu unalenga hasa kutoa mahitaji ya makundi yaliyo hatarini zaidi yaliyoathiriwa na hali nchini Sudani, hususani wagonjwa, watoto, wazee na wanawake ambao wapo katika hatari zaidi kutokana na mzozo unaoendelea - na safari hizi za ndege moja kwa moja zitatua mapengo makubwa zaidi katika utoaji wa matibabu na chakula. Maadili ya kibinadmu yaliyoshikiliwa kwa kina ya UAE yamemaanisha kwamba imehakikisha kuwa imeendelea kuwasiliana na ulimwengu kujitolea kwake kwa nguvu na kwa kudumu katika kuimarisha amani, usalama na utulivu, sio tu kikanda, lakini pia Duniani kote, wakati wa pamoja na washirika wake na jumuia ya kimataifa, nchi imendelea kusaidia watu wa Sudani wakati wa machafuko" vifaa ni pamoja na kiwewe cha kutosha, vifaa vya upasuaji wa Dharura, na dawa muhimu kufikia mara moja watu 165,000 ambao wanahitaji sana msaada wa kibinadamu. Vituo vya afya kote nchini vinaripoti kuwa vimekosa vifaa vya msingi vya afya na kwamba Bohari ya Taifa ya Dawa haifikii tena kutokana na hali ya usalama. Miongoni mwa msaada mwingine, WHO inapeleka wataalamu wawili wa vifaa vya Dharura wanaosafiri na mizigo ili kuhakikisha kwamba vifaa hivi vinasambazwa mara moja kwa vituo vikuu 13 vya afya ili kusaidia wahudumu wa afya na kupanua huduma kwa wale wanaohitaji.
translations.reference: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"The United Arab Emirates is primarily focused on meeting the needs of the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, particularly patients, children, the elderly, and women who are at greater risk due to the ongoing conflict – and these direct flights will fill the largest gaps in the delivery of medical care and food. The UAE's deeply held human values have meant that it has ensured it continues to engage with the world in its commitment to strengthening peace, security, and stability, not only regionally but also globally, while working with partners and the international community, the country has continued to support the people of Sudan during the unrest. The supplies include sufficient blankets, emergency surgical equipment, and essential medicines to reach 165,000 people who urgently need humanitarian assistance. Health centers across the country report that they are lacking basic health supplies and that the National Medicines Depot is no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other assistance, the WHO is sending two emergency medical equipment specialists traveling with cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health centers to support healthcare providers and expand services to those in need."

translations.source: WHO ina tani nyingine 30 za vifaa kwa ajili ya Malaria na magonjwa yasiyoambukiza kama vile Kisukari na Shikinizo la damu, hali ambayo inaweza kuwa mbaya kama haitibiwi. Mifumo hii na takribani 23,000 ya mifuko ya damu inasomwa ndani ya kituo cha vifaa cha kimataifa cha WHO katika jiji la kimataifa la kibinadamu, na WHO kwa sasa inachunguza uwezekano wote wa kuwasilisha vifaa hivi nchini Sudani haraka iwezekanavyo kwa ushirikiano na wizara ya afya ya Sudani. Dk Michael Ryan, mkurugenzi mtendaji, wa mpango wa Dharura ya afya wa WHO, alisema, "kituo cha usafirishaji cha WHO huko UAE kimekuwa kitovu cha muitiko wa haraka wa matukio ya Dharura ulimwenguni. Husaidia zaidi ya nchi 140 katika maeneo yote 6 ya kijografia ya WHO, kitovu hutoa njia ya kuokoa maisha kwa nchi wanachama zinazokabiliwa na Dharura za kiafya zinazotokana na milipuko ya magonjwa, majanga kama vile mafuriko na matetemeko ya ardhi, na athari za migogoro kwenye mifumo ya afya. Msaada unaotolewa na UAE huwezesha WHO kuwa mstari wa mbele katika majibu haya ya Dharura ya afya- kutoa msaada wa matibabu wa kibingwa kwa wale wanaohitaji popote na wakati wowote inapohitajika.
translations.reference: WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

WHO has another 30 tons of supplies for Malaria and non-communicable diseases such as Diabetes and High Blood Pressure, a condition which can be serious if left untreated. These supplies and approximately 23,000 blood bags are being processed at the WHO’s international logistics hub in the humanitarian city of Dubai, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities to deliver these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr. Michael Ryan, Executive Director of WHO’s Health Emergency Programme, said, "The WHO logistics hub in the UAE has been a focal point for the rapid response to global health emergencies. It assists more than 140 countries in all 6 geographic regions of WHO, the hub provides a lifeline for Member States facing health emergencies stemming from disease outbreaks, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the effects of conflicts on health systems. The assistance provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – providing expert medical assistance to those in need, wherever and whenever it is required."

translations.source: Kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuimarisha usanifu wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya Dharura ya kiafya, muitikio, na ustahimilivu UAE, WHO, na washirika wanafanya kazi bega kwa bega kupeana vifaa ili kuwafikia waliohatarini zaidi Duniani katika wakati wao wa uhitaji mkubwa" Kwa upande wake, Dk Ahmed Al- Mandhari, mkurugenzi wa WHO kanda ya mashariki ya Mediterania, alisema, "shirika la afya Duniani linaendelea kuratibu na mamlaka ya afya ya Sudani kuwasilisha vifaa muhimu vya afya kupitia njia zote zinazopatikana. Kwa sababu ya mzozo, hospitali, maduka ya dawa na vituo vya huduma ya afya ya msingi vimekosa dawa muhimu, Dawa za kuokoa maisha. Kuwasili kwa tani 30 za ziada za vifaa vya kiwewe na upasuaji wa Dharura vilevile dawa muhimu kutawezesha WHO kusaidia vituo vikuu 13 vya afya na kuanzisha upya huduma za afya kwa wale wanaohitajika. Kituo chetu cha usafirishaji cha kimataifa huko Dubai kina jukumu muhimu katika kuhakikisha usambazaji usiokatizwa wa bidhaa za afya na tunashukuru kwa usaidizi unaotolewa na umoja wa falme za kiarabu kuwezesha safari yetu ya kwanza ya kukodi ya vifaa hivi vinavyohitajika sana.
translations.reference: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"As part of efforts to strengthen international architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience, the UAE, WHO, and partners are working shoulder to shoulder to deliver supplies to reach those most at risk globally in their time of greatest need." Dr. Ahmed Al-Mandhari, Director of the WHO Eastern Mediterranean Regional Office, said, "The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential medical supplies through all available channels. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies, and primary health care centers have run out of essential medicines, life-saving drugs. The arrival of an additional 30 tons of surgical and emergency medical supplies, as well as essential medicines, will enable WHO to support 13 major health centers and resume health services for those in need. Our international logistics hub in Dubai plays a crucial role in ensuring the uninterrupted supply of health products, and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first chartered flight of these much-needed supplies."

translations.source: Ni msaada kama huu ambao unaonyesha kwa vitendo dira yetu ya kikanda ya afya kwa wote, na wote" Misaada ya matibabu iliyotolewa na shirika la afya Duniani kupita UAE ilichangia takribani 55% ya jumla ya vifaa vya matibabu vilivyotolewa kwa Sudani kutoka nje ya nchi mwaka 2022, ikionyesha nafasi ya UAE kama kitovu cha usaidizi wa kimataifa wa kibinadamu.
translations.reference: It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.hypothesis:

"It is support like this that demonstrates our regional commitment to health for all, and everyone." "Medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE contributed approximately 55% of the total medical supplies delivered to Sudan from abroad in 2022, showcasing the UAE's position as a hub for international humanitarian aid.

translations.source: Kushirikiana ndani ya nchi ni muhimu kwa maendeleo ya kimataifa kuelekea SDGs zinazohusiana na afya Ripoti mpya ya maendeleo inatolewa kwa pamoja leo na mashirika 13 yaliyotia saini mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa wa maisha ya afya na ustawi kwa wote (SDG3 GAP). Ripoti yenye anuani "Ni nini kilifanya kazi? Nini haikufanya? Nini kinafuata?" inaakisi miaka minne ya kazi ya pamoja ya mashirika makubwa ya kimataifa inayoelezea kile ambacho hakijafanya kazi katika kuimarisha ushirikiano na usaidizi ili kuharakisha maendeleo ya nchi kuelekea malengo ya maendeleo endelevu (SDGs) yanayohusiana na afya. Mwaka huu ni ishara ya nusu ya kuelekea SDGs, lakini Dunia ipo nyuma kufikia malengo ya kimataifa. Tangu kuzinduliwa kwake mwaka 2019, SDG3 GAP iliwezesha kuundwa kwa miundo mipya ya ushirikiano kati ya mashirika yaliyotia saini katika maeneo muhimu kama vile ufadhili endelevu na huduma ya afya ya msingi miongoni mwa mengine.
translations.reference: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"Collaboration within countries is crucial for international development towards health-related SDGs. A new development report is being released today jointly by 13 organizations that have signed the international implementation plan for health and well-being for all (SDG3 GAP). The report, titled "What worked? What didn't work? What's next?" reflects four years of collaborative work by major international organizations outlining what hasn't worked in strengthening cooperation and support to accelerate the development of countries towards sustainable development goals (SDGs) related to health. This year marks the halfway point towards the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind in achieving international goals. Since its launch in 2019, SDG3 GAP has facilitated the creation of new cooperation frameworks among signatory organizations in key areas such as sustainable funding and primary health services, among others."

translations.source: Angalau nchi 67 zimejihusisha kwenye moja au zaidi ya maudhui ya kichochezi ya SDG3 GAP. "Tumipiga hatua muhimu, lakini bado tuna njia ndefu ya kusafiri ili kuboresha jinsi mashirika ya kimataifa yanayofanya kazi pamoja kusaidia nchi. Lazima tusikilize kile ambacho nchi zinatuambia na kuchukua hatua kulingana na muongozo wao", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO na mwenyekiti wa kundi la wakuu wa SDG3 GAP. "Nawashukuru washirika kwa ushirikiano wao na kwa kujitathmini kwa uaminifu uliomo katika ripoti hii. "Ripoti inatoa mapendekezo sita muhimu ili kuhakikisha kuwa nchi zinanufaika kutokana na usaidizi ulioboreshwa zaidi na kutimiza ahadi zilizotolewa katika SDG3 GAP.
translations.reference: At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

"At least 67 countries have engaged in one or more of the SDG3 GAP catalytic themes. "We have made significant progress, but we still have a long way to go to improve how international organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries are telling us and take action based on their guidance," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Chief Executive Board. "I thank partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessment contained in this report." The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from improved support and fulfill the commitments made within the SDG3 GAP.

translations.source: Mapendekezo hayo ni pamoja na Kuendeleza mzunguko wa uboreshaji wa SDG3 GAP kwa afya kwa kutafuta maoni ya nchi wanachama kuhusu jinsi tunavyoshirikiana katika ngazi ya nchi na kujibu mapendekezo yanayohusiana; Kudumisha GAP kama jukwaa la ushirikiano; Kukuza ushirikiano wenye nguvu katika ngazi ya chini kuhusu huduma ya afya ya msingi na kuchunguza mada mpya kama vile mifumo ya afya inayostahimili hali ya hewa. Kutumia kwa pamoja mbinu mpya katika ngazi ya nchi kama vile uwasilishaji kwa mbinu ya athari;
translations.reference: These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"These recommendations include: Advancing the SDG3 GAP cycle for health by seeking input from member states on how we collaborate at the country level and respond to related recommendations; Maintaining the GAP as a platform for collaboration; Promoting strong collaboration at the grassroots level regarding primary health services and exploring new topics such as climate-resilient health systems. Jointly utilizing new approaches at the country level such as delivery through a results-based approach."

translations.source: Kujihusisha zaidi na mashirika ya kiraia; na Kufanya kazi na nchi wanachama ili kuimarisha vitisho vya ushirikiano kupitia uongozi wa kisiasa, mwelekeo wa utawala na ufadhili ili kusaidia ushirikiano. Matokeo muhimu ya ripoti hiyo yatajikita katika majadiliano ya mkutano ujao wa baraza kuu la umoja wa mataifa la SDG na mkutano wa ngazi ya juu kuhusu huduma ya afya kwa wote mnamo Septemba 2023. Miezi kabla ya matukio haya muhimu inatoa fursa kwa msukumo wa pamoja na nchi wanachama na mashirika ya kimataifa kutambua njia za jinsi ya kutekeleza mapendekezo na kuandaa msingi wa kuimarisha ushirikiano wenye nguvu zaidi na kuharakisha maendeleo kuelekea SDGs zinazohusia na afya katika nusu ya pili ya muda wa SDG. Maelezo kwa mhariri:
translations.reference: engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"To engage further with civil society organizations; and to work with member states to strengthen collaborative threats through political leadership, governance direction, and funding to support collaboration. The key outcomes of the report will focus on discussions at the upcoming United Nations General Assembly meeting on the SDG and the high-level meeting on health for all in September 2023. The months leading up to these important events offer an opportunity for joint advocacy with member states and international organizations to identify ways to implement recommendations and lay the groundwork for strengthening stronger collaboration and accelerating progress towards lagging SDGs and health in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to editor:"

translations.source: SDG3 GAP inaeleza jinsi mashirika 13 yaliyotia saini yatatumia njia mpya za kufanya kazi, kuendeleza ushirikiano uliopo kwenye mafanikio, na kuoanisha kwa pamoja msaada wao katika mipango na mikakati ya kitaifa ambayo inamilikiwa na kuongozwa na nchi. Waliotia saini SDG3 GAP ni Gavi, Muungano wa Chanjo; Ufadhili wa kituo cha kimataifa kwa wanawake, watoto na vijana (GFF); Mfuko wa kimataifa wa kupambana na UKIMWI, kifua kikuu na Malaria (mfuko wa kimataifa); Shirika la kazi la kimataifa (ILO);
translations.reference: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO);
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili text:

The SDG3 GAP outlines how 13 signatory organizations will utilize new ways of working, build on existing collaboration successes, and collectively align their support within national plans and strategies that are owned and led by countries. The signatories of the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; The Global Fund for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); The Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (The Global Fund); The International Labour Organization (ILO);

translations.source: Mpango wa pamoja wa umoja wa mataifa kuhusu VVU/UKIMWI (UNAIDS); Mpango wa maendeleo wa umoja wa mataifa (UNDP); Shirika la umoja wa mataifa la idadi ya watu (UNFPA); Shirika la umoja wa mataifa la kuhudumia watoto (UNICEF); Unitaid; Shirika la umoja wa mataifa la usawa wa jinsia na uwezeshaji wa wanawake (UN Women);

Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);

translations.source: Matokeo mapya ya uchunguzi yanaonyesha mifano ya afya ikianza kurejeshwa kutokana na janga Baada ya miaka mitatu ya janga la UVIKO-19, mifumo ya afya katika nchi imeanza kuonyesha dalili kuu za kwanza za kuimarika kwa mfumo wa afya kulingana na ripoti ya muda ya WHO juu ya "Duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa mapigo ya kimataifa juu ya kuendelea kwa huduma muhimu za afya wakati wa janga la UVIKO-19: Novemba 2022 Januari 2023". Ifikapo mapema mwaka 2023, nchi ziliripoti kukabiliwa na usumbufu mdogo katika utoaji wa huduma za afya za kawaida, lakini zilionyesha haja ya kuwekeza katika kurejeshwa na kustahimili nguvu zaidi kwa siku zijazo. Kati ya nchi 139 zilizojibu duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa mapigo ya moyo wa WHO, nchi ziliripoti kuendelea kukatika kwa karibu robo moja ya huduma kwa wastani. Katika nchi 84 ambapo uchunguzi wa mwenendo unawezekana, asilimia ya huduma zilizokatizwa ilipungua kwa wstani kutoka 56% mwezi Julai - Septemba 2020 hadi 23% mnamo Novemba 2022 - Januari 2023.
translations.reference: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

New survey results show signs of health systems recovering from the pandemic. After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries are beginning to show the first key signs of strengthening, according to a WHO interim report on the "Fourth round of pulse surveys on the continuation of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022 – January 2023." By early 2023, countries reported facing less disruption in the delivery of routine health services, but expressed a need to invest in recovery and build greater resilience for the future. Of the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO pulse surveys, countries reported a continued interruption of approximately a quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend monitoring was possible, the percentage of interrupted services decreased on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022 – January 2023.

translations.source: Usumbufu unaoendelea unaotokana na mahitaji na upande wa ugavi, ikiwa ni pamoja na viwango vya chini vya watu wanaotafuta huduma za afya katika jamii pamoja na upatikanaji mdogo wa wahudumu wa afya na rasilimali nyingine za afya kama vile zahanati huria au akiba inayopatikana ya dawa na bidhaa. Ni habari njema kwamba mifumo ya afya katika nchi nyingi inaanza kurejesha huduma muhimu za afya kwa mamilioni ya watu ambao hawakuzipata wakati wakati wa janga hili", alisema Dk Rudi Eggers, mkurugenzi wa WHO wa huduma za afya jumuishi. "Lakini tunahitaji kuhakikisha kuwa nchi zote zinaendelea kuziba pengo hili ili kurejesha huduma za afya, na kutumia mafunzo tuliyojifunza ili kujenga mifumo ya afya iliyoandaliwa zaidi na thabiti kwa siku zijazo". Ishara mashuhuri za kwanza za kurejeshwa Katika utafiti huu mpya, nchi chache ziliripoti kwa makusudi kupunguza ufikiaji katika majukwaa yote ya utoaji huduma na kazi muhimu za afya ya umma tangu kuripoti 2020-2021, kuonyesha hatua muhimu ya kurejea viwango vya utoaji wa huduma kabla ya janga na utendaji mpana wa mfumo.
translations.reference: Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Swahili document:

"The ongoing disruption stemming from both demand and supply-side factors, including low numbers of people seeking healthcare in communities as well as limited availability of healthcare workers and other health resources such as free clinics or accessible stocks of medicines and supplies. The good news is that health systems in many countries are beginning to restore essential health services to millions of people who did not receive them during this pandemic," said Dr. Rudi Eggers, WHO Director of Integrated Health Services. "But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to restore health services, and use the lessons we have learned to build health systems that are more prepared and resilient for the future." Prominent early signs of recovery In this new study, a few countries reported deliberately reducing access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since reporting 2020-2021, demonstrating a key step towards returning service delivery levels to pre-pandemic and broad system performance.

translations.source: Kufikia mwishoni mwa 2022, nchi nyingi ziliripoti dalili za ufufuaji wa huduma, ikiwa ni pamoja katika huduma za afya ya ngono, uzazi, kujifungua, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana; lishe; chanjo; magonjwa ya kuambukiza (ikijumuisha Malaria, VVU, TB na magonjwa mengine ya zinaa); magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyopuuzwa; magonjwa yasiyoambukiza; udhibiti wa matatizo ya akili, Neva na matumizi ya dutu; utunzaji wa wazee; na huduma za kitamaduni na/au za ziada. Idadi ya nchi zinazoripoti kukatizwa kwa mfumo wao wa kitaifa wa ugavi imepungua kutoka karibu nusu (nchi 29 kati ya 59 zinazojibu) hadi takribani robo (18 kati ya nchi 66 zinazojibu) mwaka jana. Licha ya dalili za kupona, kukatizwa kwa huduma kunaendelea kaika nchi zote katika kanda na viwango vyote vya mapato, na katika mipangilio mingi ya utoaji wa huduma na maeneo ya huduma za ufuatiliaji. Pia nchi zinakabiliana na ongezeko la mlundikano wa huduma - mara nyingi zaidi katika huduma za uchunguzi, utambuzi na matibabu ya magonjwa yasiyoambukiza - ambayo inaweza kusababisha matokeo mabaya kwani watu wanacheleweshwa kupata huduma kwa wakati. Kurejesha utoaji wa huduma muhimu za afya ni muhimu kwa sababu usumbufu- ikiwemo huduma za kukuza afya, kuzuia magonjwa, uchunguzi, matibabu, urekebishaji na kupooza - kunaweza kuwa na athari mbaya zaidi za kiafya kwa idadi ya watu na kiwango cha mtu binafsi kuliko janga lenyewe, hususani miongoni mwa watu walio hatarini.
translations.reference: By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations.
translations.hypothesis: By the end of 2022, many countries reported signs of service restoration, including in sexual and reproductive health, childbirth, newborns, children and adolescents; nutrition; immunization; infectious diseases (including Malaria, HIV, TB and other sexually transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; non-communicable diseases; mental health, neurological and substance use disorders; elderly care; and cultural and/or additional services. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply systems has decreased from almost half (29 out of 59 respondents) to approximately a quarter (18 out of 66 respondents) last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruption continues in all countries across regions and all income levels, and in many service delivery settings and areas of health service follow-up. Countries are also facing an increase in the backlog of services – often more pronounced in screening, diagnosis and treatment of non-communicable diseases – which can lead to adverse outcomes as people are delayed in accessing care in a timely manner. Restoring the delivery of essential health services is crucial because disruption – including health promotion services, disease prevention, screening, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care – can have more severe health consequences for the population and at the individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations.

translations.source: Kuunganisha huduma za UVIKO-19 katika huduma muhimu za afya katika hatua nyingine muhimu kuelekea ufufuaji na mabadiliko ya mfumo, nchi nyingi zimepiga hatua katika kuunganisha huduma za UVIkO-19 katika utoaji wa huduma za afya za kawaida. Takribani 80-90% ya nchi zimeunganisha kikamilifu huduma za chanjo ya UVIKO-19, uchunguzi na udhibiti wa kesi vilevile huduma za hali baada ya UVIKO-19 katika utoaji wa huduma za kawaida. Bado, nchi nyingi (80% yanchi 83 zinazojibu) ziliripoti angalau kizuizi kimoja cha kuongeza ufikiaji wa zana muhimu za UVIKO-19 (m.f uchunguzi wa UVIKO-19, matibabu, chanjo na vifaa vya kinga ya kibinafsi- PPE), na masuala ya wahudumu wa afya na ukosefu wa fedha unaowakilisha vikwazo vya kawaida. Msaada zaidi unahitajika kwa ajili ya kurejeshwa, uthabiti na kujitayarisha Nchi nyingi zimeanza kutumia yale ambayo wamejifunza kipindi cha janga la UVIKO-19, ikijumuisha kupitia kuweka mikakati kadhaa bunifu ya kupunguza usumbufu wa huduma katika utoaji wa huduma za afya.
translations.reference: Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Swahili document into English:

Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services represents a crucial step towards recovery and transformation of the system, and many countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into the delivery of routine health services. Approximately 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination services, testing and case control, as well as post-COVID-19 care, into routine service delivery. However, many countries (80% of the 83 respondents) reported at least one barrier to increasing access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g., COVID-19 testing, treatment, vaccines, and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health worker issues and lack of funding representing common obstacles. Further support is needed for recovery, stabilization, and preparedness. Many countries have begun to utilize the lessons learned during the COVID-19 pandemic, including implementing innovative strategies to minimize service disruptions in the delivery of health services.

translations.source: Hizi ni pamoja na kupelekea mbinu za matibabu ya simu, uhamasishaji wa huduma za nyumbani au uingiliaji wa kujitegemea, mbinu za kuimarisha upatikanaji wa wahudumu wa afya, uwezo na mbinu za usaidizi, ubunifu katika ununuzi na usambazaji wa dawa na vifaa, mawasiliano zaidi ya kawaida ya jamii,n na ushirikiano na watoa huduma na sekta binafsi. Robo tatu ya nchi ziliripoti mgao wa ziada wa ufadhili kuelekea ufufuaji wa mfumo wa muda mrefu, ustahimilivu na utayari. Nchi zilieleza haja ya msaada wa WHO ili kushughulikia changamoto zilizosalia katika muktadha wa UVIKO-19 na zaidi, mara nyingi zinazohusiana na uimarishaji wa wahudumu wa afya, kujenga uwezo wa ufuatiliaji wa huduma za afya, kubuni huduma za afya ya msingi --mfano inayolengwa ya utunzaji, utawala, sera na mipango na mipango ya fedha na ufadhili. Maelezo kwa mhariri: Katika duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa kimataifa wa Pulse wa WHO, nchi 222, wilaya na maeneo walialikwa kujibu uchunguzi sanifu wa msingi wa mtandao kati ya Novemba 2022 na Januari 2023. Uchunguzi wa nne ulifuatia tafiti za awali za WHO za 2020 na 2021: Duru ya 1(Mei - Septemba 2020), Duru ya 2(Januari - Machi 2021) na Duru ya 3 (Novemva - Desemba 2021) ambao ulionyesha kiwango ambacho janga hilo lilikuwa likiathiri muelekeo wa huduma za afya na jinsi nchi zinavyochukua hatua.
translations.reference: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Swahili document:

These include leading to telephone-based treatment approaches, promotion of home care services or self-management interventions, approaches to strengthen the availability of health workers, support capabilities and methods, innovation in procurement and distribution of medicines and supplies, more regular community communication, and collaboration with private service providers and sectors. A third of the countries reported an additional allocation of funding towards strengthening system resilience, sustainability and preparedness. Countries expressed a need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, often related to strengthening health workers, building health service monitoring capacity, designing primary health care services – a targeted example of care, governance, policies and planning and financial and funding plans. For editors' note: In the fourth round of WHO’s global Pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized online questionnaire between November 2022 and January 2023.
